ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
OF THE
COMMONWEALTH OF KENTUCKY
PASSED AT THE REGULAR SESSION OF THE GENERAL ASSEM-
BLY , WHICH WAS BEGUN IN THE CITY OF FRANKFORT , KEN-
TUCKY, ON TUESDAY , JANUARY THE EIGHTH , 1952 , AND ENDED
FRIDAY , MARCH THE TWENTY -FIRST , 1952 .
CHAPTER 1
(S. B. 1)
AN ACT relating to bribery of participants in professional or amateur
games , sports , contests , horse racing and horse shows .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Whoever gives , promises or offers to any
professional or amateur baseball , football , hockey , polo , tennis
or basketball player or boxer or any player who participates or
expects to participate in any professional or amateur game or
sport or any jockey , driver , groom or any person participating or
expecting to participate in any horse race or saddle horse shows ,
including owners of race tracks and their employees , stewards ,
trainers , judges , starters or special policemen , or to any manager ,
coach or trainer of any team or participant or prospective partici-
pant in any such game, contest or sport , any valuable thing with
intent to influence him to lose or try to lose or cause to be lost or
to limit his or his team's margin of victory in a baseball , football ,
hockey or basketball game, boxing , tennis or polo match or a
horse race or any professional or amateur sport , or game , in which
such player or participant or jockey or driver , is taking part or
expects to take part , or has any duty or connection therewith ,
is guilty of a felony , punishable by imprisonment for not less than
one year , nor more than ten years and by a fine of not more than
ten thousand dollars .
Section 2. Any professional or amateur baseball , football ,
hockey , basketball , tennis or polo player , boxer , or jockey , driver ,
or groom or participant or prospective participant in any sport or
game or a manager , coach or trainer of any team or individual
participant or prospective participant in any such game, contest
or sport , who solicits or accepts any valuable thing to influence

2 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
him to lose or try to lose or cause to be lost or to limit his or
his team's margin of victory in a baseball , football , hockey or
basketball game or boxing , tennis or polo match , or horse race or
any game or sport in which he is taking part , or expects to take
part , or has any duty or connection therewith , is guilty of a
felony , punishable by imprisonment for not less than one year ,
nor more than five years and by a fine of not more than ten thou-
sand dollars .
Section 3. WHEREAS , the conduct for which this Act
imposes a penalty is responsible for widespread indignation and
loss of confidence in the sports and activities included herein
and constitutes an imminent danger to the existence and con-
tinuation of the amateur , collegiate and professional sports and
activities included herein , and seriously endangers the welfare
of
all
ofthe persons and institutions participating
in
orconnected
with such sports
;
NOW
, THEREFORE
,
anemergency
isdeclared
toexist
and this Act shall take effect upon
itspassage and approval by
the Governor
.
Approved February
13, 1952

CHAPTER 4 3
CHAPTER 3
(S. B. 11 )
AN ACT to amend Section 23.050, Kentucky Revised Statutes , 1950
Edition , in so far as it relates to the time of holding the terms of
the Circuit Courts in the 36th Judicial District, composing the coun-
ties of Knott and Magoffin .
WHEREAS , it has become necessary , due to the increased court business
to change the times of holding Court in the 36th Judicial District in
order to create a new term and extend the duration of the terms so
as to keep up the work of the dockets in an orderly manner .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Section 23.050 ( 36 ) of Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
1950 Edition , be and the same is amended with reference to the
36th Judicial District of Kentucky , and such section as amended
shall read as follows :
(36) Thirty -sixth District. Knott County at Hindman , on
the first Monday in February , twenty -four juridical days ; on the
third Monday in April , eighteen juridical days ; on the fourth
Monday in August , eighteen juridical days , and on the second
Monday in November , 30 juridical days .
Magoffin County at Salyersville , on the second Monday in
March , twenty -four juridical days ; on the second Monday in
June, eighteen juridical days , and on the fourth Monday in
September , eighteen juridical days .
In order that no term of court in the 36th District as is now
fixed by law may be interrupted or interfered with while in
session , so that it may be known well in advance when this said
Act shall become effective , it is provided that this Act shall be in
effect on and after the first day of November , 1952 .
CHAPTER 4
(S. B. 12)
Approved February 29 , 1952
AN ACT relating to real estate brokers and salesmen .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Subsection ( 4) of Section 324.010 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is hereby repealed .

4 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 2. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
Licenses shall be granted only to persons who are trust-
worthy and competent to transact the business of a real estate
broker or salesman in such manner as to safeguard the interests
of the public , and only after satisfactory proof of such quali-
fications has been presented to the Commission .
Section 3. A new section of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is created to read as follows :
In addition to proof of honesty , truthfulness and good
reputation of any applicant for a license , each applicant must
pass a written examination conducted by said Commission , or
itsauthorized representative
, which examination shall be
of
scope and wording sufficient
inthe judgment
ofthe Commission
toestablish the competency and trustworthiness
ofthe appli-
cant to act as
areal estate broker or salesman in such manner
as
toprotect the interests
ofthe public
. Provided
, however
,
no
examination shall
berequired for the renewal
ofany present
or
future license unless such license has been revoked
, suspended
,
or
isallowed
toexpire without renewal
. The Commission shall
hold examinations
atsuch times and places
as
itmay determine
,
and
anexamination fee
oftwenty
-five dollars shall be collected
from each applicant
todefray the expenses

Section 5. Section 324.060 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
Every application for a broker's or salesman's license shall
be accompanied by a bond in the sum of three thousand dollars .
The bond shall be a cash bond or a surety bond and, if the latter ,
shall be executed by a surety company authorized to do business
in this state and shall be made to the Commission , and the bond
shall be conditioned that the applicant shall conduct his business
in accordance with the provisions of this chapter . The bond shall
be in a form approved by the Commission ; and , in addition to
the foregoing , each and every broker and salesman licensed as
of July 1 , 1952 , and thereafter , shall furnish bond in accordance
with the provisions hereof .
Section 6. This Act shall apply only to real estate brokers
and real estate salesmen who operate in any city of the first ,
second or third class, or within five miles from the corporate
limits thereof .
Approved February 29 , 1952

CHAPTER 6
(S. B. 17 )
AN ACT abolishing the position of deputy constable , except in counties
containing a city of the first or second class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. Section 70.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The appointment of deputy constables shall be author-
ized only in counties containing a first or second class city. In
such counties each constable may appoint one or more deputies
by and with the consent of the county judge . The constable
and his surety shall be liable on his bond for all the acts and
omissions of his deputies . Deputy constables must be residents
of the same district as the constable by whom they are ap-
pointed.
( 2 ) Deputy constables may be removed at any time , for
any cause deemed sufficient by the constable , by order of the
county court , entered after the filing of a written direction by
the constable .
(3) In counties containing a population of over two hun-
dred and fifty thousand , each deputy constable appointed by the
constable shall be exclusively compensated for the performance
of the duties of his office by a salary of twenty -one hundred
dollars per annum and paid in equal monthly installments out of
the county treasury .
2. This Act shall take effect on January 1 , 1954 .

CHAPTER 8
(S. B. 25)
AN ACT concerning equipment of vehicles . This Act shall be known as
the McCann Act.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. No motor vehicle shall be equipped with a tele-
vision receiver unless the owner of such vehicle shall have secured
a permit therefor from the Commissioner of State Police . The
Commissioner may grant a permit upon such terms and condi-
tions as he may prescribe .
Section. 2 Any person who equips or operates a vehicle in
violation of Section 1 of this Act shall be guilty of a misdemeanor .
Approved March 24, 1952
CHAPTER 9
(S. B. 38 )
AN ACT relating to mechanics' and materialmen's liens .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 376.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :

CHAPTER 9 9
(1) Any person who performs labor or furnishes materials
for the erection , altering or repairing of a house or other structure
or for any fixture or machinery therein , for the excavation of
cellars , cisterns , vaults , wells or for the improvement in any man-
ner of real property including the furnishing of agricultural
lime , fertilizer , concrete pipe or drainage tile, crushed rock,
gravel for roads or driveways , and materials used in the construc-
tion or maintenance of fences, by contract with, or by the
written consent of, the owner , contractor , subcontractor , architect
or authorized agent , shall have a lien thereon , and upon the land
upon which the improvements were made or on any interest the
owner has therein , to secure the amount thereof with cost. The
lien on the land or improvements shall be superior to any mort-
gage or encumbrance created subsequent to the beginning of
the labor or the furnishing of the materials , and the lien , if as-
serted as hereinafter provided , shall relate back and take effect
from the time of the commencement of the labor or the furnish-
ing of the materials . The lien shall not be for a greater amount
in the aggregate than the contract price of the original contractor ,
and should the aggregate amount of the liens exceed the price
agreed upon between the original contractor and the owner
there shall be a pro rata distribution of the original contract price
among the lien -holders .
( 2 ) The lien shall not take precedence over a mortgage
or other contract lien or bona fide conveyance for value without
notice , duly recorded or lodged for record according to law, un-
less the person claiming the prior lien shall , before the recording
of the mortgage or other contract lien or conveyance , file in the
office of the clerk of the county court of the county wherein he
has furnished or expects to furnish labor or materials , a state-
ment showing that he has furnished or expects to furnish labor or
materials , and the amount in full thereof . The lien shall not, as
against the holder of a mortgage or other contract lien or con-
veyance , exceed the amount of the lien claimed or expected to
be claimed as set forth in the statement. The statement shall ,
in other respects , be in the form prescribed by KRS 376.080 .
(3) No person who has not contracted directly with the
owner or his agent shall acquire a lien under this section unless
he notifies in writing the owner of the property to be held liable
or his authorized agent , within seventy-five days after the last

10 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
item of material or labor is furnished , of his intention to hold the
property liable and the amount for which he will claim a lien .
It shall be sufficient to prove that the notice was mailed to the
last known address of the owner of the property upon which
the lien is claimed , or to his duly authorized agent within the
county in which the property to be held liable is located .

AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Jeffersontown , in

Assembly that the population of the City of Jeffersontown , in Jef-
ferson County , is such as to justify its being assigned to the fifth class

Section 5. ( Denial, Suspension or Revocation of License ;
Hearings and Review ) The licensing agency , after notice to the
applicant or licensee and after a hearing as herein provided , is
authorized to deny , suspend or revoke a license in any case in
which it finds that there has been a substantial failure to comply
the requirements established under this act . Notices of hearing
shall be issued in the name of the licensing agency by the secre-
tary thereof and shall state the time and place of the hearing ,
which shall not be less than thirty days from service of the no-
tice , and shall also contain a concise statement of the reason
for the hearing and sufficient information to reasonably apprise
the applicant or licensee of the substance of the facts claimed
to constitute a violation of this act . Service of such notices may
be effected in the manner prescribed by law for the service of
civil process , or may be effected by any member or employee of
the licensing agency personally delivering same to the applicant
or licensee , or by the licensing agency forwarding same to the
applicant or licensee, by United States registered mail postage
prepaid at the address of the applicant or licensee as reflected by
the records of the licensing agency . The hearing may be con-
ducted by the licensing agency in person or by a hearing officer
or a hearing committee appointed by the licensing agency ;

( 12 ) ( Employee Health Examinations ) All hospital em-
ployees shall be required to have such examinations and tests
as may be necessary to determine their freedom from communi-

or other employees . The examinations shall be repeated at such
intervals as may be required by the licensing agency with the
advice and approval of the Hospital Council . The provisions
of this section shall not apply to employees in nursing homes
conducted solely for those who seek healing in accordance with
the religious tenets of an established church .
Section 7. ( Hospital Council ) ( 1 ) The Governor shall
appoint a Hospital Council consisting of ten members as herein
provided :
( a) Five physicians who are duly licensed and who are in
active practice , one of whom shall be the State Health Com-
missioner by virtue of his office who shall serve as Chairman of
the Hospital Council. The appointments shall be made from a
list of three nominees for each vacancy submitted by the Ken-
tucky State Medical Association .
( b ) Five Hospital Administrators who are actively engaged
in the field of hospital administration . The appointments shall
be made from a list of three nominees for each vacancy sub-
mitted by the Kentucky State Hospital Association .
( 2 ) All members shall serve for a term of three years except
that the original appointments shall be made so that the terms
of three members are for one year , three for two years , and
four for three years . Members whose terms expire shall hold
office until the appointment of their successors . In the event of
a vacancy occurring before the expiration of a member's term ,
the appointment shall be for the unexpired term .
( 3 ) The Council shall meet at least once each year and
shall meet additionally upon the call of the chairman or at the

24 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
request of any four members . Members of the Hospital Council
shall serve without compensation but shall be reimbursed for
necessary travel and subsistence expense incurred in perform-
ance of their duties .
( 4) The function of the Hospital Council shall be to
approve al rules and regulations established by the licensing
agency, to act in an advisory capacity to the licensing agency
with respect to hospitals and the administration and enforce-
ment of this act, and to make available to the licensing agency
information , data and material in
itspossession together with
its
views

,

time to time

Section 10. No person shall operate or conduct a hospital
without having a license under this act . Hospitals operated by
governmental units shall be exempt from the payments of fees
and licensure , but shall comply with all other provisions of this
act . Hospitals operated by the Federal Government on land
under the jurisdiction of the Federal Government are excepted.
Section 11. ( Penalties . ) The establishing , conducting ,
managing , or operating of any hospital without a license required
under this law shall be punishable by a fine of not less than one
hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars for the first
offense and by a fine of not less than five hundred dollars nor
more than one thousand dollars for each subsequent offense .
Each day of violation of any of the provisions of this act shall be
considered a separate offense .
Section 12. ( Injunction ) Notwithstanding the existence
or pursuit of any other remedy ( civil or criminal ) , the licensing
agency may maintain in its own name an action to restrain or
enjoin any violation of this act .
Section 13. ( Appropriation ) There is hereby appropriated
to the State Board of Health, out of the general expenditure fund
in the state treasury , the sum of twenty thousand dollars for each
of the fiscal years 1952-53 and 1953-54 , which appropriation
shall be in addition to any other appropriations made to the State
Board of Health or the Department of Health.
Section 14. (Effective Date ) This Act shall become effec-
tive on July 1 , 1952 .
CHAPTER 17
(S. B. 57)
Approved March 5, 1952
AN ACT relating to the practice of dentistry .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1 ) The following branches of dentistry are

26 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
hereby recognized as suitable fields for the licensing and certifi-
cation of Dental Specialist , and are known as "Specialties . "
( a ) Oral Surgery .
(b ) Orthodentia .
( c ) Pedodontia .
( d) Peridontia .
( e ) Prosthodentia .
( f ) Oral Pathology .
(2 ) As used in sections 1 through of this Act , unless the
context requires otherwise :
(a) "Board " means the Kentucky State Board of Dental
Examiners .
Section 2. No licensed dentist shall hold himself out to the
public as being especially qualified in any branch of dentistry
by announcing through the press , sign , card , letterhead or printed
matter , or any means of public advertising using such terms as
" specialist, " or inserting the name of the specialty , or using other
phrases customarily used by qualified specialists that would imply
to the public that he is so qualified , without first securing a
specialist's license for same as provided in this Act.
Section 3. ( 1 ) Any dentist who is duly licensed and
qualified to practice dentistry in this state on the date this Act
becomes effective may obtain a license as a specialist in any of
the branches of dentistry as set out in Section 1 of the Act by
presenting to the board , within six months from the date this
Act becomes effective , satisfactory evidence that , prior to the
passage of this Act, he was holding himself out to the public as
a specialist and was specializing in the branch of dentistry for
which a specialty license is sought .
(2) Any dentist who obtains a license to practice dentistry
in this state after the effective date of this Act, or who does not
meet the qualifications of subsection ( 1 ) of this section and who
desires to specialize in any of the branches of dentistry set out
in section 1 of this Act may obtain a license as such specialist by
successfully passing such reasonable examination as the board
deems proper .
(3) A license as a specialist may be issued without examina-
tion to a holder of a diploma from the National Certifying Board ,
if any, of the branch of dentistry for which a specialty license is

CHAPTER 17 27
sought provided the applicant has a license to practice dentistry
in this state and is in good standing with his respective National
Certifying Board .
Section 4. ( 1 ) To be entitled to take the examination pro-
vided for in subsection ( 2 ) of section 3 of this Act an applicant
must :
(a) File with the secretary-treasurer of the board an appli-
cation accompanied by an examination fee of forty dollars ; and
(b ) Present to the board satisfactory evidence that he has
a period of study , after graduation from a dental school , of not
less than two years in graduate or postgraduate courses , hos-
pitals , clinics , dispensaries or preceptorships under the direction
of a certified specialist , or in fundamental science laboratories
recognized by the Council on Dental Education of the American
Dental Association and by the board as competent to provide
adequate training in the special field . This period of study may
be pursued wholly in a school giving graduate or postgraduate
courses and need not lead to an advanced degree . It may also
be pursued wholly in hospitals , clinics , dispensaries , preceptor-
ships , or fundamental science laboratories ; or it may be pursued
partially in schools or preceptorships and partially in other types
of institutions . One full academic year of graduate or postgrad-
uate study will be considered as equivalent to a calendar year .
Teaching , or a Fellowship in the field of the specialty , may be
considered in partial fulfillment of this requirement . The char-
acter of this period of study will be determined by the board .
(2 ) Examinations shall be clinical and written and of such
character as to test in a satisfactory manner the qualifications
of the applicant to specialize in the branch of dentistry for which
license is sought . Examination papers and clinical examination
results , where possible , shall be retained by the secretary-treas-
urer of the board for at least two years .
Section 5. ( 1 ) All applicants for a specialty license shall
be required to pay a fee of five dollars to the secretary-treasurer
of the board for every specialty license issued to him , and five
dollars for every duplicate license issued to him by the board .
( 2 ) Every specialty license issued by the board shall bear
a serial number , the full name of the applicant , the date of issue ,
and the seal of the board . It shall be signed by a majority of
the members and attested by the president .

28 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 6. No provision in this act shall be construed as
limiting or preventing a duly licensed and qualified dentist from
performing , without a specialty license , dental acts or services
to the public in any of the branches of dentistry set out in section
1 of this Act . Nor shall any provision in this act be construed
as preventing a dentist holding a specialty license from engaging
in the practice of dentistry in the other fields of dentistry .
Section 7. The board may suspend or revoke any specialty
license upon any grounds set out in KRS 313.130 , and the pro-
cedure for said suspension or revocation shall be the same as pro-
vided in KRS 313.150 to 313.180 for the revocation or suspension
of a regular license to practice dentistry .
Section 8. All fees imposed and collected under the pro-
visions of Sections 1 to 9 of this Act shall be disposed of in the
same manner as is provided in KRS 131.350 for fees imposed
and collected under the provisions of KRS 313.260 to 313.340 .
Section 9. Any dentist who violates the provisions of Sec-
tion 2 of this Act shall , upon conviction thereof , be fined not less
than one hundred dollars nor more than five hundred dollars ,
or imprisoned for not less than thirty days nor more than sixty
days , or both .
Section 10. Section 313.140 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
Unprofessional conduct means any of the following acts :
( 1 ) Employing "cappers" or "steerers " to obtain business ;
or employing directly or indirectly and student or any sus-
pended or unlicensed dentist to perform operations of any kind ,
or to treat lesions of the human teeth or jaws , or correct mal-
positioned formations thereof .
( 2 ) Obtaining any fee by fraud or misrepresentation ; wil-
fully betraying any professional secrets ; habitual intemperance ,
gross immorality , or the commission of a criminal operation ; ad-
vertising to use drugs , patents , nostrums , or proprietary medi-
cines ; or splitting fees .
(3) Making use of any advertising statements of a character
tending to deceive or mislead the public; advertising pro-
fessional superiority or the performance of professional service
in a superior manner ; advertising prices for professional service ;
advertising credit or terms of credit ; advertising by means of
electric sign , illuminated sign, sign that sets forth more than the

CHAPTER 18 29
name , profession , title ( such as D.D.S. or D.M.D. ) and office
hours of the dentist , sign more than six inches in height , or dis-
play the lettering of which is more than six inches in height ; ad-
vertising by means of a sign or display that contains or is a rep-
resentation or reproduction of a tooth , bridgework or any por-
tion of the human head ; advertising free dental work or free
examinations or advertising to guarantee any dental service or to
perform any dental operation or act painlessly ; or employing or
making use of advertising solicitors or free publicity press agents .
(4) Unprofessional conduct does not include the use of pro-
fessional cards that contain only a name , title ( such as D.D.S.
or D.M.D. ) , address , telephone number and office hours . It is
unprofessional conduct for any dentist to announce or hold him-
self out to the public as being especially qualified in any branch
of dentistry without having obtained a license as a specialist
therein from the Board .

CHAPTER 25
(S. B. 82)
AN ACT relating to liquefied petroleum gas .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act the term "liquefied petroleum
gas " means and includes any material which is composed pre-
dominantly of any of the following hydrocarbons , or mixtures
of them , whether in the liquid or in the gaseous states : Propane ,
propylene , butane ( Normal butane or isobutane ) , and butylene .
Section 2. No person shall transport in any manner within
this state liquefied petroleum gas in containers of a type not
approved by the Commissioner of Insurance .

( c ) Handling in quantities of less than one gallon U. S.

its utilization .
(d) Use as a fuel, while in the fuel tank of a motor
vehicle .
( 3 ) Any person engaged in any business for which a
license is required under the provisions of subsection ( 1 ) and
who engages in such business at more than one office or place
of business in this state shall obtain a separate license for each
such office or place of business and shall pay therefor the
required license fee; except that for the purposes of issuance of
licenses under subsection ( 1 ) all facilities for the storage only
of liquefied petroleum gas for resale within a radius of twenty
miles of an office or place of business shall be considered a part
of such office or place of business and shall not require separate
licensure .
Section 4. ( 1 ) The license required under Section 3
shall be issued on a calendar year basis and shall be procured from
the Commissioner of Insurance prior to the time of beginning
business . All licenses and renewals thereof shall expire on
December 31 of each year .
(2) Any person applying for a license after July 1 of any
year shall be required to pay therefor only one-half of the
applicable fee for that calendar year .
( 3 ) Every person licensed under the provisions of this
Act who desires to continue in business shall , during January
of each year , procure from the Commissioner of Insurance a
renewal license for which he shall pay a fee equal to the original
license fee . Failure to procure a renewal license prior to Jan-
uary 31 of the year for which it is issued shall result in the
imposition of a penalty equal to twenty per cent of the renewal
fee which shall be paid to the Commissioner of Insurance in
addition to the renewal fee .
Section 5. The Commissioner of Insurance , after a public
hearing thereon , shall make, promulgate and enforce regulations
setting forth minimum general standards covering the design ,
construction , location , installation and operation of equipment

CHAPTER 26 43
for storing , handling , transporting by tank truck , tank trailer ,
and utilizing liquefied gases , and specifying the odorization of
said gases and the degree thereof . Said regulations shall be
such as are reasonably necessary for the protection of the
health , welfare and safety of the public and persons using such
materials , and shall be in substantial conformity with the gen-
erally accepted standards of safety concerning the same subject
matter .
Section 6. It is hereby declared that regulations in sub-
stantial conformity with the published standards of the National
Board of Fire Underwriters for the design , installation and
construction of containers and pertinent equipment for the
storage and handling of liquefied petroleum gases as recom-
mended by the National Fire Protection Association shall be
deemed to be in substantial conformity with the generally
accepted standards of safety concerning the same subject matter.
Section 7. All moneys collected under the provisions of
this Act shall be paid into the State Treasury and credited to a
trust and agency fund to be used by the Department of Insurance
solely for the administration and enforcement of this Act .
Section 8. After having been given notice to cease and
desist and ten days' time in which to correct the violation , any
person who continues to violate any of the provisions of this Act ,
or any of the rules and regulations promulgated thereunder ,
shall be fined not less than twenty -five nor more than two
hundred dollars .
Section 9. Section 234.010 to 235.050 and 234.990 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes are repealed .
Section
10.This Act shall take effect on January
1, 1953
.
Approved March
4, 1952

CHAPTER 27
(S. B. 85)
Approved March 4, 1952
AN ACT relating to control of Black Shank and other diseases of tobacco
and to appropriate money for that purpose .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station
shall carry on and extend the present program in the field of
tobacco breeding , research and education for the control of
black shank and other diseases in tobacco . For this purpose
there shall be available to the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment
Station , such sums as may , from time to time, be appropriated
by the General Assembly for the purpose .
Section 2. For the purposes of this act , there is hereby
appropriated to the Kentucky Agricultural Experiment Station
out of the General Expenditure Fund of the State Treasury , the
sum of $60,000 per year for each of the fiscal years 1952-53 and
1953-54 ; without lapse in the year 1952-53 .

AN ACT relating to a study of the problem of Alcoholism in the State ,
providing for an Alcoholic Study Commission and for the furnishing
of assistance and facilities by the Legislative Research Commission ;
and making an appropriation . This Act to be known as the BAER-
MARTIN ACT .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :

50 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 1. There is created a commission to be known as the
Alcoholic Study Commission which shall consist of not more
than eleven members to be appointed by the Governor imme-
diately after the effective date of this Act . The Governor shall
name one of the members of the Commission as chairman .
Sec . 2. The Alcoholic Study Commission in conjunction
with the Legislative Research Commission shall make a com-
prehensive study on the problem of alcoholism in the State , the
existing methods and facilities for treatment , the present program
for public education on alcoholism and the present status of the
laws of the State in regard to alcoholism .
Sec . 3. The Legislative Research Commission is authorized
and directed to secure and provide adequate facilities , services
and assistance to enable the Alcoholic Study Commission to
carry out the object and purposes of this Act .
Sec . 4. The Alcoholic Study Commission in conjunction
with the Legislative Research Commission shall prepare and sub-

the purposes of this Act a report of the result of

AN ACT relating to public assistance and welfare of needy aged , needy
blind and needy chidren , and amending sections 205.010 , 205.200,
205.210 , 205.240, and 205.990 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes and
repealing subsection ( 3 ) of section 205.170 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Definitions
Section 205.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby
amended and re-enacted to read as follows :
205.010 Definitions . As used in KRS 205.160 to 205.250
and this Act , unless the context requires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Department " means the Department of Economic
Security ;
( 2 ) " Commissioner ” means the Commissioner of Economic
Security or his authorized representative ;
( 3 ) "Division" means the Division of Public Assistance in
the Department of Economic Security ;
(4 ) "Public assistance " means money grants , assistance in
kind, or service to or for the benefit of needy aged, needy blind ,
needy children or relative with whom a needy child lives ;
(5) "Needy child " means a child under the age of eighteen
( 18 ) who has been deprived of parental support by reason of
the death , absence or incapacity of a parent , and who is unable
to provide for himself and who does not otherwise have pro-
vided for him a subsistence compatible with decency and health ;
( 6 ) " Parent , " in addition to natural or adopted parent , shall
include step-parent ;
(7) "Needy aged" means a person who has attained the
age of sixty- five ( 65 ) and who is unable to provide for himself
and who does not have otherwise provided for him a subsistence
compatible with decency and health ;
1

52 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 8 ) "Needy blind" means a person who has no vision or
whose vision is so defective as to prevent the performance of
ordinary activities for which eyesight is essential and who is
unable to provide for himself and who does not have otherwise
provided for him a subsistence compatible with decency and
health ;
( 9 ) "Relative with whom a needy child lives " means the
individual , of the degree of relationship prescribed by regula-
tion , with whom such child is living in a place of residence main-
tained by such individual by himself or together with one ( 1 )
or more other persons of the prescribed degree of relationship ;
( 10) "Private institution ” means any establishment or place
other than a public institution operated or maintained by any
individual , association , corporation or other organization which
provides a group living arrangement for four ( 4) or more individ-
uals , who are cared for and maintained in residence for com-
pensation or othewise ;
( 11 ) "Public institution " means any establishment or place
which is the responsibility of and administered by the state or
any political subdivision thereof providing a group living arrange-
ment in which one ( 1) or more individuals are cared for and
maintained in residence ;
( 12) "Public medical institution " means any public
in-

ofwhich

and medical treatment

205.200 Eligibility for public assistance .
( 1 ) A needy aged person , a needy blind person , a needy
child , or a relative with whom a needy child lives shall be eligible
to receive a public assistance grant only if he has made a proper
application or an application has been made on his behalf in
the manner and form prescribed by regulation . No individual
shall be eligible to receive public assistance under more than
one ( 1 ) catagory of public assistance for the same period of
time .
( 2 ) Public assistance shall not be payable to or in behalf
of any individual who is an inmate of a public institution , except

( 3) The commissioner may , by regulation , prescribe as a
condition of eligibility that a needy child regularly attend school ,

Section 9. Claim and lien ; recovery during life of recipient .
(1) Any claim under the Act may be precipitated and the
lien provided by this Act may be enforceable during the life-
time of any person who has received public assistance as needy
aged or needy blind, in order to recover any amount obtained
as a result of such person knowingly making a false statement
or representation or knowingly failing to disclose a fact to pro-
cure , increase, or continue any material benefit for himself .
Section 10. Revolving Fund ; deposits .
The amounts recovered under Sections 7, 8, or 9 of this Act
shall be used for the expense and administration of said sections
and to reimburse the fund from which the amount recovered was
originally disbursed . All money recovered shall be deposited to
a special account in the Revolving , Trust and Agency Fund ac-
count of the department to be administered and disbursed in the
same manner and under the same conditions and requirements
as is provided by law for other special funds in the State Treasury .
Any balance in this fund shall not lapse at any time but shall be
continuously available to the department for the purposes set
out in this section . A general statement that all continuing ap-
propriations are repealed shall not be construed as repealing this
section .

58 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 11. Standards for public medical and private in-
stitutions .
( 1 ) Each private institution or public medical institution
that accepts or has facilities available to public assistance recip-
ients shall make provisions for and comply with reasonable stand-
ards established by the department for fire protection and safety ,
plumbing and sanitation , lighting , heating and ventilation , living
arrangements , diet and health facilities , and such other factors
as may be necessary to promote the health and welfare of the
residents cared for in such institutions .
(2 ) The department shall make , or cause to be made , an
inspection of such institutions at least once each year and shall
prescribe by regulation the manner and form of reports such
institutions must file with the department and the records to be
kept by such institutions .
If any other state agency or authority is authorized by law
to inspect , approve or license any institution as defined by this
Act regarding any of the standards established by this Act , the
department shall accept such inspections , approvals or license as
compliance with such standards . Such other state agency or
authority shall , at the request of the department , furnish to the

CHAPTER 34
(S. B. 102 )
AN ACT establishing County Social Service Advisory Committees for
public assistance and child welfare .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Organization
( 1 ) There is hereby established for each county in this
Commonwealth a Social Service Advisory Committee for the
purpose of advising the Department of Economic Security on
the administration in each county of the public assistance and
child welfare programs .
( 2 ) Each Social Service Advisory Committee shall consist
of not less than seven ( 7) members , each of whom shall be a
representative citizen resident in the county .
(3 ) Members of each Social Service Advisory Committee
shall be selected as follows :
( a ) The county judge in each county shall either serve
as a member or shall select another member of the fiscal court of
the county to serve .
(b ) The ministers of the county shall nominate one ( 1 )
of their number to serve ; but if they decline to nominate a mem-
ber , then the Commissioner of Economic Security shall nominate
a minister to serve .

60 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( c) The businessmen of the county through such organiza-
tion or organizations as they may have or may form for this pur-
pose , including service clubs , shall nominate two ( 2 ) of their
number to serve ; but if they decline to nominate two ( 2) mem-
bers, then the Commissioner of Economic Security shall nominate
one ( 1 ) or ( 2 ) businessmen, as the case may be, to serve .
( d) The veterans of the county through such organization
or organizations as they may have or may form for this purpose
shall nominate one ( 1 ) of their number to serve; but if they
decline to nominate a member , then the Commissioner of Econo-
mic Security shall nominate a veteran to serve .
( e) The women of the county through such organization
or organizations as they may have or may form for this purpose ,
such as a League of Women Voters , Women's Club or Parent
Teachers Association , shall nominate two ( 2 ) of their number
to serve ; but if they decline to nominate two ( 2 ) members , then
the Commissioner of Economic Security shall nominate one ( 1 )
or two ( 2 ) representative women , as the case may be, to serve .
(f) No nominations from organizations formed for the
purpose of providing membership herein shall be accepted by the
Commissioner of Economic Security unless either there is no
organization in the county representative of the groups herein
designated for membership , or there are more organizations in
the county representative of the groups herein designated for
membership than there are members to be nominated and a spe-
cial organization is formed from among the several existing or-
ganizations to make nominations .
(g ) In the event more nominations are received from ap-
propriate organizations than there are members to be nominated ,
the Commissioner of Economic Security shall request the or-
ganizations to agree among themselves to provide the correct
number of nominations , but if they do not so agree then the Com-
missioner of Economic Security shall select the appropriate num-
ber of members from among the nominations received .
(4) Each member nominated to the Social Service Ad-
visory Committee shall be appointed by the Governor to serve
for a period of four (4) years , the initial term beginning July 1,
1952 , and until a successor has qualified , and may be removed
by the Governor for good cause only ; provided that the county
judge or his representative shall serve during the term for which

CHAPTER 34 61
the county judge is elected or appointed . In the event of a
vacancy , nomination for the unexpired term of such vacancy shall
be made in accordance with subsection ( 3 ) of this section .
(5) The county judge or his representative shall serve as
chairman of the committee in his county and shall designate
another member of the committee to serve as secretary. Each
committee shall meet once each month and at such other times
as it may deem necessary .

AN ACT concerning Commonwealth's attorneys , assistant Common-
wealth's attorneys , district detectives and stenographers .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 69.070 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
In counties having a population of 200,000 or more , the
Commonwealth's attorney shall appoint a first, second and third
assistant Commonwealth's attorney for terms of four years each ,
but they may be removed at any time by the Commonwealth's
attorney . In such counties where there are two or more criminal
divisions of the circuit court , the Commonwealth's attorney may
appoint , in addition to a first, second and third assistant Common-
wealth's attorney , such other assistant Commonwealth's attorneys ,
not to exceed four or a total of seven assistant Commonwealth
Attorneys , as the Commonwealth's attorney may deem necessary ,

64 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
one of whom shall act as secretary to the grand jury. The first
and second assistant Commonwealth's attorneys shall each receive
an annual salary of not less than $5,000 nor more than $6,000 .
Other assistant Commonwealth's attorneys in counties having
two or more criminal divisions of the circuit court , shall receive
an annual salary of not less than $4,800 . Salaries of assistant
Commonwealth's attorneys shall be payable in equal monthly
installments out of the county levy. Such salaries may be in-
creased by the fiscal court in accordance with KRS 64.520 .
Section 2. Section 69.100 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
In counties having a population of 200,000 or over the Com-
monwealth's attorney shall appoint three stenographers for terms
of four years subject to removal at any time by the Common-
wealth's attorney . Two of the stenographers shall do steno-
graphic work and typewriting for the Commonwealth's attorney
and perform other duties assigned to them by the Common-
wealth's attorney . They shall each receive an annual salary of
not less than $2700 . One stenographer shall perform the duties
of record clerk and shall receive an annual salary of not less than
$3600 . The salaries of all stenographers shall be payable out
of the county levy in equal monthly installments and such salaries
may be increased by the fiscal court in accordance with the pro-
visions of KRS 64.520 . The stenographers shall have the same
power of administering an oath as a notary public .
Section 3. Subsection ( 1 ) of section 69.110 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) In counties having a population of 150,000 or more
the Commonwealth's attorney shall appoint four district detec-
tives for a term of four years subject to removal at any time by
the Commonwealth's attorney . The district detectives shall each
be paid a salary of $3,600 annually , paid out of the county levy
in equal monthly installments . Such salaries may be increased
by the fiscal court in accordance with KRS 64.520 .
Section 4. The salaries of assistant Commonwealth's at-
torneys , stenographers and district detectives prescribed by sec-
tion 1 , 2 and 3 of this Act shall be paid notwithstanding the pro-
visions of KRS 64.520 .

CHAPTER 38 65
Section 5. Whereas , in counties having a population of
200,000 or more , an increase in the number of prosecutions in
the criminal branch of the circuit courts has necessitated the
creation of additional criminal division of such courts ; and
Whereas it has been necessary to employ additional as-
sistant Commonwealth's attorneys and stenographers for the con-
duct of the business of such courts ; and
Whereas it is immediately necessary to provide for adequate
compensation to such persons;
An emergency is hereby declared to exist and this Act shall
become a law immediately upon
itspassage and approved by
the Governor
.
Approved March 12
, 1952

80 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
"The Secretary of State may appoint as many notary pub-
lics as he deems necessary , who shall hold office for four years .
Any person desiring to be appointed a notary public shall make
written application to the Secretary of State. The application
shall be approved by the Circuit Judge, Circuit Clerk , County
Judge , County Clerk , Justice of the Peace or a member of the
General Assembly of the county of the residence of the appli-
cant . No officer shall charge or accept any fee for approving
the application . A notary public must be twenty -one years of
age , a resident of the county from which he makes his applica-
tion , of good moral character and capable of discharging the
duties imposed upon him by this chapter , and the endorsement
of the officer approving the application shall so state. The Secre-
tary of State in his certificate of appointment to the applicant
must designate the limits within which the notary is to act. Be-
fore a notary acts , he must take an oath in the county court of
his county that he will honestly and diligently discharge the
duties of his office . He must in the same court give an obligation
with good security for the proper discharge of the duties of his of-
fice. Every certificate of a notary public shall state the date of the
expiration of his commission . The Secretary of State shall give
to each notary appointed a certificate of his appointment under
the seal of the Commonwealth of Kentucky in lieu of a commis-
sion heretofore required to be issued to said notary by the Gov-
ernor of Kentucky , and receive the same fee for the certificate
that he now receives for attesting the commission issued by the
Governor.
Section 2. All acts and parts of acts in conflict with this
act are hereby repealed to the extent of the conflict ."

AN ACT relating
tosocial and welfare services in certain cities and
counties
.
Be
itenacted
bythe General Assembly
ofthe Common-

ofKentucky

CHAPTEF 48 85
Section 1. The legislative body of any city of the
third class or fiscal court of the county containing such city
is authorized to pay public funds to any private
agency or organization , or combination of such agencies or
organizations , located in the city or county , performing
charitable and welfare work among the citizens generally
of the city or county .
Section 2. The legislative body of any city of the
third class or the fiscal court of the county containing such
city, or both acting jointly , may create a department , board
or other agency for the purpose of serving either the county
or the city or both , in the administration and supervision
of : All forms of public assistance , general relief , and social
services to adults and children in homes or eleemosynary
and correctional institutions ; city and county eleemosynary
institutions ; city and county penal institutions including
the establishment of rules and regulations for the custody,
government , and parole of inmates ; other public welfare
activities or services which may be placed within the con-
trol of the board .

(S. B. 135)
Approved March 24, 1952
AN ACT to prevent trespassing upon posted railroad property .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Any person who trespasses upon any part of a
railroad yard , place of storage , shop or other railroad structure
without the consent of an authorized official of the railroad com-
pany owning or controlling the property , and after the railroad
company has posted such part of
itsproperty with
asign
atleast
twelve by twenty
-four inches bearing conspicuously thereon the
words
"Railroad Property
-No Trespassing
"and showing gener-
ally
orspecifically the extent
ofthe posted property
, shall
be
fined not less than ten dollars nor more than one hundred dollars
,
orimprisoned for not less than ten days nor more than thirty
days
,
orboth
. For the second and each subsequent offense the
maximum and minimum penalties shall
bedoubled
.

86 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 2. This Act does not apply to railroad stations ,
ticket offices or office buildings , to the peaceful presence or activ-
ities of employees of a railroad company on any railroad proper-
ty during a labor dispute or at any other time, or to public or pri-
vate roads , streets , ways or easements crossing railroad property
or over or on which railroad tracks are maintained .
Section 3. If the authorized official of a railroad company
notifies the parent or guardian of a minor in writing that such
minor has been found trespassing upon any railroad property in
violation of this Act , and thereafter the parent or guardian know-
ingly permits or directs the minor to trespass upon railroad prop-
erty that has been posted as provided in Section 1 of this
Act, or fails to make reasonable effort to prevent the minor from
so trespassing , the parent or guardian shall be fined not less than
ten dollars nor more than one hundred dollars .
Approved March 25 , 1952
CHAPTER 49
(S. B. 138)
AN ACT relating to the condemnation of property for construction ,
reconstruction , and maintenance of highways and bridges and facili-
ties related thereto , and repealing KRS 177.075 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Commonwealth of Kentucky , Department
of Highways , when it has, by Official Order, designated the
route , location or relocation of a highway , limited access high-
way , bridge , roadside park , borrow pit , quarry , garage or other
property or structure deemed necessary for the construction , re-
construction , or maintenance of an adequate system of highways ,
may , if unable to contract or agree with the owner or owners
thereof , condemn the lands or material , or the use and occupancy
of the lands designated as necessary . All property acquired
under the provisions of this Act shall be in fee simple when-
ever so specified in the petition filed in the action . The Official
Order of the Department of Highways shall be conclusive of
the public use of the condemned property and the condemnor's
decision as to the necessity for taking the property will not be

CHAPTER 49 87
disturbed in the absence of fraud, bad faith , or abuse of dis-
cretion .
The provisions of this Act shall apply whether cost of prop-
erty condemned is borne by Commonwealth of Kentucky , De-
partment of Highways , or any county or municipality thereof .
The proceedings for condemnation shall be as provided in
this Act .

Section 4. Upon the application of the petitioner , and
the filing of any necessary affidavits , the Clerk of the Court
shall issue process against the owner to show cause why the
petitioner does not have the right to condemn the lands , or the
use and occupation thereof , and material sought to be con-
demned . The summons shall contain a statement of the amount
of the award . The Clerk shall make such orders as to non-
residents and persons under disability as are required by the
Civil Code in actions against them in Circuit Court .
Section 5. Any answer or other pleading filed by the
owner or owners in response to the summons shall be filed on
or before the first day of the first regular term of the County
Court after the owner or owners have been summoned the time
prescribed by the Civil Code , and shall be confined solely to
the question of the right of the petitioner to condemn the prop-
erty sought to be condemned , but without prejudice to the
owner's right to appeal from the amount of the compensation
awarded in the manner provided in Subsection (1) of Section 7
of this Act .
Section 6 .
(1) At the first regular term of the County Court after the
owner has been summoned the length of time prescribed by the
Civil Code before an answer is required , the Court shall examine
the report of the Commissioners to determine whether it con-
forms to the provisions of Section 3 of this Act. If the report of
the Commissioners is not in the proper form the Court shall re-
quire the Commissioners to make such corrections as are neces-
sary.
(2) If no answer or other pleading is filed by the owner

CHAPTER 49 89
or owners putting in issue the right of the petitioner to con-
demn the lands or the use and occupation thereof and the ma-
terial sought to be condemned , the Court shall enter a judg-
ment which shall contain , in substance :
(a) A finding that the petitioner has the right, under the
provisions of Section 1 of this Act, to condemn the lands or the
use and occupation thereof and material ;
(b) A finding that the report of the Commissioners con-
forms to the provisions of Section 3 of this Act ;
(c) An authorization to take possession of lands and ma-
terials for the purposes and under the conditions and limitations ,
if any , set forth in the petition , upon payment of all costs and
the payment to the owner , or to the Clerk of the Court, the
amount of the compensation awarded by the Commissioners ;
(d) Proper provision for the conveyance of the title to the
land and material , to the extent condemned , as adjudged therein ,
in the event no appeal is taken as provided in Subsection (1) of
Section 7 of this Act .
Any appeal from such judgment by either party or both
parties shall be confined solely to exceptions to the amount of
compensation awarded by the Commissioners .
(3) If the owner has filed answer or pleading putting in
issue the right of the petitioner to condemn the lands or use and
occupation thereof and the material sought to be condemned , the
Court shall , without intervention of jury, proceed to hear and
determine whether or not the petitioner has such right. If the
Court determines that petitioner has such right, judgment , as
provided for in Subsection 2 of this Section , shall be entered . If
the Court determines that petitioner does not have such right,
it shall enter a judgment which shall contain , in substance :
(a) A finding that the report of the Commissioners con-
forms to Section 3 of this Act ;
(b) A finding that the petitioner is not authorized to con-
demn the lands or the use and occupation thereof and the ma-
terial for the purposes and under the conditions and limitations
set forth in the petition , stating the particular ground or grounds
on which the petitioner is not so authorized ;
(c) An order dismissing the petition and directing the
petitioner to pay all costs .

90 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 7 .
(1) Within 30 days from the date of a judgment authoriz-
ing the petitioner to take possession of the land and/or material ,
an appeal may be taken to the Circuit Court by either or both
parties by filing with the Clerk of the Circuit Court a certi-
fied copy of the judgment , a statement of the parties to the
appeal, and any exceptions the appellant has to the award made
by the Commissioner . No transcript shall be required to be filed
by the appellant of other parts of the record , but when an
appeal is filed with the Clerk of the Circuit Court shall certify
that fact to the Clerk of the County Court and the latter im-
mediately shall transfer the original papers to the Clerk of the
Circuit Court . The statement of grounds of appeal of either
party may be amended as provided in the Civil Code for the
amendment of a pleading. The appeal shall be tried de novo ,
but shall be limited to the questions which are raised in the
original exceptions or statement of the grounds of appeal , or
as amended. Provided , however , that in any case where the
road , bridge , or other structure has not been completed by the
time the action ordinarily would stand for trial on appeal , either
party may file an affidavit stating that fact , and the case shall
not stand for trial until such project is completed , but no trial
shall be delayed for this reason beyond one year from the date
of filing said affidavit . All questions of fact pertaining to the
amount of compensation to the owner or owners shall be deter-
mined by jury , which jury on the application of either party , shall
be sent by the Court , in the charge of the Sheriff, to view the
land and material . Upon an appeal to the Circuit Court and
after a jury trial therein , and if possession of the land and ma-
terial condemned has not previously been taken by the con-
demnor , it may do so upon payment to the owners , or to the
Clerk of the Circuit Court , the amount of the compensation ad-
judged by the Circuit Court to be due the owners and by paying
all costs of the action .
(2) Within 30 days from the date of a judgment dismissing
a petition , an appeal may be taken to the Circuit Court by the
petitioner by filing with the Clerk of the Circuit Court a state-
ment of the parties to the appeal and a certified copy of the
judgment . No transcript shall be required to be filed by the

CHAPTER 49 91
appellant of other parts of the record , but when an appeal is
filed the Clerk of the Circuit Court shall certify that fact to the
Clerk of the County Court and the latter immediately shall
transfer the original papers to the Clerk of the Circuit Court .
Upon reasonable notice to the owner , such an appeal shall be
tried at the earliest convenience of the Court without the inter-
vention of a jury , at any regular or special term or by the Judge
in vacation . At such trial, either party may present evidence
relating to the right of the petitioner to condemn the land and
material sought to be condemned . If the Judge of the Circuit
Court finds that the petitioner has the right to condemn said
property , the case shall not be remanded but the Judge or Court
shall enter an order setting forth his findings and authorizing the
petitioner to take possession of the lands and materials for the
purposes and under the conditions and limitations set forth in
the petition upon the payment to the owner , or to the Clerk of
the Circuit Court, the amount of compensation awarded by the
Commissioners in their report to the County Court. Within 30
days from the date of the order of the Circuit Court authorizing
the petitioner to take such possession , either party may file ex-
ceptions to the award of the Commissioners . If no exceptions

shall enter a final judgment in the case . If either party files
exceptions pertaining to the amount of the award , the issues of
fact shall be tried by a jury in the Circuit Court as though on
appeal from a judgment of the County Court, as provided in
Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 7 of this Act .

CHAPTER 50
(S. B. 140)
AN ACT relating to a Department of Mental Health , and its establish-
ment , organization , personnel , powers , duties , functions , funds and
facilities , including provisions for the transfer of functions , funds
and facilities from other state departments or agencies .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. ( 1 ) There hereby is created a Department of
Mental Health which shall constitute a statutory administrative
department of the state government within the meaning of Chap-
ter 12 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes . The department shall
be headed by a Commissioner of Mental Health who shall be
the executive officer and shall have sole charge of the adminis-
tration of the department .
(2) The commissioner shall have authority , with the ap-
proval of the Governor , to appoint an assistant commissioner
and directors of the various divisions in the department , and
prescribe their duties . The assistant commissioner may be
designated as director of a division .
(3) The commissioner shall have authority to prescribe
rules and regulations for the administration of the department
and of the institutions under the control of the department .
Sec . 2. ( 1) The Commissioner of Mental Health shall be
a qualified physician with approved training in psychiatry and
with a minimum of five years ' successful experience in clinical
psychiatry and administrative psychiatry . The assistant com-
missioner shall have the same qualifications , except that the
minimum experience requirement shall be three years . The
superintendents of the institutions operated by the department
shall be qualified physicians with a minimum of three years'
experience in the administration of the hospitals providing care
for the mentally
ill. Professional personnel
ofthe department

94 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall possess such qualifications as are prescribed by the com-
missioner .
(2) The compensation of the commissioner shall be fixed
by the Governor , without regard to any limitation imposed by
KRS 64.640 . The compensation of other personnel of the de-
partment shall be fixed by the commissioner in accordance with
KRS 64.640 and 64.650 . The commissioner , the assistant com-
missioner , and the superintendents of the institutions operated
by the department shall be allowed maintenance for themselves
and family in addition to salary . Maintenance may be allowed
to institutional employes in accordance with regulations pre-
scribed by the Department of Finance under KRS 64.640 .
Sec . 3. There hereby is created an Advisory Council of
Mental Health , within the Department of Mental Health , which
shall consist of the Commissioner of Mental Health , ex officio ,
and twelve members , including physicians , psychiatrists and
laymen , appointed by the Governor , who shall serve for terms
of four years , and until their successors are appointed , except
that the members first appointed shall be so appointed that the
terms of three members will expire each year . The appointed
members shall receive their actual and necessary expenses in
attending meetings of the council . The Governor shall each
year appoint a chairman from among the psychiatrist members
of the council . The council shall serve in an advisory capacity to
the Commissioner of Mental Health .
Sec . 4. The Department of Mental Health shall :
(1) Exercise all functions of the state in relation to the
administration and operation of all state institutions for the care
and treatment of the mentally
ill,not including any institution
for the feeble
-minded
.
(
2) Establish
oracquire
,
inaccordance with the provi-
sions
ofKRS 56.440
to56.550
, other
oradditional facilities for
psychiatric care and treatment
ofpersons who are
ormay be-

Sec . 7. No employe of an institution operated by the
Department of Mental Health , after having served in his position
continuously for six months , shall be removed from his position
except as provided in this section . The superintendent of the
institution may suspend the employe , from pay and from duties ,
upon a written statement of cause furnished to the employe .
Within ten days thereafter the employe may apply to the Com-
missioner of Mental Health for a hearing . Thereupon the com-
missioner shall convene a board of review consisting of the com-
missioner and two other suitable persons appointed by him , and
shall fix a time and place at which the board will conduct a
hearing , of which reasonable notice shall be given to the employe
and the superintendent . Upon the basis of the hearing , the
board shall either authorize the removal of the employe , or
direct that he be reinstated , either in his former position or in a
similar position in another of the institutions operated by the
department . Any order of removal or reinstatement shall
relate back to the date of suspension. Unless application for a
hearing is made within the time specified , the employe shall be
deemed to have been removed as of the date of suspension.
Sec . 8. The Commissioner of Mental Health may transfer
between the institutions operated by the department , or to the
headquarters of the department , any superintendent or institu-
tional employe . Necessary moving expenses involved in such
transfers shall be paid by the department .
Sec . 9. Neither the commissioner , the assistant com-
missioner , the director of a division , the superintendent of any
institution , nor any other officer or employe of the Department
of Mental Health shall be permitted to engage in any political
activity . Any political activity on the part of an institutional
employe shall be cause for his dismissal , in accordance with
section 8 of this Act .
Sec . 10. No officer or employe of any institution operated
by the Department of Mental Health shall be required to give
personal attendance as a witness in any civil suit out of the
county in which the institution is located , but his deposition shall
be taken in lieu thereof .

SECTION 1. As used in this chapter , unless the context
requires otherwise :

the City , whether it is the Board of Aldermen , General Council ,
Board of Commissioners , or otherwise .

fumes , gasses , odors , or particulate matter , or any combination
thereof .
(c) "District" means an air pollution control district .
(d) "Person " means any individual , firm , co-partnership ,

organization , estate , trust , receiver , syndicate , any county , city ,
municipality , district (for air pollution control or otherwise ), or
other political subdivision , or any group or combination acting
as a unit , and the plural as well as the singular unit .
(e) "Ringelmann Chart " means that standard published by
the United States Bureau of Mines to determine the density of
smoke.

SECTION 2. In each county of the Commonwealth

county within which it is situated .

prevent or control the emission of smoke, fumes, or other
substances which cause or contribute to such pollution .
SECTION 8. The legislative body of a city of the first
or second class may adopt an ordinance declaring that there is
need for an air pollution control district to function if from the
evidence received at such public hearing it finds :
(1) That the air within such city is so polluted with air
contaminants as to be injurious to health , or an obstruction to
the free use of property , or offensive to the senses of a consider-
able number of persons , so as to interfere with the comfortable
enjoyment of life or property .
(2) And further that for any reason it is not practical to
rely upon the enactment or enforcement of local ordinances to
prevent or control the emission of smoke , fumes , or other sub-
stances which cause or contribute to such pollution .
SECTION 9 .
(1) A resolution of the fiscal court declaring that there is
need for an air pollution control district to function is sufficient
if it finds that there is need for an air pollution control district
to function , and finds in substantially the wording of Section 7 of
this chapter that both the conditions enumerated therein exist .
No further detail is necessary .
(2) An ordinance of the legislative body of a city of the
first or second class declaring that there is need for an air pol-
lution control district to function is sufficient if it finds that there
is need for an air pollution control district to function , and
finds in substantially the wording of Section 8 of this chapter
that both the conditions enumerated therein exist. No further
detail is necessary .
SECTION 10. A copy of a resolution of the fiscal court
declaring that there is need for an air pollution control district ,
duly certified by the county clerk, is admissible in evidence in
any suit , action or proceeding .

SECTION 15 .
ARTICLE II
OFFICERS
(1) The members of the fiscal court of a county shall be ,
and they are hereby designated as , and empowered to act as ,
ex officio the air pollution control board of the air pollution
control district in such county .
(2) All county officers , their assistants , clerks , deputies and
employees , and all other county employees shall be ex officio
officers , assistants , deputies , clerks and employees respectively
of the air pollution control district in the county in which they
are employed . Except as otherwise provided in this chapter , they
shall perform respectively the same various duties for the air
pollution control district as for the county without additional
compensation , in order to carry out the provisions of this chapter .
(3) The provisions of this section shall not be applicable to
any county containing a city of the first or second class .
SECTION 16 .
(1) In a county containing a city of the first or second
class , the air pollution control board of the air pollution control
district shall consist of seven members , three of whom shall be
appointed by the County Judge , subject to the approval of the
fiscal court , and four of whom shall be appointed by the Mayor
of the city of the first or second class within such county . The
Mayoral appointments shall be subject to the approval of the
legislative body of the city .
(2) The Mayor shall appoint , subject to the approval of
the legislative body of the city, one member for a term of one
year , one member for a term of two years , one member for a
term of three years, and one member for a term of four years , and
the County Judge , subject to the approval of the fiscal court ,
shall appoint one member for a term of two years , one member
for a term of three years , and one member for a term of four
years , and upon the expiration of each of said terms respectively ,

110 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
and thereafter , the term of each of such members shall be four
years , and until their successors are appointed and qualified .
(3) All air pollution control board members appointed
pursuant to this section must be freeholders within the district ;
those appointed by the County Judge must be residents of such
county , and those appointed by the Mayor must be residents of
such city .
(4) Not more than four of the seven board members
appointed pursuant to this section shall be of the same political
party affiliation , nor shall an officer or employee of such city or
county , whether holding a paid or unpaid position , be eligible
for appointment to the board .
(5) A member of the air pollution control board is eligible
to succeed himself . A vacancy in the membership shall be filled
by an appointee of the Mayor or of the County Judge as the
case may be , for the unexpired portion of the term . An appointee
to a vacancy shall have the same qualifications as any regularly
appointed member .
(6) Any member of the board appointed by a Mayor may
be removed, for cause , after a hearing , by the legislative body of
such city , and after ten days notice in writing shall have been
given to the member , which notice shall embrace the charges
preferred against him . At the hearing he may be represented
by counsel . The finding of the legislative body shall be final and
removal results in vacancy in such office .
(7) Any member of the board appointed by a County Judge
may be removed , for cause , after a hearing , by the Fiscal Court
of such County , and after ten days notice in writing shall have
been given to the member , which notice shall embrace the
charges preferred against him . At the hearing he may be repre-
sented by counsel . The finding of the Fiscal Court shall be final
and removal results in vacancy in such office .
(8) As used in this section "Mayor" means the chief ex-
ecutive of the city whether the official designation of his office
is Mayor , City Manager , or otherwise .
SECTION 17. All air pollution control board members
shall serve without compensation other than recovery of actual
expenses from the district funds .

CHAPTER 56
(S. B. 151 )
AN ACT concerning organization and government of cities of the first
class and agencies thereof , repealing Section 69.420, 91.010 , 91.030,
91.040 , 91.050, 91.070, 93.030 ( 2) and (3 ) , 93.040, 93.130, 93.140, 93.150,

Revised Statutes and amending Sections 93.060, 93.110, 93.120 , 95.160 ,
95.180, 95.200 and 95.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Section 69.420 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes pertaining to the appointment qualifications , duties and
compensation of assistant city attorneys in cities of the first class ,
is hereby repealed .
SECTION 2. The following statutes pertaining to the finan-
cial administration in cities of the first class are hereby repealed :
( a ) Section 91.010 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes de-
limiting the fiscal year .
( b ) Section 91.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the receipt , custody and disbursement of city funds .
( c ) Section 91.040 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the selection and requirement of depositories for city
funds .
( d ) Section 91.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the accounts and records kept by the director of
finance ; and auditing of claims against the city; the counter-
signed of city checks .
( e ) Section 91.070 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the reports of the director of finance to city officials .

CHAPTER 56 131
SECTION 3. The following statutes pertaining to the
administration of the department of works in cities of the first
class are hereby repealed :
( a ) Section 93.030 ( 2 ) and ( 3 ) of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes pertaining to the examination of the chief engineer by
an examining board of the city legislative body as a requisite
for the position .
(b ) Section 93.040 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to a separate plumbing department within the depart-
ment of works .
SECTION 4. Sections 93.130 , 93.140 , 93.150 , 93.160 ,
93.170 , 93.180 , 93.190 , 93.200 , 93.210 , 93.220 , 93.230 , 93.240 ,
93.250 , 93.260 , and 93.270 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
pertaining to the creation , functions of the sewerage commission ,
the construction of a sewerage system under such commission
and the issuing of bonds therefor are hereby repealed .
SECTION 5. The following statutes pertaining to the
functions of the department of safety and the members of the
police and fire departments in cities of the first class are hereby
repealed :
( a ) Section 95.080 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to disciplinary powers of the director of safety over
members of the police and fire departments .
(b ) Section 95.100 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to rules of the director of safety for the police depart-
ment .
( c) Section 95.110 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the rank and appointment of personnel of the police
department .
( d ) Section 95.120 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the compensation of personnel of the police depart-
ment .
( e ) Section 95.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the appointment of the chief and assistant chiefs of
the police department by the director of safety.
( f) Section 95.140 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes per-
taining to the powers and duties of the chief of police .

( g ) Section 95.150 ( 2 ) , ( 3 ) , ( 4) and ( 5) of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes pertaining to the powers of the police
to preserve peace , enforce laws and ordinances , report violations
