CHAPTER 81
(S. B. 204)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Danville, in Boyle
County.
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Danville , in Boyle
County , is such as to justify its being classified as a city of the third
class .
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The City of Danville , in Boyle County, is transferred from
the fourth to the third class of cities .
CHAPTER 82
(S. B. 208)
AN ACT relating to election or rejection of the Workmen's Compensa-
tion Act by employes , amending section 342.395 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 342.395 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
In the event an employer elects to operate under this
chapter, then every employe of such employer , as a part
of his contract of hiring or who may be employed at the
time of the acceptance of the provisions of this chapter by
such employer , shall be deemed to have accepted all the
provisions of this chapter and shall be bound thereby unless
he shall have filed, prior to the injury , a written notice to
the contrary with the employer . Until such notice to the
contrary is so given to the employer, the measure of lia-
bility of such employer shall be determined according to the
compensation provisions of this chapter . Any such employe

208 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may, without prejudice to any existing right or claim , with-
draw his election to reject this chapter by filing with the
employer a written notice of withdrawal , stating the date
when the withdrawal is to become effective . Following the
filing of such notice, the status of the party withdrawing
shall become the same as if the former election to reject this
chapter had not been made , except that withdrawal shall
not be effective as to any injury sustained less than one
week after the notice is filed .
Approved March 24, 1952
CHAPTER 83
(S. B. 210 )
AN ACT relating to the transfer of the Division of Child Welfare to the
Kentucky Children's Bureau within the Department of Welfare ,
and services to needy children .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. The Child Welfare Division of the Depart-
ment of Economic Security , with its personnel , properties ,
facilities , funds and appropriations , hereby is transferred
to and made a division of the Kentucky Children's Bureau
created by H. B. 157 of the 1952 General Assembly . The
Division of Child Welfare shall be headed by a director
who is thoroughly trained and experienced in child welfare
work , and who shall be appointed by the Commissioner of
Welfare with the prior written approval of the Governor .
The Division of Child Welfare shall continue to exercise all
powers and duties now conferred or imposed upon it by
law.
Sec. 2. Section 195.020 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The Department shall exercise all functions of the
state in relation to:
(a) Administration and supervision of state unem-
ployment insurance .
(b) Administration and supervision of state employ-
ment offices .

CHAPTER 83 209
(c) Administration and supervision of all forms of
public assistance including general home relief , outdoor and
indoor care for persons in need , old age assistance , aid to
dependent children , and aid to the blind and otherwise
handicapped .
(d) Administration and supervision of social services
to children and families who are applicants or recipients of
public assistance .
(2) The functions heretofore performed by the De-
partment of Industrial Relations in respect of the functions
above outlined are hereby transferred to and vested in the
Department of Economic Security . All administrative func-
tions of the nature outlined above , heretofore performed by
other agencies of the state , are hereby abolished and shall
hereafter be performed by the Department of Economic
Security .
Sec . 3. Section 195.030 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The department shall be divided into the following
administrative bureaus and divisions :
(a) A Bureau of Employment Security which shall be
divided into :
1. A Division of Unemployment Insurance ; and
2. A Division of Employment Service .
(b) A Bureau of Social Services which shall be
divided into :
1. A Division of Public Assistance ; and
2. A Division of Service to Needy Children .
(c) Such other bureaus , divisions , or combinations of
existing bureaus , divisions , or agencies as the commissioner
may from time to time prescribe in accordance with KRS
12.030 , and to which he may assign duties , functions , and
responsibilities .
(2) The Governor may, by executive order , transfer
any bureau , division , or agency of the State Government to
the Department of Economic Security for the purpose of
economy, efficiency , and improved administration .
Sec . 4. Section 195.050 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :

210 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
( 1) The Bureau of Social Services shall be headed by
an executive director , and all the powers , functions , and
duties heretofore performed by the Division of Public
Assistance in the Department of Welfare are hereby trans-
ferred to and vested in the Bureau of Social Services . The
executive director , with the prior approval of the commis-
sioner , is authorized to integrate and combine the functions
and duties of the divisions within such bureau insofar as
he considers such integration and combination economical
and practicable for the proper and efficient administration
of the bureau .
( 2 ) The existing Division of Public Assistance in the
Department of Welfare is hereby transferred to and shall
hereafter constitute a division of the Bureau of Social
Services . All the powers , functions , and duties heretofore
performed by the Division of Public Assistance in the De-
partment of Welfare shall continue to be vested in the
Division of Public Assistance in the Bureau of Social
Services .
(3) There is hereby established a Division of Service
to Needy Children which shall constitute a division in the
Bureau of Social Services .
Sec . 5. Section 195.080 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
It is the purpose of KRS 195.010 to 195.090 to provide
a unified , integrated , and comprehensive plan or organiza-
tion of those services , functions , and duties heretofore per-
formed by the Unemployment Compensation Commission ,
the Employment Service Commission , and the Division of
Public Assistance, under the direction , supervision , and
control of the Commissioner of Economic Security . After
KRS 195.010 to 195.090 are in full effect no such functions
shall be exercised by any agencies other than as provided
in those sections . All laws or parts of laws in conflict with
KRS 195.010 to 195.090 are hereby repealed to the extent
of such conflict .
Sec . 6. Section 199.011 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 199.410 to 199.670 , unless the context
otherwise requires :

CHAPTER 83 211
(1) " Commissioner " means the Commissioner of
Welfare .
(2) " Department " means the Department of Welfare ,
acting through the Kentucky Children's Bureau and the
Division of Child Welfare .
(3) "Division " means the Division of Child Welfare
in the Kentucky Children's Bureau .
(4) " Child" means any person who has not reached
his twenty -first birthday .
(5) " Child-caring institution or agency " means any
agency or institution , other than a state institution or one
operated primarily for educational or medical purposes , in
which four or more children , not related by blood , adoption
or marriage to the person maintaining the institution , are
cared for and maintained in residence for compensation or
otherwise .
(6 ) " Child-placing institution or agency " means any
agency or institution other than a state agency which
arranges for the placement of children in child -caring
homes or child -caring institutions , or which arranges for
the placement of children for adoption ;
(7) " Child welfare worker " means any employe of
the department designated as such by the commissioner or
social worker employed by a county or city who has been
approved by the department to handle , under its super-
vision , services to children .
Sec . 7. Section 199.410 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The provisions of KRS 199.380 to 199.400 shall
not affect or apply to boarding homes in which children
under the care, custody or control of the department , or
receiving aid from the department, are being boarded , and
which have been approved by the department as meeting
the standards of the department for placement , nor shall
the provisions of KRS 199.380 to 199.400 affect persons
caring for and providing for children related to them by
blood or marriage .
(2 ) KRS 199.380 to 199.400 shall apply only to coun-
ties containing a city of the second class .

212 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 8. Section 199.420 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The commissioner shall have the power and au-
thority to adopt , amend or rescind such rules and regula-
tions as he deems necessary or suitable for the proper ad-
ministration of the functions of the division , including
qualification for the receipt of federal funds and for co-
operation with other state and federal agencies . Such rules
and regulations shall become effective in the manner and
at the time prescribed by the commissioner .
(2) In the administration of KRS 199.410 to 199.670
the commissioner shall cooperate to the fullest extent pos-
sible with any agency of this state or any other state of the
United States .
(3) The commissioner is authorized , subject to the
provisions of KRS Chapters 12 , 42, 45 and 64 , to appoint ,
fix the compensation and prescribe the duties and powers
of such officers and employes as may be necessary in the
performance of his duties under KRS 199.410 to 199.670 .
All positions shall be filled by persons selected and ap-
pointed on a nonpartisan merit basis , in accordance with
merit standards established by law . The commissioner shall
not employ or pay any person who is an officer or commit-
tee member of any political party organization . The com-
missioner may delegate to any such person so appointed
such power and authority as he deems reasonable and
proper for the effective administration of KRS 199.410 to
199.670 .
(4) The commissioner shall have the power and au-
thority to elect coverage for the workers in the division
under the provisions of KRS Chapter 341 , and may elect
coverage for such workers under the Workmen's Compen-
sation Law of this state . In the event such coverage is
elected the payment of contributions under KRS Chapter
341 and premiums under the Workmen's Compensation
Law shall be deemed a proper cost of administration .
( 5 ) The salaries and expenses of the commissioner and
his staff shall be considered a proper cost of administration
of KRS 199.410 to 199.670 , to be charged to the Child Wel

CHAPTER 83 213
fare and Benefit Fund in that proportion which the cost of
such services rendered in the administration of KRS
199.410 to 199.670 bears to the over-all cost of the services
rendered in the administration of the department .
Sec . 9. Section 199.430 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) In the discharge of the duties imposed by KRS
199.410 to 199.670 the commissioner or his duly authorized
representative may administer oaths and affirmations , take
depositions , certify official acts , and issue subpoenas to
compel the attendance of witnesses and production of books ,
papers , correspondence , memoranda and other records con-
sidered necessary and relevant as evidence in connection
with the administration of the division . Such subpoena shall
be served in the same manner as a subpoena issued out of
a circuit court . Witnesses subpoenaed shall be allowed wit-
nesses' fees according to the rates prescribed by KRS
421.010 for each day their attendance is actually required at
a hearing .
(2) No person shall be excused from attending and
testifying or from producing books , papers , correspondence ,
memoranda or other records in response to such subpoena
on the grounds that the evidence required of him may tend
to incriminate him or subject him to a penalty or forfeiture .
No person shall be prosecuted or subjected to any suit ,
penalty , or forfeiture on account of any transaction , matter ,
or thing concerning which he or his agent or worker is
compelled , after having claimed privilege against self-
incrimination , to give evidence , except that such witness so
testifying shall not be exempt from punishment for perjury .
(3) All letters , reports , communications , and other
matters , written or oral , to the division or any of its agents ,
representatives , or employes , or to any board or official
functioning under KRS 199.410 to 199.670 , which have
been written , sent , or made in connection with the require-
ments and administration of the division shall be absolutely
privileged and shall not be the subject matter or basis for
any suit for slander or libel in any court , but no person
testifying before the commissioner or his duly authorized

214 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
representative shall be exempt from punishment for per-
jury . Information obtained shall not be published or be
open for public inspection , except to public employes in
the performance of their duties, but any interested party at
a hearing before the commissioner or his duly authorized
representative shall be supplied with information from such
records to the extent necessary for the proper presentation
ofhis case .
Sec . 10. In addition to any other appropriation there
is hereby appropriated out of the General Expenditure
Fund to the Department of Economic Security the follow-
ing sums for the following purposes for the designated fiscal
years .
Division of Service to Needy Children . Administration
and services . For necessary expenses of administration of
and service by the Division of Service to Needy Children :
1952-53
1953-54 ......
$100,000.00

Sec . 11. This Act shall take effect on July 1, 1952 .
Approved March 24 , 1952
CHAPTER 84
(S. B. 212)
AN ACT relating to pleading , practice and procedure in civil actions and
criminal proceedings .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. The Statute Revision Commission shall trans-
fer all sections of the Civil Code of Procedure , not repealed
herein , to the Kentucky Revised Statutes . The commission
shall make such transfer in accordance with the provisions
of KRS 447.070 to 447.140 , and shall include in the Com-
parative Section Table the disposition of sections of the
Civil Code indicating the assigned KRS number . The com-
mission is authorized to omit any section of the Civil Code
in all cases where there is an identical statute contained in
the KRS . The Reviser of Statutes shall include in the cer-
tificate , required by KRS 447.110 , a statement that he has

CHAPTER 83 215
compared each section so transferred and printed in the
Kentucky Revised Statutes with the section as printed in
the present Civil Code of Procedure , and that the sections
in the Kentucky Revised Statutes are correctly printed .
Sec . 2. The Kentucky Revised Statutes , including all
sections of the Civil Code of Procedure transferred by
virtue of this Act, and the Code of Practice in Criminal
Cases shall be subject to the following rules of construc-
tion:
a. Any reference to a particular title , chapter , article ,
subdivision or section of the Civil Code of Procedure , shall
be to the corresponding section or sections of the Rules of
Civil Procedure , the Rules of the Court of Appeals , or to the
section number of the KRS assigned to the Civil Code
section , whichever is applicable .
b . Any reference to "this Code" or to the "Civil Code
of Procedure , " and like references , shall be to the Rules of
Civil Procedure , the Rules of the Court of Appeals or to the
appropriate section of the KRS transferred from the Civil
Code pursuant to this Act , whichever is applicable .
c. Any reference to a proceeding "in equity ," or words
of like import , shall be to the corresponding action provided
in the Rules of Civil Procedure , for all procedural purposes ,
and , in all such cases an action authorized to be brought in
equity shall not be tried by jury as a matter of right .
Sec . 3. Where a statute grants a right of appeal to
the Court of Appeals in special civil cases , the Rules of
Civil Procedure shall govern the taking of the appeal , unless
in conflict with a specific provision of the statute , except
that any notice of appeal required by the Rules shall be
given within the time specified in the Rules . In all cases
where the statute limits the taking of the appeal to a spe-
cific time the appeal shall be docketed in the Court of Ap-
peals within the time so specified .
Sec . 4. ( 1 ) Each circuit court shall establish , by rule ,
for that court , a regular day or days certain at which time
motions requiring notice and hearing may be heard and dis-
posed of, and other court business dispatched ; provided ,
however , that a minimum of at least one day each month

Sec . 7. Section 156 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The return day of the order of arrest, if issued at the
commencement of the action , shall be the first day of the
next term of the court , which does not begin within ten
days from the date of the order ; if issued afterward , it may
be another day in term , at the option of the plaintiff .
Sec . 8. Section 184 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The order shall not be complied with by the sheriff
until there has been executed in his presence , by one or
more sufficient sureties of the plaintiff, a bond to the de-
fendant, to the effect that the plaintiff shall duly prosecute
the action , and that he shall perform the judgment of the
court therein , by returning the property , if a return thereof
shall be adjudged, and by paying such sums of money as
may be adjudged against him in the action, not exceeding
double the value of the property and the costs of the action .
Any party , or intervenor , establishing a claim to the prop-
erty , shall be entitled to the benefit of the provisions of
said bond .
Sec . 9. Subsection ( 1) of section 194-a of the Code of
Practice in Civil Cases is amended to read as follows :
Any person in whose favor a final judgment in per-
sonam has been entered in any court of record of this state

218 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
may, upon filing affidavit in the office of the clerk of the
court in which the judgment was entered , and in the same
cause in which said judgment was obtained showing the
date of the judgment and the amount due thereon , and that
one or more named persons hold property belonging to , or
are indebted to, the judgment debtor , obtain an order of
garnishment directed to the sheriff , with as many copies as
the plaintiff may direct , requiring him to summon the gar-
nishees named in the affidavit in the manner and at the time
required for an answer by the Rules of Civil Procedure , and
to make due return thereof . The order shall be made re-
turnable as an order of arrest is directed to be returned .
Sec . 10. Section 199 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases in amended to read as follows :
The order of attachment shall be directed and delivered
to the sheriff , with as many copies thereof as the plaintiff
may direct. It shall require him to attach and safely keep
the property of the defendant in his county not exempt from
execution , or so much thereof as will satisfy the plaintiff's
claim specified in his affidavit , which shall be stated in the
order, and the probable costs of the action, not exceeding
thirty dollars ; also to summon the garnishees to answer in
the action in the manner and at the time required for an
answer by the Rules of Civil Procedure , and to make due
return thereof. The order shall be made returnable as an
order of arrest is directed to be returned .
Sec . 11. Section 203 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The order of attachment shall be executed by the
sheriff without delay in the following manner-
1. Upon real property , by leaving with the occupant
thereof, or , if there be no occupant , in a conspicuous place
thereon , a copy of the order .
2. Upon personal property capable of manual deliv-
ery , by taking it into his custody and holding it subject to
the order of the court ; or , if it be held by another officer
under process or under a distress for taxes , by delivering to
him a copy of the order , with a notice specifying the prop-
erty attached .

CHAPTER 83 219
3. Upon other personal property , by delivering a copy
of the order , with a notice specifying the property attached ,
to the person holding it ; or , as to a debt or demand, to the
person owing it ; or , as to stock in a corporation , or property
held , or a debt or demand owing by it, to the officer or
agent upon whom a summons may be served , and by sum-

in the action . The sheriff shall deliver copies to, and sum-
mon, such persons as garnishees as the plaintiff may direct .
But no notice need be given in any case describing or spec-
ifying the debt or demand attached , but only a notice that
the persons or corporation to whom the order of attachment

manner and at the time provided for an answer by the
Rules of Civil Procedure .
Sec . 12. Section 224 of the Code of Practice in Civil

ance may be in person; or by the affidavit of the garnishee ,
served and filed in the manner and at the time required for

ments of the affidavit on which the attachment is issued ;
and , thereupon, the attachment shall be considered as con-
troverted , and the affidavits of the plaintiff and defendant
shall be regarded as the pleadings concerning the attach-

tained at the commencement of the action, the defendant
may file his affidavit at such time after the levy of the at

220 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tachment as is given by the Rules of Civil Procedure for
filing his defense after the service of the summons -which

Sec . 14. Subsection (4) of section 407 of the Code of

The revivor may be made upon a rule in the action , ten

Sec . 15. Section 414 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
That when the Commonwealth shall have received
money under a judgment rendered upon constructive serv-
ice , and the defendant did not appear , and such judgment
is thereafter modified or set aside under the provisions of
the Rules of Civil Procedure , upon a retrial , the court in
making an order for the restoration of such money, shall
direct the proper financial officer of the Commonwealth to
draw his warrant upon the Treasurer for the proper amount
in favor of the party in whose favor the order of restoration
may be made , and it shall thereupon become the duty of
such officer to draw his warrant in obedience to such
order .
Sec . 16. Section 417 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The title of purchasers in good faith to any property
sold under an attachment or judgment shall not be affected
by a new trial allowed a defendant constructively sum-
moned , except the title of property obtained by the plaintiff
and not bought of him in good faith by others .
Sec . 17. Section 419 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
No personal judgment shall be rendered against a de-
fendant constructively summoned , and who has not ap-
peared in the action .
Sec . 18. Section 445 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
Notice of such motion shall be served on the party

CHAPTER 84 221
against whom judgment is sought , at least ten days before
the motion is made . The motion and notice thereof shall be
served by an officer authorized to serve a summons , and the
service and return shall be made in the manner prescribed
for service of summons by the Rules of Civil Procedure .
Sec . 19. Section 489 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
1. The real property or any right , title or interest in
real property of a person under the age of twenty - one years ,
or of a person of unsound mind , whether such person be a
resident or a non -resident of this State , may be sold under
or by decree of a Court of Equity of the County in which
the property or any part thereof is situated for any purpose
deemed by the chancellor to be necessary or proper or bene-
ficial to such person under disability .
2. The decree may be rendered on a petition , counter-
claim or cross -petition filed by the statutory guardian of an
infant , or by the committee of a person of unsound mind , or
by a next friend , or by any party against a guardian or
committee , if he have one residing in this State , in any kind
of similar proceeding that may be maintained against a per-
son who is not under disability .
Except in actions brought under sub -section eight ( 8)
hereof , if the guardian or committee of a person under dis-
ability be a party to the action, then it shall not be necessary
for the person under disability to be made a party per-
sonally .
If the guardian or committee of a person under dis-
ability be not a party to the action, then it shall be necessary
for the person under disability to be a party personally and
brought before the Court in the manner authorized by the
Rules of Civil Procedure .
Nothing contained in this sub -section shall limit the
purposes of sale named in sub -section 1 hereof .
If the suit be filed against a guardian or committee and
he shall fail to appear and defend or show that no good de-
fense can be made , the Court shall appoint a guardian ad
litem to do so.
3. The pleading seeking a sale shall file the title

shall particularly describe the property and the interest
therein of the person under disability ; set forth fully the
reasons or purposes for which it is sought to have the same
sold ; and the nature and extent of all liens upon the interest
of the person, if any, so far as the same may be known to
the petitioner .
4. The case may be referred by order of Court to the
Master Commissioner for hearing of evidence and report
on the facts and his recommendation with respect to the
relief sought in the pleadings .
5. The allegations of the pleading shall not be taken
as confessed but shall be proved by competent evidence
heard on deposition or orally by the chancellor or commis-
sioner . A transcript thereof shall be filed as part of the
record.
6. The proceeds of any sale may be paid to the guard-
ian or committee provided that he shall have executed in
the Circuit Court a bond with surety to the Commonwealth
for the use and benefit of the person under.disability that
he will faithfully discharge his trust and will account for,
pay and deliver to said person all money and other property
due or belonging to him when he shall reach his majority ,
or if he be a person of unsound mind when he shall be le-
gally restored , or to his heirs or personal representative , or
in compliance with an order of any court of competent juris-
diction . The court shall endorse

CHAPTER 84 223
the sale privately and convey title ; subject, however , to the
approval of the chancellor before consummation .
8. Without previous judicial proceeding or judgment
the guardian of an infant or the committee of a person of
unsound mind may sell privately or at public auction and
convey any interest in real estate owned by a ward for such
reasons and purposes and upon such terms and conditions
as he shall deem best for the interest of said ward . Pro-
vided , however , that no such sale shall be consummated
until same shall have been approved by the judge of the
Circuit Court in the County where the land or a portion
thereof is situated in a suit in equity filed by the guardian
or committee against said infant or person of unsound mind .
The petition shall set forth the reasons and purposes of the
sale and the terms thereof .
If the person under disability be brought before the
Court by service of summons , a guardian ad litem shall be
appointed for such person. Proof shall be taken only on
notice to said guardian ad litem or, if the person under
disability be constructively summoned , on notice to his
warning order attorney . Proof may be taken by deposi-
tions or orally before the chancellor and thereafter tran-
scribed and filed in the record of the case . The guardian
ad litem or warning order attorney shall make a defense or
file a report stating that after careful examination of the case
he is unable to make a defense .
If the chancellor shall deem the sale advisable and the
terms and price satisfactory , he shall enter an order approv-
ing the same ; provided , however , that the judgment shall
not be entered until the guardian or committee shall have
executed a bond with approved surety in an amount not less
than the sum due, or to become due , his ward from said sale
providing that he will faithfully discharge all his duties as
such guardian or committee and will comply with the judg-
ment and orders of the court , and account for , pay and de-
liver the proceeds of the sale or property in which it shall
be invested to his ward or other person entitled thereto . The
bond shall be approved by the court and recorded in the
order book . Provided if the share of the infant in the pro

ceeds does not exceed $ 100.00, exclusive of interest, no bond
shall be required of the guardian . This proceeding may be
tried and judgment rendered in accordance with the Rules
of Civil Procedure .
9. The court shall at all times enter such orders as
will conserve and protect the rights of the person under
disability .
10. No defect or error in the proceeding shall render
the judgment or sale void unless the same is of the nature
and character that would render the proceeding void if all
parties were sui juris .
Sec . 20. Section 518 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The court in which a judgment has been rendered shall
have power to vacate or modify it when any paper pur-
porting to be the last will of any person has been , or may be
hereafter admitted to probate , and a later will has been dis-
covered . A judgment on this ground , however , shall not
be vacated or modified , unless proceedings to that end shall
be instituted within twenty -five years after the death of the
testator . If the earlier will shall have been established by
judgment of the circuit court on appeal from a judgment of
the county court , the proceedings to vacate shall be insti-
tuted in the circuit court . The proceedings to vacate shall
be by petition to which all persons interested in the earlier
will shall be made defendants . If no appeal shall have been
taken from the judgment of the county court probating the
earlier will , the proceedings to vacate shall be instituted in
that court . Provided , however , that a judgment on the
ground herein named shall not be vacated or modified until
the validity of such later will has been determined in such
proceedings . When such former judgment has been vacated
or modified such later will shall be admitted to probate in
the manner and under the laws governing the probation of
wills before the expiration of five years . The judgment
establishing such later will and vacating the judgment es-
tablishing the former will shall be without prejudice to the
vested rights of innocent third parties. The court in which
relief is sought for the reasons herein set out, may enter

CHAPTER 84 225
such orders as may be necessary to protect the rights of the
parties during the pendency of the proceedings . In all such
proceedings either party shall have the right of appeal to the
circuit court and the Court of Appeals as in other will cases .
Sec . 21. Section 584 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
For issuing a subpoena or warrant of arrest , and for
summoning or arresting a witness , the same fees shall be
paid , to officers taking depositions or interrogatories as are
allowed to clerks and sheriffs for similar services .
Sec . 22. Section 639 a-5 of the Code of Practice in
Civil Cases shall be amended to read as follows :
Any party aggrieved by a declaratory judgment , order
or decree , rendered in the circuit court , may take and per-
fect an appeal to the Court of Appeals in the manner pro-
vided in the Rules of Civil Procedure for appeals . Such
appeal shall be at once docketed in the Court of Appeals ,
and may be advanced for immediate hearing and submis-
sion . The Court of Appeals shall prepare proper rules as
to arguments and briefs applicable to cases brought before
it under this Act , and advanced as above prescribed .
Sec . 23. Section 671 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases shall be amended to read as follows :
The clerk of any court may administer any oath or take
any affidavit requested or permitted in the progress of an
action ; and shall make orders of survey , during vacation ,
upon the application of any party , after service of process ,
and shall endorse the same on the petition , noting the date
thereof and the party for whom it was made ; and said or-
ders of survey shall have the same effect as if made by the
court , if the party obtaining the order give to his adversary
notice that the order has been made and of the time when it
is to be executed .
Section 24. Section 672 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The clerk of any court shall prepare in a proper man-
ner every bond to be taken by or given before him or his
court .
Section 25. Section 673 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
1

226 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
The clerk of any court shall refuse any surety offered
in a bond to be taken by him who is, in his opinion , insuf-
ficient .
Section 26. Section 678 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
Any duty enjoined by law or by the Rules of Civil
Procedure upon a ministerial officer , and any act permitted
to be done by him , may be performed by his lawful deputy .
Sec . 27. Section 692 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The plaintiff in an action for enforcing a lien on prop-
erty shall state in his petition the liens held thereon by
others , making them defendants ; and may ask for and ob-
tain a judgment for a sale of the property to satisfy all of
said liens which are shown to exist , though the defendants
fail to assert their claims . Such defendants shall not , how-
ever, be allowed to withdraw or receive any of the pro-
ceeds of such sale , until they have shown their right thereto
by answer and cross claim , which shall be asserted as pro-
vided in the Rules of Civil Procedure .
Sec . 28. Section 693 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
In an action for the recovery of money in which the
defendant may have given bond with sureties , or for whom
sureties may have given bond, pursuant to the provisions of
sections 214 , 221 , 258 ; or in which an appellant may have
given bond with sureties , or for whom sureties may have
given bond , for the purpose of appeal from an inferior court
to a circuit court or for the purpose of a supersedeas pend-
ing an appeal to the Court of Appeals-if such defendant or
appellant have thereafter become a bankrupt under the
laws of the United States , upon notice thereof from his ad-
versary being served on the sureties , by summons , the court
shall decide whether or not anything , and, if anything , how
much , should have been adjudged against the defendant or
appellant, but for the bankruptcy ; and such decision shall
be conclusive as to the liability of the sureties ; but they
shall not be liable upon a bond given pursuant to section
214 , if property released from an attachment by reason

CHAPTER 84 227
thereof , or its proceeds , have lawfully come to the defend-
ant's or appellant's assignee in bankruptcy .
Sec. 29. Section 701 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
A summons , order for a provisional remedy and other
process , from quarterly courts or county courts, shall be
directed to the sheriff , coroner or a constable , at the option
of the plaintiff; those from police , city and mayors ' courts
shall be directed to the marshal or a constable ; and those
from courts of justices of the peace shall be directed to a
constable ; or to a special agent appointed by the justice , by
an indorsement on the process , upon an affidavit of the
plaintiff or his agent being filed before the justice , to the
effect that the process can not be executed , according to
the belief of the affiant , unless such special agent be ap-
pointed ; but such process may be served by any officer or
person who is authorized by law to serve a summons .
Sec . 30. Section 710 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
In an action commenced in a Justice Court, if the de-
fendant reside in the county in which the summons is is-
sued , it shall be returned for trial in the district of his resi-
dence , unless he otherwise consent in writing , signed by him
and indorsed on the summons ; or, unless the justices in
such district be interested or refuse to act, in which case
it shall be returned in an adjoining district . If it be issued
by the judge of the quarterly court , it shall be returned
before him .
Sec . 31. Section 715 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
In quarterly courts , county courts , police courts , and
courts of justices of the peace , only one docket is required
to be kept , on which shall be entered all the actions and
motions as they shall be returned . Each presiding judge
and justice of the peace shall , in a book provided for that
purpose , keep a full and fair record of judicial proceedings
in his court ; and keep a good cross -index of the names of
the litigants , referring to the pages of the book of every
entry in each case .
1

228 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 32. Section 726 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
Appeals from quarterly courts , county courts , police
courts and courts of justices of the peace shall be docketed
and stand for trial as other civil actions , and shall be tried
anew , as if no judgment had been rendered . The appeal
shall be taken as required by the Rules of Civil Procedure
or as required by any applicable statute .
Sec . 33. Section 734 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
The mode of bringing the judgment of an inferior court
to the Court of Appeals , for reversal or modification, shall
be by an appeal . The appeal shall be taken and perfected
as provided in the Rules of Civil Procedure .
Sec . 34. Section 745 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
An appeal to the Court of Appeals may be taken by an
infant not under coverture , or a person of unsound mind , or
a prisoner who did not appear by his attorney , or by their
representatives , within one year next after their deaths or
the removal of their disabilities , whichever first may
happen .
Sec . 35. Section 764 of the Code of Practice in Civil
Cases is amended to read as follows :
Upon the affirmance of an appeal , or the dismissal of
an appeal after it has been docketed in the Court of Ap-
peals , where the appeal is from a judgment for the pay-
ment of money, the collection of which , in whole or in
part , has been superseded , as provided in the Rules of Civil
Procedure , 10 % damages on the amount superseded shall
be awarded against the appellant .
Sec . 36. Section 94 of the Code of Practice in Criminal
Cases is amended to read as follows :
(1) Upon the forfeiture of a bail bond the court shall
order a summons to be issued against the bail , requiring
them to show cause why judgment should not be rendered
against them , on the forfeiture , for the sum fixed in the
bail bond .
(2) If the court fail to make such order , the attorney
for the Commonwealth may, at any time after the close of

CHAPTER 84 229
the term , cause such summons to be issued by the clerk of
the court .
(3) No pleadings shall be required on the part of the
Commonwealth , but the summons shall be served , answer
made , and all subsequent proceedings taken, at the same
time and manner as in other civil actions .
Sec . 37. Section 95 of the Code of Practice in Criminal
Cases is amended to read as follows :
When the sheriff or other officer shall return the sum-
mons , issued under section 94 , not executed , in whole or in
part , and that the defendants , or any of them , are non-
residents of this Commonwealth , or so concealed themselves
or himself that the summons can not be executed upon them
or him , the attorney for the Commonwealth may sue out
an attachment against the estate of the defendants as to
whom such return shall have been made , and proceed as
provided by law , except no affidavit nor bond shall be re-
quired ; or he may in any case , after forfeiture , sue out an
attachment for any of the causes enumerated by law mak-
ing an affidavit and executing bond as required by law .
Sec . 38. Section 153 of the Code of Practice in Crim-
inal Cases is amended to read as follows :
If it appear by affidavit that a material witness is about
to leave this State without the procurement or consent of
the defendant, or is physically unable to attend for exami-
nation in court ; or that his death is apprehended ; "or that
he is a non-resident of this State , and beyond the reach of
the process of the court ," the court or the judge , "or any
circuit judge " in vacation , may authorize the defendant to
take his deposition. The court or judge shall , by written
order , prescribe the manner of taking the deposition ,
whether by written interrogatories , or upon notice to the
attorney for the Commonwealth ; and such deposition shall
only be used if the witness be dead or is absent from the
State , or physically unable to attend for examination in
court at the time of trial. The deposition may be taken by
any officer who is authorized to take depositions by law :
Provided , That the deposition of a non-resident shall only
be taken upon interrogatories to be filed with the clerk of
the court in which the trial is pending , in the same time and

230 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
manner that is prescribed in the Rules of Civil Procedure
for taking depositions in civil actions .
Section 39. Section 280 of the Code of Practice in
Criminal Cases is amended to read as follows :
Upon the trial of Criminal Prosecutions , either party
may except to any decision of the court by which the sub-
stantial rights of such party are prejudiced .
Sec . 40. Section 282 of the Code of Practice in Crim-
inal Cases is amended to read as follows :
(1) (a) An exception is an objection taken to a de-
cision of a court upon a matter of law .
(b ) A party may, without a previous objection , ex-
cept to a decision against him , unless it be made at the
instance of the adverse party .
(c) But a party can not except to a decision made at
the instance of the adverse party , unless objection shall
have been made to the motion , offer or request of the ad-
verse party .
(2 ) The party objecting must except when the deci-
sion is made ; and time may be given to prepare a bill of
exceptions , but not beyond a day in the succeeding term ,
to be fixed by the court .
If the judge of said court shall die or resign, or his term
of office shall expire before the term of court to which time
is given to file bill of exceptions or if the regular judge of
the court , or any special judge who shall have tried the case ,
does not preside at the said next term of court , then it shall
be the duty of the regular judge of said court , if present
and presiding, or any special judge who may be presiding
at said term of court , to sign said bill of exceptions , and at-
test the transcript of testimony taken by the official steno-
graphic reporter , and make all necessary orders with ref-
erence thereto .
If no court is held at the term to which time is given to
file bill of exceptions , then the party offering the bill of ex-
ceptions shall have until the next term of court to prepare
the bill of exceptions ; Provided : That time may be given
to a date in vacation within which the bill of exceptions
may be tendered , and when same is approved and signed
by the then judge of the court in which the case was tried ,

CHAPTER 84 231
and filed by the clerk of said court , said bill of exceptions
and the transcript of evidence shall have the same effect
as if filed in term time .
(3) ( a) No particular form of exception or bill of ex-
ceptions , is required . A party must not, except as herein-
after provided , state the evidence , but must state the ma-
terial facts which the evidence conduced to prove ; and , if
the exception be to a decision admitting or excluding tes-
timony , or concerning its meaning or effect, so much of
the evidence as is necessary to explain the exception , and
no more, shall be stated .
(b) If the judge refuse to sign the bill of exceptions , as
presented ; or if the party wish to appeal upon the ground
that the verdict is not sustained by the evidence , it shall be
stated in full , and the bill of exceptions be presented to the
judge .
(c) But if a number of instruments of substantially
the same character be used upon a trial it shall be sufficient
to furnish a copy of one of them in the bill of exceptions ,
accompanied by a statement showing its resemblance to the
others , and how many such others were used on the trial ;
and such copy and statement shall have the same effect as
if all of said instruments had been copied in the bill of
exceptions .
(4) If the decision objected to be entered on the record,
and the grounds of objection appear in the entry , the ex-
ception may be taken by the party causing to be noted, at
the end of the decision , that he excepts .
(5) ( a) If the decision be not entered on the record ,
or the grounds of objection do not appear in the entry , the
party must prepare his bill of exceptions and present it to
the judge for his signature .
(b ) Exceptions taken during the trial need not be
noted of record , nor reduced to writing, unless by order of
court until after the trial . During the term at which the
judgment becomes final the party excepting shall unless
further time be given to him , prepare his bill of exceptions ,
which shall include all the decisions of the court excepted
to, in consecutive order ; and, if he except to a decision of

-232 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
the court in granting or refusing any instruction , all the
instructions given and refused shall be also included .
(c ) If the bill of exceptions be approved by the judge ,
he shall sign it, and it shall be filed as part of the record ,
but not spread at large on the order book . If not approved ,
he shall correct it, or suggest the correction to be made ,
and sign it. A party objecting to the judge's correction of
an exception which purports to state the evidence may,
within five days after the bill is signed , file the exception
as written by him , if its truth be attested by the affidavits
of two bystanders ; but its truth may be controverted and
maintained by other affidavits filed in the clerk's office , not
exceeding five on either side .
(d) Affidavits controverting an exception not signed
by the judge must be filed in the clerk's office , and notice
of the filing given, within fifteen days after the filing of
such exceptions ; and affidavits sustaining such exception
must be filed within fifteen days after such notice.
(e ) If the judge who presided at the trial does not pre-
side when a motion for a new trial is overruled , the bill of
exceptions may be certified by bystanders , and be con-
troverted and maintained , pursuant to the provisions of
subsections ( c ) and ( d) of this section .
(6 ) No exceptions shall be regarded, unless the de-
cision to which it relates be prejudicial to the substantial
rights of the party excepting .
(7) In cases in which , by subsection ( 1) (b ) , the evi-
dence is required to be stated in full , the judge shall certify
in the bill of exceptions that it contains all the evidence . In
all other cases he shall certify that the bill of exceptions is
true .
Sec . 41. Section 315 of the Code of Practice in Crim-
inal Cases is amended to read as follows :
The clerk shall issue subpoenas for witnesses at the
request of the officers of the city or of the defendant, and
the court may compel their attendance in the manner and
by the process provided by law .
Sec . 42. Section 329 of the Code of Practice in Crim-
inal Cases is amended to read as follows :
The justice shall issue subpoenas for witnesses and for

CHAPTER 84 233
:
the production of documentary evidence , and enforce their
attendance , or its production in the manner prescribed by
law .
Sec . 43. Section 370 of the Code of Practice in Crim-
inal Cases is amended to read as follows :
Upon a judgment being rendered against the defendant
on an appeal , and an execution issued thereon, in one year
from its rendition , being returned unsatisfied , a summons
shall be issued by the clerk , at the request of the Common-
wealth's attorney , against the surety on the appeal and sus-
pending covenants , requiring him to answer in the time
and manner required by the Rules of Civil Procedure for
civil actions , to show cause why judgment should not be
rendered against him on said covenants , which summons
shall be directed , delivered , served and returned as a sum-
mons in a civil action and be tried as a civil action. If the
surety have any defense thereto, he shall make it by an-
swer , and the issues of law or of fact thereon shall be tried
and decided .
Sec . 44. Section 370 a- 1 of the Code of Practice in
Criminal Cases is amended to read as follows :
Every supersedeas bond or appeal and supersedeas
bond executed under the provisions of the Rules of Civil
Procedure and under section 349 of Criminal Code of Prac-
tice in contemplation of an appeal to the Court of Appeals
for the purpose of superseding a judgment shall have the
force and effect of a judgment , upon which the clerk of the
court of appeals shall issue execution only for the cost
adjudged in the court of appeals in favor of the appellee
and against the appellant .
Sec . 45. KRS 23.150 is amended to read as follows :
The Judge of each Circuit Court, other than those of
continuous session , may make or direct in vacation or term
time at his chambers , at the circuit clerk's office or at any
other place in any county in the district , any order , rule , or
judgment in any proceeding on the criminal docket, upon
reasonable notice to the parties , except where trial by jury
is called for or ordered by the court . In civil actions the
judge may act out of term time as authorized by the Rules
ofCivil Procedure .

234 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Sec . 46. KRS 24.210 is amended to read as follows :
(1) The court shall , in general term , make rules of
court governing the assignment of civil actions to the civil
branches, and the divisions thereof , for the purpose of mak-
ing up issues and for the purpose of trial . The rules shall ,
in so far as practicable , provide for the assignment of all
actions wherein the parties are not entitled to a jury as a
matter of right to the chancery branch of the court .
(2) All criminal prosecutions and all proceedings on
bail bonds and recognizances in criminal cases shall be
brought and prosecuted in the criminal branch .
Sec . 47. KRS 28.050 is amended to read as follows :
The clerk of quarterly courts , police courts and courts
of justices of the peace , and circuit clerks in all criminal
proceedings shall from his minutes draw up the proceedings
of each day in a plain , legible manner , which , after being
corrected as ordered by the court , and read in an audible
voice, shall be signed by the presiding judge .
Sec . 48. Subsection ( 2) of KRS 28.430 is amended to
read as follows :
(2) In all criminal proceedings , upon the motion of
either party the court shall order the reporter to make a
full and accurate transcript and duplicate copy of his notes .
The original shall be filed among the papers to be used in
making up the bill of exceptions to the Court of Appeals .
The duplicate copy shall be filed with the papers , and re-
main in the office of the clerk of the court as a public
record .
In all civil actions the record of proceedings and the
transcript of evidence shall be made up in accordance with
the Rules of Civil Procedure .
Sec. 49. KRS 371.030 is amended to read as follows :
The consideration of any writing , with or without seal ,
may be impeached or denied by pleading .
Sec . 50. Subsection (1) of KRS 421.010 is amended
to read as follows :
(1) Except as provided in KRS 421.020 and subsection
(2) of this section , a witness , subpoenaed or recognized to
attend , shall be allowed one dollar for each day's
attendance on court or arbitrators , or to give a deposition ,

CHAPTER 84 235
or to give evidence on any legal occasion . Officers attending
out of their county , under a subpoena duces tecum , shall
be allowed two dollars per day . A witness who resides more

CHAPTER 87 243
CHAPTER 86
(S. B. 222)
AN ACT relating to the investment of state funds and moneys ; amend-
ing KRS 41.380 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 41.380 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) There shall be a State Investment Commission
composed of the Governor , the State Treasurer and the
Commissioner of Finance . The commission shall have
authority to and may invest any and all surplus funds of
the state in direct obligations of the United States Govern-
ment or of the Commonwealth of Kentucky . All such in-
vestments shall be in obligations that will mature within
five years from the date of the investment .
(2) The commission shall also have authority to and
may , whenever in its opinion the cash in the State Treasury
is in excess of the amount required to meet current expendi-
tures , invest any and all of said excess cash in United States
Treasury Bills or Certificates of Indebtedness which will
mature within one year from the date of the investment .
(3 ) The authority granted by this section to the State
Investment Commission shall not extend to any funds that
are specifically provided by law to be invested by some other
officer or agency of the state government .
Approved March 24 , 1952

CHAPTER 88
(S. B. 224 )
Approved March 24, 1952
AN ACT relating to the furnishing of school services to children in
certain state institutions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act, unless the context
otherwise requires :
(1) " Out-of-district children ," means those children
of school age resident in a state -operated institution who
were placed therein from school districts other than the one
in which the institution is located .
(2) " Current costs and expenses ," means all expendi-
tures , other than for capital outlay and debt service , which ,
in the final result , are an actual charge on the tax revenue
of the school district for the furnishing of school services to
out-of-district children . Among other things, "current costs
and expenses " does not include those expenditures for which
the school district receives reimbursement from any source ,
other than from the state under the provisions of this Act .
Section 2. Any county or independent school district
which provides elementary or secondary school services to
out-of-district children shall be reimbursed by the Depart-
ment of Education , out of funds not required by law to be
expended for other purposes or in any other manner , in an
amount equal to all current costs and expenses of the district

CHAPTER 89 245
for furnishing such services in excess of any per capita dis-
tribution received from the Common School Fund on behalf
of the children . The school services furnished to out-of-
district children shall be substantially equal to those fur-
nished to other school children of the district .
Section 3. The school district shall make to the
Superintendent of Public Instruction such reports as he
requires concerning school services for out-of-district chil-
dren , and shall file with the state agency operating the in-
stitution in which the children are resident a copy of the
annual report made to the Superintendent of Public In-
struction .
Section 4. The school district , at the end of each
school year , shall make application to the Superintendent of
Public Instruction for the reimbursement due on behalf of
school services furnished during the year just ended . After
approving the application , the Superintendent of Public
Instruction shall notify the Commissioner of Finance who
shall cause a warrant , in the appropriate amount , to be
drawn in favor of the school district .
Section 5. There is hereby appropriated out of the
General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury , to the
Department of Education , the sum of $35,000 for each of
the fiscal years of 1952-53 and 1953-54 , to be used to carry
out the provisions of this Act .

CHAPTER 90
(S. B. 226)
Approved March 24, 1952
AN ACT providing the manner and time of electing members of county
boards of education in counties containing a city of the first class ,
if and when an independent school district in a city of the first
class is merged with the county school district , and providing for
the filling of vacancies in office .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That upon a merger under the provisions
of Section 160.040 and Section 160.041 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes of an independent school district in a city
of the first class with a county school district in counties
containing a city of the first class , the members of the
county board of education of the merged county school
district , shall be elected from the county school district at
large .
Section 2. Each member of the respective boards of
education at the time of the merger of the districts , may
continue to hold office until the expiration of his or her term
of office , but any vacancy occurring among such members
for any reason shall not be filled .
Section 3. At the regular November election during
the first even number year nearest to the time of the merger
of the districts , as above set out , two ( 2 ) members of the
county board of education shall be elected from the county
at large for a term of four (4 ) years , and two (2 ) years after
such first election , three (3) members of the county board

CHAPTER 91 247
of education shall be elected for a term of four (4) years ,
and in each even numbered year thereafter an election shall
be held from the county at large to fill the membership of
the county board of education for the term that will expire
on the first Monday in January following , and the regularly
elected members shall hold office for four (4) years , and
until their successors are elected and qualified .
Section 4. Any vacancy occurring in the membership
of the county board after members have been elected from
the county at large , shall be filled as now provided by
Section 160.190 Kentucky Revised Statutes .

CHAPTER 92
(S. B. 233 )
AN ACT relating to the letting of work by the Commissioners of Water
Districts , amending Kentucky Revised Statutes 74.260 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Kentucky Revised Statutes 74.260 be amended
to read as follows :
Upon the final reference to it by the court of any pro-
ceedings under this chapter, the commission shall give
notice in some newspaper published in the county in which
the improvement is located and such publication elsewhere
as it deems expedient, of the time and place of letting the
work of construction of the improvements . In such notice
the commission shall specify the approximate amount of
work to be done and the time fixed for the completion of
the work. On the date appointed for the letting , the com-
mission shall convene and let the work to the lowest re-
sponsible bidder . The work may be let either as a whole or
in sections , and either for a lump sum bid or upon the basis
of cost plus a fixed fee or percentage of the cost of the im-
provement, as the commission deems most advantageous .
In the event that the work is let upon a lump sum basis ,
no bid shall be entertained that exceeds the estimated cost ,
unless it is shown that the original estimate was erroneous .
The commission may reject any or all bids and advertise the
work again , if in its judgment the interest of the district
will be served by so doing . The commission may require
each bidder to execute a bond or put up a certified check
payable to the Treasurer of the Water District , in an amount
fixed by the commission, to the effect that the bidder will
enter into a contract with the commission if his bid is ac-
cepted . The successful bidder shall be required to enter into
a contract with the commission and to execute a bond for
the faithful performance of the contract , with sufficient
surety , to the commission for the use and benefit of the
district in an amount equal to 50 % of the estimated cost of
the work awarded to such bidder .
Approved March 25, 1952

CHAPTER 94 249
CHAPTER 93
(S. B. 238)
AN ACT relating to Workmen's Compensation .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Whenever an employe receives a personal injury which
is or may be compensated under the Workmen's Compen-
sation Law and said employe is examined or treated for such
injury by a doctor or physician under the provisions of
KRS 342.020 the employer shall cause to be furnished to
the employee , if requested by him , and to the board , if re-
quired by it immediately following said examination or
treatment a copy of the report of the doctor or physician
showing the nature and condition and effect of the em-
ploye's injury . Upon any future examination or examina-
tions by a doctor or physician the employer shall cause to
be furnished to the employe, if requested by him , and to the
board , if required by it, immediately following such exam-
ination copies of the reports thereof containing the infor-
mation mentioned above .
If the employe should be examined or treated by any
doctor of his own choosing the employe shall , immediately
following said examination , cause a copy of the report
thereof to be furnished to his employer or to the board ,
if required by either .

1. Any circuit judge may, if he deems it necessary to aid
the court and the stenographic reporter , require that a device
for the recording of the testimony be used in any proceeding in a
circuit court ; provided that such a device may not be used for
recording the testimony in a proceeding unless the regular or a
special stenographic reporter is present to take full stenographic
notes of the proceeding .
2. The fiscal court of any county may purchase a record-
ing device for the use of the circuit court of that county if it
deems the purchase of such a device to be to the best interest of
the county , considering the county finances and other permis-
sible expenditures .
3. Recording device , as used in this act, means any device ,
materials and appurtenances , by which the voices of speakers
are recorded so that they may be reproduced later in audible
form .
Approved March 24, 1952

252 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 97
(S. B. 251 )
AN ACT relating to persons convicted of a crime while serving sentences
in the State Penal Institutions .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 432.390 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is hereby
amended to read as follows :
(1) Any person convicted of a crime who, while serving a
sentence of imprisonment in the penitentiary , escapes from the
penitentiary or wherever he may be confined , or flees from
whatever bounds he may be assigned, whether under guard or
as a trusty , shall be further confined to the penitentiary for not
less than three nor more than six years , to commence after he
has served out the sentence for which he was originally confined.
(2) Any convict confined in a locked cell , hospital or lock-up
in the penitentiary who escapes therefrom to another part of
the penitentiary , or aids another convict in doing so , shall be
confined in the penitentiary for not less than one nor more than
six years , to be added to the term of his former conviction .
(3) For any offense mentioned in subsection (1 ) or sub-
section (2) of this section, the circuit court of the county in which
the convict was confined , or of the county from which he was
sent out under assignment to work, shall have jurisdiction .

AN ACT to amend Section 64.640 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
relating to fees and compensation of public officers and employes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 64.640 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) Subject to the limitations prescribed in subsection (2)
of this section , and excepting officers elected by popular vote ,
commissioners of the Court of Appeals , employes of the General
Assembly , and members of boards and commissions , the Division

CHAPTER 98 253
of Personnel in the Department of Finance shall prepare a
schedule of compensation , payable out of the State Treasury ,
with a minimum and maximum salary rate , and such intermediate
salary rates as are deemed necessary or advisable , for the office or
position of employment of every state officer and employe ,
including specifically the offices and positions of employment
in every constitutional administrative department , statutory ad-
ministrative department , independent agency , or other unit of
the state government . Such schedules shall be based upon
studies of the duties and responsibilities of the offices and
positions and upon a comparison with rates being paid for
similar or comparable services elsewhere , and in the preparation
of such schedules the Division of Personnel shall ascertain and
record the duties , responsibilities and authority pertaining to the
various offices and positions in the state service , and classify
such positions in the manner provided in KRS 42.120 . No such
schedule shall become effective until it has been approved by
the Governor .
(2) Based upon an independent study and analysis hereto-
fore made of the duties , responsibilities and authority pertaining
to the various offices and positions in the state service , and taking
into consideration the character and variety of the services of
each agency involved , the maximum annual salary rates payable
out of the State Treasury , for the offices and positions in the
state service hereinafter named shall be as follows :
(a) Commissioner of Finance , Commissioner of Revenue ,
Commissioner of Highways , Commissioner of Health , Commis-
sioner of Welfare, Commissioner of Economic Security , Com-
missioner of Conservation , Reviser of Statutes, Manager of the
Kentucky State Fair , and Commissioner of Alcoholic Beverage
Control , $7,500 .
(b) Commissioner of State Police , Commissioner of Bank-
ing , Commissioner of Industrial Relations , Commissioner (Di-
rector of the Division) of Insurance , Commissioner (Director of
the Division ) of Motor Transportation , Director of the Legislative
Research Commission , Administrator of Distilled Spirit Unit and
Administrator of Malt Beverage Unit , $7,000 .
(c) Commissioner of Aeronautics , Director of the Division

Mines and Minerals , Director of the Agricultural and Industrial
Development Board , Adjutant General , and Private Secretary to
the Governor , $6,500 .
(d) Heads of statutory departments other than those named
in paragraphs (a), (b) and (c) of this subsection $5,000 .

of Chapter 271 of Kentucky Revised Statutes .

with the Secretary of State and the Clerk of the County

corporation is located in the same manner as articles of
amendment are required to be signed and acknowledged
and filed under Kentucky Revised Statutes , Section 271.450 .

to law and the articles of the corporation , and when all fees
have been paid as required by law , he shall endorse his
approval thereon and shall issue to the corporation , or its
representative , the approved statement and shall make and
retain a copy thereof . Upon the approval of such statement
by the Secretary of State, the resolution establishing and
designating the series and fixing and determining the rela-
tive rights and preferences thereof shall become effective .
2. WHEREAS , important expansion of Kentucky in-
dustries is being delayed by inadequate provisions in the
Kentucky Corporation Law for the issuance of shares in
series to finance such expansion .
NOW, THEREFORE , in order to prevent slowing down
and delay in such expansion, an emergency is declared to
exist and this act shall take effect upon its passage and ap-
proval by the Governor .

CHAPTER 101
(Н. В. 1)
AN ACT relating to the issuance of duplicate game , hunting and fishing
licenses and tags , where such original license or tag is lost or
destroyed ; providing fees for such duplicates .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. If any game, hunting or fishing license certificate
or tag provided for in Chapter 150, Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
is lost or destroyed , the person to whom such game, hunting
or fishing license or tag was issued may obtain a duplicate or
substitute by applying in person to the County Court Clerk of
the County in which such original license certificate or tag was
issued, furnishing proof satisfactory to the Clerk that the license
certificate or tag has been lost or destroyed , and payment of
twenty -five ( 25c ) cents , which shall be retained by the Clerk .
Approved March 4, 1952 .

AN ACT , changing the classification of the City of Elsmere , in Kenton
County.

Assembly that the population of the City of Elsmere , in Kenton
County , is such as to justify its being assigned to the fourth class

NOW THEREFORE
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The City of Elsmere , in Kenton County, is transferred from
the sixth to the fourth class of cities .

PART FIVE
GENERAL PROVISION
1. Nothing in this Act shall be construed to repeal any
appropriation for the fiscal year ending June 30, 1952 and nothing
in this Act is to be construed to amend or alter the provisions of
Chapter 42 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes pertaining to the
duties and powers of the Commissioner of Finance . All questions
that arise in interpreting any appropriation in this Act as to the
purposes for which the same may be expended shall be decided
by the Commissioner of Finance in accordance with the detailed
estimates embodied in the budget report as considered by the
General Assembly , and the decision of the Commissioner of Fi-
nance shall be final and conclusive .
2. Whereas , the finances of the Commonwealth can not be
ascertained at this time , and since the actual needs of each officer ,
department , board , commission , institution , or subdivision of
the State Government can only be anticipated and not finally
determined by the Legislature , and since the total amount of
revenue accruing to the General Expenditure Fund from which
these appropriations are hereby made can only be anticipated
through existing laws and those that may hereafter be enacted,
it is the purpose and intention of this Legislature that the State
expenditures for each fiscal year covered in this Act shall not
exceed the total revenues anticipated to accrue to the General
Expenditure Fund for the same fiscal year . As the conditions
can not be finally determined until after the adjournment of this
session of the Legislature , and as the needs of each officer, de-
partment , board , commission , institution or subdivision of the
State Government and the total revenues accruing to the General
Fund can not be fully determined at this time , it is the intention
and the purpose of this Legislature that the Commissioner of

CHAPTER 106 297
Finance , with the advice of the Commissioner of Revenue and
the approval of the Governor of the Commonwealth , be and he
is hereby authorized , empowered and directed to ascertain and
determine the needs of each officer , department , board , commis-
sion, institution , or subdivision of the State Government , and to
ascertain the total revenue anticipated to accrue to the General
Expenditure Fund of the Commonwealth , and upon the ascer-
tainment and determination of said facts, he, the Commissioner of
Finance , in the manner set forth and as provided by general law ,
is hereby authorized , empowered and directed by the Legislature
to prevent an overdraft or deficit in any fiscal year for which ap-
propriations are herein made , by equitably reducing , according
to need, without discrimination , the appropriations herein made
to any officer , department , board , commission , institution , or sub-
division of the State Government ; provided that the power hereby
vested shall not permit any reduction of the appropriation of any
officer, department , board , commission , institution or subdivision
of the State Government that will actually impair the necessary
constitutional functions of any agency whose operations and func-
tions are determined to be necessary constitutional functions of
government .
3. Since the total amount of revenue accruing to the Gen-
eral Expenditure Fund can only be anticipated and not finally
determined at this time and since it cannot be finally ascertained
at this time whether total revenues accruing to the General
Expenditure Fund will equal appropriations therefrom , it is the
intention and purpose of this General Assembly that any available
Surplus in the General Expenditure Fund may be used to fulfill
appropriations made in Part One of this Appropriation Act , but
only in the event such appropriations exceed the total of revenues
accruing to the General Expenditure Fund .
4. In the event receipts to the General Expenditure Fund
from current revenues for the fiscal year 1952-53 exceed the sum
of Seventy Eight Million Dollars ($78,000,000 ) such excess , but
not to exceed Three Million Dollars ($3,000,000 ) is hereby ap-
propriated to the Governor's Special Emergency Fund to be used
by him for the fiscal year of 1953-54 for supplementing existing
appropriations for ordinary recurring and extraordinary govern-
mental expenses deemed emergencies by the Governor and to be

298 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
expended by the Governor in his discretion for any emergency
that he may determine requires the expenditure of
all
orany
part
ofsaid fund
.
5.
Inenacting this Budget Appropriation Law
,

isthe
deliberate intention
ofthe General Assembly
toenact each sec-

, with particular

itenacted
bythe General Assembly

:
Section
1. Section 118.040
ofthe Kentucky Revised
Statutes
isamended to read as follows
:
The polls shall
beopened
at
6
a.
m. and kept open con-
tinuously until

p.

ofthe same day
. Before receiving any

Section
2. Section 119.040

Statutes
isamended to read as follows
:
The primary election for the nomination
ofcandidates by

to
bevoted for

CHAPTER 107 299
tween the hours of 6 a. m. and 5 p . m., at the regular polling
places in each election precinct .
Section 3. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 118.350 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) When the hour of 5 p. m . arrives the election officers
shall announce the fact at the door of the voting room and
close the polls . They shall take charge of the ballot box, and
in the presence of each other shall place the lock on the small
vent in the lid and securely lock it. The election officers shall
then place upon all unused stubs and ballots , with rubber stamp
or by writing, the word, "Unused ," and place them , with the
stub book and other utensils or election material , into the linen
envelope provided by the county clerk for the purpose , and
shall seal with sealing wax across the seal of the envelope and
place the county election seal upon the wax , so as to make the
letters plainly visible in three different places on the envelope .
The sheriff of election and the judge of opposite faith shall
forthwith convey the ballot box, the key to the vent lock, and
the envelope to the county clerk, taking his receipt therefor .
The sheriff and judge shall see that no person other than them-
selves has access to, or custody of, the ballot box, and they shall
each remain in the presence of the other until the box, key and
envelope are delivered to the county clerk .
Section 4. Subsection ( 3 ) of Section 118.370 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 3 ) The county board of election commissioners shall meet
for the purpose of counting the ballots , issuing certificates and
making a report , at 6 p . m . on the day of the election , in the
office of the county clerk or in some other room designated by
the board. The board shall proceed as speedily as practicable
to count the ballots of every precinct in the county . At the
finish of each precinct the board shall announce the results
at the door of the room where the count is being conducted ,
and shall fill out the blank in the back of the stub book
provided for that purpose , and each commissioner shall sign
the blank in the proper place . The board shall remain in session
for the purpose of ascertaining the results of the election until
midnight on the election day, and if the count is not then com-
pleted it may then adjourn until the following day at 8 a. m. ,

300 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
unless the following day is a Sunday , in which case it shall
adjourn until 8 a. m. , on the day following the Sunday . If the
day following the election is a legal holiday , the board may
adjourn until 8 a. m. on the day following the legal holiday . On
the adjourned day the board shall remain in session , if necessary
to complete the count , until 6 p. m. , or longer in
itsdiscretion
.
Ifthe count
isnot then completed
, the board shall reconvene
during the same hours
oneach succeeding day
, except Sundays
and
onsuch legal holidays
asthe board decides not
tomeet
,
until the count
iscompleted
.
Approved March 25
, 1952
.
