CHAPTER 114
(Committee Substitute for H. B. 39
)
AN ACT relating
topublic library service and making an appropriation
therefor
. This bill shall be known as the Hays
-McClain bill
.
WHEREAS less than one
-half of the total population
ofKentucky has
access
tolocal public library facilities
; and

CHAPTER 114 307
WHEREAS only twenty percent of the residents of rural areas have
public library service ; and
WHEREAS aid from the state is essential for the provision of adequate
local public library service throughout the state;
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
1. For promoting , aiding and equalizing public library
service in Kentucky , particularly in rural areas , there is
hereby appropriated out of the General Expenditure Fund
of the State Treasury , to a special fund to be known as the
Public Library Service Fund, the sum of $50,000 for the
fiscal year 1952-53 , and $60,000 for the fiscal year 1953-54 .
2. The Director of the Library Extension Division of
the Department of Library and Archives , the Commissioner
of Finance and the Attorney General shall constitute a
Public Library Service Commission for the purpose of
administering the provisions of this Act . The commission
shall elect a chairman and a secretary from among its
members . It shall promulgate rules and regulations for the
administration of this Act and for the allocation of funds
appropriated for the purposes of this Act . In allocating funds
to counties , the commission shall give primary consideration
to the need for additional library service in each county .
3. Any funds appropriated for the purposes of this
Act shall be expended for aiding in the establishment , ex-
tension and development of local public library facilities
(exclusive of library buildings , equipment and furniture )
through the purchase of books or bookmobiles, through
technical assistance , educational and promotional programs ,
and such other methods and services as will best accomplish
the purpose. Funds in the Public Library Service Fund shall
be paid out by the State Treasurer on warrants drawn by
the Commissioner of Finance on order of the Chairman of
the Public Library Service Commission .
4. To the extent of funds available , grants shall be
made to counties applying for state aid , in the order in which
they meet the requirements established by the Public Li-
brary Service Commission . Grants -in-aid thus provided

308 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall not exceed $5,000 to any county . Each county receiving
a state grant shall expend from local funds, for county -wide
public library service during the years for which aid is
received a sum equal to at least 5% of the amount received
from state funds ; the ratio of matching to be based on local
financial ability as determined by the assessed valuation
of the county .
5. The fund appropriated under this Act shall be
separate and apart from the appropriation to the Library
Extension Division , which appropriation shall not be af-
fected by this Act or appropriation hereunder .
6. It is the intention of this Act to add to the public
library service given by local communities and not to relieve
such communities of their responsibilities .

AN ACT relating to zoning and planning in cities of the second class
and in counties containing such a city .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The lawful use of land for trade , industry or
residence , in cities of the second class and in counties contain-
ing such a city , existing at the time of the adoption of any zoning
regulation or restriction , or at the time of the adjustment or re-
vision thereof , or amendment thereto , although such use does
not conform to the provisions of such new regulations or restric-
tions , may be continued .
Section 2. The use of a building or structure existing at
the time of the adoption of any zoning regulation or restriction ,
or at the time of any adjustment or revision thereof or amend-
ment thereto , although such use does not conform to the pro-
visions of such new regulations or restrictions , may be continued ,
and a nonconforming use of the building or structure may be
changed to another nonconforming use of the same or more
restricted classification .

, amending section 218.210 of the

.
Be
itenacted by the General Assembly
ofthe Common-
wealth
ofKentucky
:
Section 218.210
ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes
is
amended to read as follows
:
(1) Any person who sells
, supplies
, prescribes
, administers
,

orfurnishes
,
orcauses
to
besold
,supplied
, prescribed
,

inviola-

312 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
shall be guilty of a felony and shall be fined not more than five
thousand dollars and confined in the penitentiary for not less
than twenty years , and may be so confined for a longer period
or for life .
(2 ) Any person who violates any provision of this chapter ,
for which a specific penalty is not otherwise provided , except
KRS 218.250 , shall be guilty of a felony and shall , for the first
offense, be punished by confinement in the penitentiary for not
less than two years nor more than five years or by a fine of not

lars , both , and , for each subsequent offense , be punished by con-
finement in the penitentiary for not less than five years nor
more than twenty years , and by a fine of not more than five
thousand dollars .
(3 ) No court or judge thereof , shall , pursuant to KRS
439.020 to 439.060 or otherwise , postpone or suspend judgment
or sentence or probate a defendant when the defendant has
pleaded guilty to or been convicted of selling , supplying , pre-
scribing , administering , dispensing or furnishing , or causing to
be sold , supplied , prescribed , administered , dispensed or fur-
nished , any narcotic drug in violation of this chapter to a person
under twenty - one years of age , or when the defendant has pre-
viously pleaded guilty to or been conviced of a violation of any
of the other provisions of this chapter , except KRS 218.250 , or
has previously pleaded guilty to or been convicted of a violation
of any of the narcotic laws of the United States of America or
any state or territory thereof .
( 4 ) Whenever a person charged with a violation of sub-
section (1 ) of KRS 218.250 waives a jury, the court or judge
thereof may, upon the defendant pleading or being found guilty ,
sentence the defendant to imprisonment for any period of time
not exceeding twelve months without the intervention of a jury.
Notwithstanding the provisions of subsection ( 2 ) of KRS 218.250 ,
no court or judge thereof shall , pursuant to KRS 439.020 to
439.060 or otherwise , postpone or suspend judgment or sentence
or probate a defendant when the defendant has pleaded guilty
to or been convicted of a violation of KRS 218.250 unless the
defendant consents to enter and forthwith enters an institution
approved by the court for the treatment of narcotic addicts and

CHAPTER 124
(Н. В. 69
)
AN ACT relating to income taxation
.
Be
itenacted by the General Assembly
ofthe Common-
wealth
ofKentucky
:

CHAPTER 124 315
Section 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 141.020 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
An annual tax shall be paid for each taxable year by every
resident individual of this state, upon his entire net income as
defined in this chapter for purposes of taxation , at the following
rates , less the tax credits as authorized by KRS 141.060 :
( a ) Two per cent of the amount of net income not exceed-
ing three thousand dollars .
( b ) Three per cent of the amount of net income in excess
of three thousand dollars but not in excess of four thousand
dollars .
( c ) Four per cent of the amount of net income in excess
of four thousand dollars but not in excess of five thousand
dollars .
(d) Five per cent of the amount of net income in excess
of five thousand dollars but not in excess of eight thousand
dollars .
( e ) Six per cent of the amount of net income in excess
of eight thousand dollars .
Section 2. Section 141.040 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
Every corporation organized under the laws of this state
and every foreign corporation doing business in this state , or
deriving income from activities or other sources in this state ,
except state and national banks and trust companies , building
and loan associations organized under the laws of this state
and under the laws of this state and under the laws of the United
States and making loans to members only, insurance companies ,
including farmers' or other mutual hail, cyclone , windstorm or
fire insurance companies , insurers and reciprocal underwriters ,
and religious , educational , charitable and other corporations not
organized or conducted for pecuniary profit, shall pay for each
taxable year a tax to be computed by the Department of Revenue
upon the entire net income of the corporation derived from busi-
ness done , property located , activities or sources in this state.
This tax shall be at the rate of four and one-half per cent of the
entire net income of the corporation , or the portion thereof tax-
able within this state, determined as provided in this chapter .

316 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 3. Subsection ( 1) of Section 141.060 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) The taxes due as computed under the provisions of
KRS 141.020 or under the provisions of KRS 141.025 shall be
credited with the following amounts :
( a) Twenty dollars , in the case of an unmarried individual ;
(b) Forty dollars , in the case of a married individual , living
with husband or wife and in the case of a head of a family . If
such husband and wife make separate returns , the tax credit
allowed by this subsection may be taken by either or divided
between them ;
(c ) Ten dollars for each person , other than husband or wife ,
or the first dependent of a head of a family, dependent upon
and receiving more than fifty per cent of his support from the
taxpayer during the taxable year for which the return is filed,
if the dependent person is under eighteen years of age or is
incapable of self-support because mentallly or physically de-
fective .
Section 4. Section 141.180 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended and reinacted to read as follows :
( 1 ) Every individual having for the taxable year a net
income of one thousand dollars or over or a gross income of
fifteen hundred dollars or over , if single, or if married and not
living with husband or wife ; and every married individual living
with husband or wife whose net income combined with the net
income of his or her spouse exceeds two thousand dollars , or
whose gross income combined with the gross income of his or
her spouse exceeds twenty -five hundred dollars , shall make to
the Department of Revenue a return stating specifically the
items which he claims as deductions and tax credits allowed by
this chapter . If the individual is unable to make his own return ,
the return shall be made by a duly authorized agent .
(2 ) A husband and wife not living together shall make
separate returns . A husband and wife living together may make
a joint return , or may make separate returns .
( 3 ) An optional return properly filed under the provisions
of KRS 141.025 shall be deemed sufficient to satisfy the pro-
visions of this section .

CHAPTER 125 317
(4) Each individual return shall be verified by a written
declaration that it is made under the penalties of perjury .
Section 5. The provisions of this act shall be effective with
respect to individuals and corporations reporting under the
provisions of Chapter 141 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes on
a calendar year basis beginning January 1, 1952 , and with respect
to individuals and corporations reporting on a fiscal year basis
with the first fiscal year that begins on or after January 1, 1952 .
Section 6. Sections 141.250 , 141.260 and 141.270 , of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes are hereby repealed .

CHAPTER 126
(Н. В. 72)
Approved March 4, 1952 .
AN ACT relating to the births and deaths , amending section 213.150 of
the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 213.150 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) Each local registrar shall be entitled to receive the
sum of fifty cents , and each physician or midwife shall be entitled
to receive the sum of twenty -five cents, for each birth certificate
and for each death certificate properly and correctly filled out ,
signed , and registered with or reported by him and correctly
copied and duly returned to the Division of Vital Statistics . In
case no births or deaths are registered during any month , the
local registrar shall be entitled to receive the sum of twenty -five
cents for each report to that effect promptly made in accordance
with this chapter .
(2) All amounts payable to local registrars, physicians and

320 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
midwives under this section, shall be paid by the treasurer of
the county in which the registration district is located upon cer-
tification by the Division of Vital Statistics .
(3 ) The Division of Vital Statistics shall certify , at least
annually to the treasurers of the several counties , the number of
births and deaths registered together with the names of the
local registrars , physicians and midwives entitled to fee pre-
scribed by this section, and the amount due each at the rate
fixed herein .
(4) No local registrar , physician , midwife , sexton or under-
taker shall charge or collect from any member of a family in
which a birth or death occurs , any fee for complying with the
provisions of this chapter .

in this state.
Section 4. If a person comes into this state in obedience
to a summons directing him to attend and testify in this state
he shall not while in this state pursuant to such summons be
subject to arrest or the service of process , civil or criminal , in
connection with matters which arose before his entrance into
this state under the summons .
If a person passes through this state while going to another
state in obedience to a summons to attend and testify in that
state or while returning therefrom , he shall not while so passing
through this state be subject to arrest or the service of process ,
civil or criminal , in connection with matters which arose before
his entrance into this state under the summons .
Section 5. This act may be cited as " Uniform Act to Secure
the Attendance of Witness from Within or Without a State in
Criminal Proceedings ."

operate gas systems .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1) Any county court , except in counties containing a city
of the first class, upon petition of seventy-five resident free

acquire , develop , maintain , and operate a system for the dis-

the county . The petition shall describe the territory intended to
be included in the area to be served and shall set out the reasons
a gas distribution system is needed .
(2 ) When the petition is filed, the court shall give notice of
the filing by publication in three issues of a newspaper of general
circulation in the county , or if there is no newspaper in the
county , by posting notices in three public places in the water
district . Within thirty days after the publication of notices , any
resident of the district may file objections , and the Court shall
set the case for hearing within ten days . If the court finds the
establishment of a gas distributing system by such district rea-
sonably necessary for the public health , convenience , and com-
fort of the residents , it shall make an order authorizing the
establishment or acquisition of the gas distributing system .

Section 9. Transferring registration . (1) When a voter
registered outside of cities of the first class changes his place of
residence from one precinct to another in the same county , he
may apply to the county clerk in person or by letter to have his
registration record transferred to the proper precinct . If by
letter , the clerk shall mail to the voter , a form to be prescribed
by the State Board of Registration and Purgation which the
voter shall fill out and return to the clerk . The clerk shall then
mail a notice to the address of the voter appearing on the exist-
ing registration records , stating that such application has been
made and the particulars thereof , and that the change will be
made unless protest is filed at the office of the county clerk
within five days from the date of the notice . If no protest is
filed within the period of five days the county clerk shall change
the registration record to the proper precinct as requested . If
protest is filed within the five -day period , the change shall not
be made until the clerk has investigated the matter and given
the protesting voter an opportunity to be heard by the board .
When a change is so made , the clerk shall , under the head
"remarks " or on the back of his original and copy registration
record forms , write the words "removed to pre-
cinct ," and the date . Such transfers shall be registered only on
those days when registrations are allowed under Section 4 or as
provided in Section 53 of this Act .

class shall be annexed by said city then the clerk shall transfer

(2) If a vacancy occurs , the mayor shall immediately notify
the city executive committee that nominated the member with
respect to whom the vacancy has occurred , and that committee
shall without delay submit to the mayor a written list of five
qualified persons . If the persons so nominated are not, in the
opinion of the mayor , competent or desirable for appointment ,
the mayor shall immediately request the committee to submit
a new list , and the committee shall without delay submit to the
mayor a new written list of five qualified persons . From the
two lists the mayor shall at once fill the vacancy for the unex-
pired term by appointment .
(3) If any member of the board , during his term of office ,
accepts any office , whether elective or appointive , or becomes
a candidate for any elective office , he shall ipso facto vacate his
office on the board . No member of the city executive com-
mittee of either party shall be a member of the board or hold
any clerical or other position under the board .
Section 19. Bond of board members . Each member of the
board of registration commissioners shall execute bond , with
good security , to be approved by the mayor . The bond shall ,
when approved by the mayor , be filed with the comptroller and
inspector of the city and shall be a public record . Suit may be
maintained upon the bond against the principal and sureties for
any violation by the principal . The suit shall be brought in the
name of the city, and the amount recovered in the suit shall be
paid to the board of registration commissioners for the purposes
of the board .
Section 20. Employes of board . The board of registration
commissioners shall appoint a registrar and an assistant registrar
who shall act respectively as secretary and assistant secretary
of the board . They shall have general charge of

board and shall perform the duties that the board prescribes

Section 23. Supplies ; purchase of; officers not to be in-
terested in contracts ; no future charge to be created . All sup-
plies required under the statutes relating to registration and
purgation in cities of the first class shall be bought by the board
by competitive bids upon uniform specifications , and the con-
tract shall be awarded to the lowest and best bidder. No mem-
ber , officer or employe of the board of registration commissioners
shall be directly or indirectly interested in any contract with the
board . The board shall not , to any extent or under any device ,
create a charge upon the income of a future year .
Section 24. Offices of board ; office hours ; registration sup-
plies . ( 1) The board of registration commissioners shall acquire
and maintain a permanent office and equip it in such manner
as it deems necessary. The office shall be maintained and kept
open to the public every day , except Sundays and holidays ,
between the hours of 9:00 a.m. and 4:30 p.m . except that the .
board may authorize the office to be closed at 1:00 p.m. on
Saturdays . The board also may keep the office open to the
public at night or at such other hours as the board may determine ,
not inconsistent with the provisions of this Act. The board
may establish such temporary branch offices , not exceeding one
for each ward , as it deems necessary , and keep them open for
not more than one week in each of the months of May , June ,
August and September .
(2) The board shall prescribe and provide all forms , rec-
ords and paraphernalia required for the conduct of registration .
Section 25. Method of registration . (1 ) During the time
the office of the board of registration commissioners is lawfully
open for registration the registrar and assistant registrar and
such deputy registrars as the board appoints shall register every
person who applies in person at the permanent or any temporary
branch office of the board and complies with the provisions of
subsection (2) of this section .
(2) Every person applying to be registered shall make oath
on the form prescribed by the board that he possesses the legal
qualifications for registration , and shall be required to sign his
name on two registration record forms . The signature shall be
attested by two employes of the board of opposite political
af-
filiations
. One
ofthe registration record forms shall
befilled

but only the signatures of the registrant and of two employes of
the board of opposite political affiliations shall appear on the
duplicate form . The registrar or assistant or deputy registrar
shall enter on the registration record form , in the proper places ,
the name, address , color , sex , apparent height , weight , party
affiliation , occupation , birthplace and mother's name of the
person applying for registration , and any distinguishing physical
characteristic of the person that may serve as an additional
means of identification . He shall also enter the date of registra-
tion and whether or not the person is over twenty -one years of
age , and, if the person is naturalized , he shall enter the time and
place of naturalization . If a person applying for registration
states under oath that he cannot write his name, the registrar
or assistant or deputy registrar shall sign the registration form
for him , and he shall sign the form by making his mark which
shall be attested by the registrar or assistant or deputy registrar
and by another employe of the board of opposite political

Section 30. Comparative signature books ; comparison of
voter's signature with registration record ; voting record . ( 1)
Prior to every primary , special , or regular election in a city of the
first class , the county clerk shall have prepared for each precinct
of the city a comparative signature book which shall be in
permanent form and contain sufficient blank sheets of non-
detachable type to afford reasonable space for as many signa-
tures as there are registered voters in the precinct . The county
clerk shall follow the instructions of the board of registration
commissioners with respect to the exact form and makeup of the
comparative signature books and they shall be furnished and
paid for in the same manner as other election paraphernalia . The
county clerk shall deliver the comparative signature books along
with other election paraphernalia to the clerks of election of the
several precincts who shall sign a receipt therefor . Before a
vote is cast by any person , at any primary , special , or regular
election in a city of the first class, he shall sign his name in the
comparative signature book , unless he signed his registration
record by making his mark , and the officers of election shall
compare the signature of every such person with the signature
of that person on the registration record form in the precinct
register . The challengers of the respective political parties
present shall also have the right to compare the signature at
the time the comparison is made by the officers of election . If
the comparison of signatures creates a doubt in the minds of two
of the election officers as to whether the person applying to
vote is the same person as the registrant , no vote shall be cast
by him unless he otherwise satisfactorily establishes his identity
as the registrant . When the polls close, the precinct sheriff and
judge of the opposite political faith shall forthwith convey the
comparative signature book to the place where the county clerk
receives other election paraphernalia . The board of registration
commissioners shall have at that place , plainly identified , a duly
authorized representative to whom the sheriff and judge afore-
said shall deliver the comparative signature book and secure a

CHAPTER 134 347
receipt therefor . The board shall retain the comparative signa-
ture books for at least the next two succeeding primary and
general elections .
(2) If any person applying to vote at an election states that
he cannot sign his name , the judges of election shall immediately
swear him as to his name and his inability to write , and there-
upon the judges and the challengers of the respective political
parties shall examine the registration record in the precinct
register , and if it is shown by that record that the person of that
name signed his name on the registration record , said person
shall not be permitted to cast a vote , unless his inability to sign
his name at the date of the election is due to some physical dis-
ability arising subsequent to his registration . The election of-
ficers shall make and return to the board a record sheet of each
person so sworn , showing the disposition made of each case ,
which shall be delivered to the Commonwealth's attorney within
ten days of the election . The record sheets shall be provided
as other election paraphernalia .
(3) The clerk in each precinct shall , immediately after a
person has cast his vote at every primary , special , and regular
election , indicate at the proper place on the registration record
that such person voted at the election .

Section 33. State Board of Registration and Purgation ;
membership , oath . There shall be a State Board of Registration
and Purgation composed of the same members that compose the
State Board of Election Commissioners . The members of the
board shall take an oath to faithfully discharge the duties im-
posed upon them by the laws of this state .
Section 34. General duties of state board . The State Board
of Registration and Purgation shall supervise registration and
purgation under the provisions of this Act outside cities of the
first class , and shall perform such duties as are imposed upon
it by this Act in regard to registration and purgation in cities of
the first class . The board shall see that each county and each
city of the first class complies with the registration and purga-
tion laws applying to them , and shall ascertain whether the
county court clerks and boards of registration commissioners
have
allthe necessary registration equipment
toproperly register
the voters

ofthe first class

toproperly
carry out the registration and purgation laws
ofthe state

Section 39. Certificate of appointment of county board
members ; officers . (1) The members of each county board
of registration and purgation shall receive a certificate of ap-
pointment signed by the chairman and attested by the secre-
tary of the State Board of Registration and Purgation , except
the third member , when appointed by the two members, in
which event the two members shall prepare the certificate . The
certificate of appointment shall be presented by the members
to the county clerk of the county of their appointment , who shall
administer the oath of office to them .
(2) The members of each county board of registration and
purgation , when appointed and qualified , shall meet and select
one of their members as chairman . The county court clerk shall
act as secretary of the board . The chairman of the board shall

If no protest is filed within that time , the registration shall be

tion , and shall not cancel the registration if the person is legally
entitled to registration .
Section 46. Purgation for primary election in cities of first
class by house to house canvass . The city board of registration
of each county containing a city of the first class shall cause a
purging and correcting of the registration lists and records of
the city for primary elections upon the request in writing of the
county executive committee of either of the two political parties
having representation upon the State Board of Election Com-
missioners , or upon the request in writing of any candidate for
office in the primary . The purging and correcting shall be con-
ducted as provided in this section .
(2) The registration commissioners of the city , shall cause
to be made a house to house canvass in each precinct of the city
with respect to which a request has been made under subsection
(1) of this section . The canvass shall be made jointly by two
investigators in each precinct , who shall be appointed by the
board of registration commissioners ten days in advance of the
date specified for the commencement of the canvass . One of the
investigators shall be selected from each of the two political
parties having representation upon the State Board of Election
Commissioners . The city board of registration may remove any
investigator and appoint a substitute . The canvass shall be made
during the first two weeks of the sixty -day period next preced-
ing the date upon which the primary is to be held. The board
of registration commissioners shall prepare and furnish each in-
vestigator with a list of names and addresses of the registered
voters in the precinct which he is to investigate , arranged in
convenient order to facilitate investigation .
(3) The investigators for each precinct shall make a written
report to the board of registration commissioners not later than
the last day of the two weeks period provided for making the
canvass , giving the names and addresses of
allpersons properly

CHAPTER 134 357
registered in the precinct who are legally entitled to vote in the
primary election to be held in that year . When the reports of
the investigators in any precinct agree that any person registered
in the precinct is not legally entitled to vote at the election to
be held in that year , the city board of registration commissioners
of the city shall remove , transfer or cancel such registration as
the case may be , so as to make the record correspond with the
report made by the investigators and to correct the registration
record to the date of the report . No change shall be made until
after notice has been mailed by the board of registration com-
missioners to the person affected , at the address appearing upon
the registration record on file in the office of the board , stating
that it is the intention of the board of registration commissioners
to change , transfer or cancel his registration unless protest is
filed within five days in the office of the board of registration
commissioners . If protest is filed, no such change shall be made
until after the protestant has been given a reasonable oppor-
tunity to be heard , at which hearing the right of the protestant
to vote shall be determined by the board of registration com-
missioners .
(4) Each investigator provided for in this section shall be
paid reasonable compensation by the board , the board to de-
termine the amount .
Section 47. Purgation for regular election in cities of the
first class by house to house canvass . (1 ) The board of registra-
tion commissioners of each city of the first class shall cause to
made a house to house canvass in each precinct of the city in
each year , for the purpose of checking the registration records .
The canvass shall be made jointly by two investigators in each
precinct . One of the investigators in each precinct shall be ap-
pointed by the board from a list of three names for that precinct
submitted in writing by one of the city executive committees ,
and one in each precinct from a list of three names for that
precinct submitted in writing by the other city executive com-
mittee . The lists shall be submitted ten days in advance of the
date specified for the commencement of the canvass . If either
committee fails to submit a list within that time , the board shall
appoint the investigators to represent that party from persons
registered as members of that party. The board may remove

the first class to recommend purgation of the registration lists by
stamping with the rubber stamp provided in section 49 of this
Act the registration cards of persons who have since the date of
their registration , died , moved from the precinct , or otherwise
become disqualified to vote, upon reasonable proof of such dis-
qualification of any voter . The judges of election shall stamp
the registration record of such voter with the stamp provided for
in section 49 of this Act, and shall each sign their names on the
blank lines below the words “PURGATION RECOMMENDED ."
Section 51. Assistants to precinct judges in purgation . The
judges of election may appoint other persons to assist them in
performing their duties under section 50 of this Act, but the
persons so appointed shall serve without compensation .
Section 52. Purgation by county board , outside cities of
first class ; notice and hearing . Within ten days after the primary
election day , the county board shall meet at the county court
clerk's office for the purpose of passing upon the purgation rec-
ommendations made by the precinct judges . The board shall
examine purgation recommendations , and determine which of
the persons whose purgation was recommended are not legally
entitled to be registered . The board shall forthwith mail a no-
tice to each such voter at his address as shown on the registration
records , notifying him that his registration will be cancelled
unless he appears before the board within five days and shows
cause why his name should not be stricken . If the voter appears
and desires to be heard , the board shall hear him and determine
whether or not his name should be purged . If the board deter-
mines that the name should be purged , it shall enter the word
"Purged ," and the date, upon the registration record of such
person , and the chairman and one other member of the opposite
political party of the board shall sign their names immediately
below the word "Purged ." The county court clerk shall there-
upon remove the registration records of such persons from the
registration books , and place them in the "Cancelled Registra-
tion File . " If the board determines that any name recommended
forpurgation
bythe precinct judges should not
bepurged
,
it
shall enter the words
"Purgation Refused
," and the date
, upon
the registration record
ofsuch person
, and the chairman and
one other member
ofthe opposite political party
ofthe board

CHAPTER 134 361
shall sign their names immediately below the words "Purgation
Refused ." If any voter to whom notice is mailed as provided in
this section fails to appear within five days , the board may there-
upon purge his name without further notice or hearing .
Section 53. Written notices. (1) All written notices af-
fecting the registration of a voter required , by this Act , to be sent
by mail shall be sent first - class with the name and address of the
sender shown on the upper lefthand corner of the envelope ,
with the following notation thereunder : "If undeliverable at
address shown , return to sender immediately ." When the mail
notice has been returned , as provided herein , the county or city
board , or whoever is requested to send out the notice , shall pre-
pare a list of said addressees with their addresses , and submit
same to the postmaster at whose office the voter has designated
on his registration card as his P. O. address , and request that said
list be corrected as to addresses . When said list is returned to the
board , or whoever is the sender of the notice , it shall immediately
get out another notice exactly like the first one and send it to
the new address indicated on the corrected list returned by the
postmaster, and shall enclose therein a form affidavit requesting
a sworn statement as to when the addressee moved to the new
address . If the affidavit is returned and shows that the addressee
moved to the new address prior to the fifty -nine day period , then
the registration card shall be placed by the board in the proper
precinct and the registrant shall be eligible to vote at the coming
primary or general election . If the affidavit shows that the ad-
dressee moved subsequent to the qualifying period , then the
registration card shall be placed in the suspended file and the
registrant shall not be entitled to vote in the coming primary or
general election as the case may be, but shall be eligible to vote
at the next primary or general election when he satisfies all the
requirements of a voter imposed by law. If the list returned by
the postmaster , does not show a forwarding address , or if the
registrant fails to reply to the second notice or return the
re-
quired affidavit
, then the registrant's card shall
bemarked
"Can-
celled
.
"
(2) All written notices required
ofthe county board
bythis
Act shall be sent out over the name of the chairman of the
board
.

362 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 54. No purging to be done later than twenty days
before election . No decision of any purgation officer , county
board of registration and purgation , or board of registration com-
missioners of a city of the first class , affecting the right of a voter
to vote in any primary , regular or special election , or removing ,
transferring , cancelling or suspending his registration , shall be
rendered by such purgation officer or board later than twenty
days before any election .
Section 55. Appeals from decisions of purgation officers
or boards . Any person whose right to vote has been denied or
whose name has been stricken from the registration lists or reg-
istration books , or who is otherwise aggrieved by any act in
respect to his registration , by the county board of registration
and purgation , or by the board of registration commissioners of
a city of the first class , and any person or party executive com-
mittee that has challenged the registration of any voter and is
aggrieved by any act of the board of registration commissioners
of a city of the first class or the county board of registration and
purgation , may appeal from said boards . In the case of voters
in cities of the first class , the appeal shall be taken to any judge
of the circuit court of the county in which the city is located .
In the case of other voters , the appeal shall be taken to the
county judge or to a judge of the circuit court of the county .
The appeal shall be taken by presenting to the judge a written
statement of the act of the board complained of, and the grounds
of the complaint , or the statement shall be made on printed forms
furnished by the board . Before the appeal is taken , written
notice shall have been given to the board complained of, stating
the time and place at which and the name of the judge before
whom the appeal will be presented , and the grounds therefor ,
so as to give the board a reasonable opportunity to be present
at such time and place .
(2) The appeals provided for by this section shall be per-
fected not less than five days prior to any primary , regular or
special election , and if not perfected within that time no county
or circuit judge shall have jurisdiction to hear the appeal . There
shall be no court costs taxed on appeals .
(3) The judge to whom the appeal is taken shall , at the
time fixed in the notice of appeal or at a subsequent time fixed

CHAPTER 134 363
by him, summarily decide the question presented and endorse on
the statement of complaint an order disposing of it. The action
of the judge shall be final , and upon return of the statement of
complaint , with the order of the judge endorsed thereon , to the
county clerk or to the office of the board of registration com-
missioners , the clerk or board shall correct its record in accord-
ance with the order .
Section 56. Suspended lists . If the county board of regis-
tration and purgation of any county or the board of registration
commissioners of any city of the first class finds that there are
names of voters upon the registration lists or registration books
who are properly and legally registered but who are not entitled
to vote in the next primary , regular or special election , they shall
not cancel the registration but shall cause the registration au-
thorities to remove the registration cards or names of such voters
from the registration lists or registration books so that the names
shall not be sent to the precincts on election day for the coming
election in which such voters are not qualified to participate .
The registration officers shall place the registration cards in some
secure place to be held and identified as suspended lists of regis-
tered voters , and the names of such voters shall not be replaced
in the registration lists or registration books until such time as
they are eligible to vote . Such voters shall not be required to
re -register .
Section 57. Record of canceled registrations . The county
clerk and the city registration board shall preserve , for not less
than two years ,

Section 58. Grand jury investigation . It shall be the duty
of the first grand jury which convenes in each calendar year to
make an investigation of the registration records of each county
and seek to determine whether or not the board , the county clerk
and the employes of the board have carried out the provisions of
this Act , and make a report thereon .
Section 59. Fees of county clerk in connection with purga-
tion. The county clerk is entitled , for all services with relation
to the purging of the registration outside cities of the first class,
to a fee of 10 cents for each name ordered off or canceled , as
provided in section 45 of this Act , to be paid by the county .
Section 60. Old boards continued until successors ap-
pointed . ( 1 ) Each county board of registration and purgation
appointed under the provisions of KRS 117.390 shall continue in

CHAPTER 135
(Η . Β. 96)
Approved March 4, 1952 .
AN ACT relating to election precincts , amending section 116.180 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 116.180 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
116.180 Election precincts ; number of voters in . ( 1 ) Each
county shall be divided into election precincts . The county
court shall make the division as often as necessary to comply
with the provisions of this section, and shall establish the name
or number and the boundaries of the precincts . Each precinct
shall contain , as nearly as practicable , an equal number of voters,
based on the number of registered voters in the precinct , not to
exceed three hundred and fifty voters to a precinct . This section
is not intended to affect KRS 125.180 , relating to precincts
wherein voting machines are used or may be used .
( 2) If the county judge fails to perform his duty herein
required , then any citizen and voter of the county may apply to
the circuit court of the county for a summary mandatory order
requiring him to perform his duty . Appeals may be taken to the
court of appeals by either party .
Approved March 4, 1952 .

CHAPTER 136
(Η . Β. 97)
AN ACT relating to voting procedure , amending section 118.280 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 118.280 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
118.280 Issuance and marking of ballot . ( 1 ) Any person
desiring to vote and legally entitled to vote shall give his name
and residence to the clerk holding the ballots , who shall write
the same upon the stub of the ticket in the blank places provided
thereof , together with the voter's registered number . The clerk
shall enter the same information on the secondary stub. The
clerk shall then detach the ballot , with the secondary stub
attached , from the main stub . The ballot shall then be signed
on the back by one of the election officers . No ballot not so
signed in the handwriting by one of the election officers shall
be counted by the canvassing board . After the ballot has been
signed by one of the election officers , it shall be handed to the
voter . The clerk shall give the voter only one ballot , and on
request of the voter shall explain the manner of voting . When
there are separate ballots for offices or public questions to be
voted for by only a portion of the voters in a precinct , such
ballots shall be given only to such voters as reside in the portion
of the precinct that is within the territory for which the office
is provided or with respect to which the public question is sub-
mitted . The separate ballots for the election of members of
boards of education shall be issued as provided in KRS 160.240 .
(2 ) On receipt of his ballot , the voter shall forthwith , and
without leaving the room , retire alone to one of the voting
booths . Before marking his ballot, he shall examine it to
ascertain if the facsimile signature of the county clerk and the
signature of the election officer shows upon the ballot . The
method of marking ballots for the election of members of boards
of education shall be as provided in KRS 160.260 . As to other
ballots the voter shall prepare his ballot by marking a cross
mark in the appropriate square immediately following the name
of the candidate of his choice for each office to be filled , and

CHAPTER 136 367
in the case of questions submitted to a vote of the people by
marking a cross mark in the appropriate square against the
answer he desires to give . If any voter desires to vote for each
and every candidate of one party , he shall mark a cross mark
in the circle under the device of the party , and the vote shall
then be counted for all the candidates under the device . If a
cross mark is made in the circle under the device of the party ,
and a cross mark is also made in the square after the name of
one or more candidates of a different party , the vote shall be
counted for the candidates so marked , and not for the candidates
for the same offices of the party so marked , but the vote shall
be counted for the other candidates under the party device . If
the voter marks more names than there are persons to be
elected to an office , or if for any reason it is impossible to deter-
mine the voter's choice for an office to be filled, his ballot shall
not be counted for that office . No ballot shall be rejected for
any technical error that does not make it impossible to deter-
mine the voter's choice . Any voter may, in a regular or special
election , vote for any qualified person other than those whose
names are printed on the ballots , for any office to be filled , by
writing the name of that person under the designation of the
office and placing a cross mark to the right of the name . All
marking upon the ballots shall be done with black ink stencil ,
except the writing in of names as provided in the preceding
sentence , which shall be done with black lead pencil . There
shall he kept in each booth the necessary stencils and pencils ,
securely fastened by a cord of sufficient length to enable voters
to use them .

Section 2. Section 126.170 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
126.170 Verification of registration ; list of nonregistered
applicants ; registration of applicants in Armed Forces . ( 1 ) Upon
receipt of any application for an absent voter's ballot , the county
court clerk shall examine the registration books for the purpose
of ascertaining whether or not the applicant is a registered voter
as stated in the application . If the county court clerk finds that
the applicant is so registered , he shall forward a ballot to the
voter as provided in KRS 126.190. If the county clerk does not
find the applicant to be so registered , and if the application does
not indicate that the applicant is a member of the Armed Forces ,
or if an employe or the wife of an employe of the United
States Government stationed abroad , the clerk shall immediately
mail a letter to the applicant , at the address stated in the appli-
cation , advising him that his application cannot be honored be-
cause of the fact that he is not registered , and advising him what
period of time , if any , remains in which he may register prior to
such election , and the clerk shall place the application in a file
entitled "Nonregistered Applicants for Absent Voters ' Ballots ."
If the clerk thereafter , and prior to the election , receives notice
from the applicant advising the clerk that the applicant has
registered , the clerk shall again examine the registration books ,
and if he finds that the applicant has registered he shall remove
the application from the nonregistered applicants ' file and for-
ward a ballot as provided in KRS 126.190 . Any applications
not so removed from the nonregistered applicants ' file shall be
retained in such file until thirty days after the election , when
they shall be burned by the clerk in the presence of the judge
and sheriff of the county . If the application of a person found
not to be registered indicates that the applicant is a member of
the Armed Forces , or an employe or the wife of an employe
of the United States Government stationed abroad , the county
court clerk, upon being satisfied that the applicant is entitled
to be registered, shall cause the applicant to be registered in
the registration records of the proper precinct , and shall mark
upon such registration records the words , "Absentee Registra-
tion . " Such registration may be made at any time prior to the
election for which an absent voter's ballot is applied for.

CHAPTER 138
(Н. В. 99)
Approved March 4, 1952 .
AN ACT relating to support of children , amending section 405.020 of the
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 405.020 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
( 1 ) The father and mother shall have the joint custody ,
nurture and education of their minor children . If either of the
parents dies, the survivor , if suited to the trust , shall have the
custody , nurture and education of the minor children . The
father shall be primarily liable for the nurture and education of
his minor children .
(2 ) The father and mother shall have the joint custody ,
care and support of their adult children who are wholly depend-
ent because of permanent physical or mental disability . If

CHAPTER 140 371
either of the parents dies , the survivor , if suited to the trust ,
shall have the custody , care and support of such children . The
father shall be primarily liable for the custody , care and support
of wholly dependent adult children .
Approved March 4, 1952 .
CHAPTER 139
(Н. В. 100)
AN ACT relating to the Department of Welfare including smoking
regulations in the Penal Institutions and Houses of Reform .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Not withstanding the provisions of KRS 438.020
to 438.040 the Commissioner of Welfare is authorized to permit
or prohibit the practice of smoking by the inmates of the Penal
Institutions and Houses of Reform , regardless of the age of the
inmates , whenever he believes such regulation will enhance or
improve the discipline of the inmates of these institutions .

(4 ) No officer or employee covered by the provisions of
this Act shall directly or indirectly give , solicit or receive or be
in any manner whatever concerned in the giving , receiving ,
soliciting or publicizing any assessment , gift, subscription or
contribution to or for any political party or candidate for public
office.
( 5) No individual , corporation , association or group of
individuals shall orally or by letter or otherwise solicit or be in
any manner concerned in soliciting or collecting any assessment ,
subscription , gift or contribution of any character for any po-
litical party or any candidate for public office from any officer or
employee covered by any of the provisions of this Act . No person
shall use or promise to use his personal influence or official
authority to secure any appointment or promotion to any position
of employment covered by the provisions of this Act , as a reward
or return for personal or partisan political service . No candidate
applying for original appointment or promotion to any position
of employment covered by this Act shall sign or execute or
promise to sign or execute a resignation dated or undated in
advance of such appointment or promotion . No officer or em-
ployee covered by the provisions of this Act shall be suspended,
laid off, demoted , promoted , fined , disciplined or threatened , or
in any way changed in rank , duty or compensation for with-
holding or neglecting to pay or make any contribution of any

376 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
sort , or character , either in money , goods or services or anything
of value for any political purpose whatsoever .
No person shall take part in preparing any political assess-
ment , subscription or contribution with the intent that the same
shall be sent or presented to or collected from any officer or
employee covered hereby and no person shall knowingly send
or present in any manner any such assessment , subscription ,
request for gift or contribution or request payment of any of the
foregoing to or from any officer or employee covered by this Act .
No officer or employee covered by this Act shall act as an
officer of any political organization , be a member thereof or take
an active part in any political campaign or serve as a member of
any committee of any such political organization or circulate or
seek signatures to any petition or official paper provided for by
any primary or general election laws , or act as a worker for any
political party , or act as a worker at the polls in favor of or
opposed to any candidate for election or nomination to any
public office whether Federal , State, County or Municipal .
No examination question in any examination held by the
Board shall relate to any political or religious opinion , belief,
affiliation or service and no appointment , promotion , demotion ,
suspension , fine or removal shall be brought about , effected ,
affected or influenced by such opinions , beliefs , affiliations or
services .
No officer or employee covered by this Act shall foster , pro-
mote , or be concerned with any actions involving political or
religious controversies or prejudices .
Nothing contained in this Act shall be so construed as to
abridge the rights of any officer or employee with respect to his
or her personal opinions or beliefs or right to vote .

CHAPTER 143
(Н. В. 119 )
AN ACT relating to salaries of mayors and commissioners .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 89.480 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1) In cities of the second class the mayor shall receive

Legislative Body.

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-

Section 1. Section 161.640 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-

Retirement annuities shall be payable monthly . The first

CHAPTER 145
(Н. В. 125 )
AN ACT relating to common schools with particular reference to en-
trance age requirements .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec. 1. Section 158.030 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
A "common school " is an elementary or secondary school
of the state supported in whole or in part by public taxation . No
school shall be deemed a "common school " or receive support
from public taxation unless the school is taught by a qualified
teacher for a term of eight or more months during the school
year and every child residing in the district who is six years of
age and over has had the privilege of attending it. Provided ,
however , that any child who is six years of age or who may
become six years of age by December 30 following the opening
of school may enter school provided that he enters within thirty
calendar days of the beginning of that school year , with the
exception that in schools having mid-year promotions any child
who is six years of age or who may become six years of age
within thirty calendar days after the opening of the second
semester may enter school at the beginning of that semester .
Any child who becomes six years of age later than December 30

382 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
following the opening of the school year shall not enter school
during that year and in schools having mid -year promotions any
child who becomes six years of age later than thirty days after
the opening of the second semester shall not enter school during
that semester .
Sec . 2. Any member of a school board who votes to permit
entrance to a school of any child not eligible therefor under the
provisions of KRS 158.030 shall be fined not less than five dollars
nor more than fifty dollars .
CHAPTER 146
(Н. В. 127)
AN ACT concerning waterworks in cities other than first class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 96.320 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read a follows :
"Cities of the second class that own a waterworks may
operate such waterworks as a department of the city, or may
appoint a commission to operate such waterworks . If such a
commission is appointed , it shall be styled "Commissioners of
Waterworks ," and shall be composed of from three to five mem-
bers to be appointed by the mayor , subject to the approval of
the city legislative body. If a commission is composed of five
members , the mayor shall appoint , in addition to the five mem-
bers , a member of the legislative body of the city who shall be
an ex officio member of the commission . They shall be residents
and legal voters of the city. The terms of the members shall
be fixed by the city legislative body, or they may be appointed
for indefinite terms , subject to removal by the city legislative
body for cause. The commissioners shall give bond for the faith-
ful performance of their duties in the sum of five thousand dol-
lars. The commissioners shall manage the water system of the
city. They may appoint a superintendent , secretary, treasurer
and other necessary employees and
fixtheir salaries
. They shall
make full monthly reports
tothe city legislative body
ofthe

operation and condition of the water system , including
all
receipts and expenditures
. Three members
ofthe board shall
constitute
aquorum for the transaction
ofbusiness
.

4, 1952

Section 3. ( 1 ) Except as provided in subsection ( 2 ) of
this section, no person shall engage or attempt to engage in the
practice of medicine or osteopathy within this state , or open ,
maintain or occupy an office or place of business within this state
for engaging in the practice of medicine or osteopathy , or in any
manner announce or express a readiness to engage in the practice
of medicine or osteopathy within this state, unless he holds a
valid and effective license or permit issued by the board as
hereinafter provided .
( 2 ) The provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of this section shall
not apply to :
( a ) Commissioned medical officers of the armed forces
of the United States , or medical officers of the United States
Public Health Service , the United States Veterans Administration
and other agencies of the government of the United States of
America , while said persons are engaged in the performance ,
within this state , of their official duties under federal laws .
(b ) Persons , who being non -residents of Kentucky and
lawfully licensed to practice medicine or osteopathy in their
states of actual residence , infrequently engage in the practice of
medicine or osteopathy within this state, when called to see or
attend particular patients , without opening , maintaining or oc-
cupying an office or place of business within this state .
( c ) Graduates of medical or osteopathic schools approved
by the board , while engaged in performing supervised intern-
ship training approved by the board at hospitals in this State ;
provided , however , that this shall not be construed to otherwise
exempt internes , or to exempt in any manner resident or staff
physicians of hospitals , from the licensure requirements of this
Act .
Section 5. ( 1 ) Subject to the provisions of this Act , the
board shall exercise all of the administrative functions of the
state in the prevention of empiricism and in the regulation of
the practice of medicine and osteopathy . The board is author-

quirements , standards and tests to determine the moral , physical ,
intellectual , educational , scientific , technical and professional
qualifications of applicants for licenses and permits to practice
medicine and osteopathy, and to issue , deny, suspend and revoke

CHAPTER 150 399
Section 15. ( 1 ) All licenses heretofore issued by the
board for the practice of medicine or osteopathy , or any limited
school or system , are hereby declared to be valid and in full
force and effect unless they have become expired , cancelled ,
suspended or revoked in accordance with law.
(2) Nothing in this Act shall be construed as hereafter
conferring upon the board any authority to issue , suspend or
revoke any license for the practice of dentistry , optometry , chiro-
practic , nursing or pharmacy .
Section 16. This Act may be cited as the " Kentucky Medi-
cal and Osteopathic Practice Act of 1952. "
Section 17. ( 1 ) Each first violation of section 3 shall
be punishable by a fine of not less than two hundred and
fifty nor more than five thousand dollars or by imprisonment for
not less than one month nor more than twelve months , or both .
Each subsequent violation of section 3 shall constitute a felony
and be punishable by a fine of not less than one thousand nor
more than ten thousand dollars or by imprisonment in a peniten-
tiary for not less than one nor more than three years , or
both .
(2 ) Each violation of subsection ( 1 ) of section 9 shall
constitute a misdemeanor and be punishable by a fine of not less
than one hundred nor more than five hundred dollars or by im-
prisonment of not more than thirty days , or both , and each day
shall constitute a separate offense .
( 3 ) Each violation of section 10 shall constitute a felony
and shall be punishable by a fine of not less than one thousand
nor more than ten thousand dollars or by imprisonment in a
penitentiary for not less than one nor more than three years .
Conviction , under this subsection , of a holder of a license or
permit , shall result automatically in permanent revocation of
such license or permit .
(4) Conviction of willfully resisting , preventing , impeding ,
obstructing , threatening or interfering with the board or any of

ofany officer
, agent
, inspector

CHAPTER 151
(Committee Substitute for H. B. 140 )
AN ACT relating to reports required in connection with motor vehicle
accidents .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Subsection ( 1 ) of Section 187.320 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The operator of every motor vehicle which is in
any manner involved in an accident within this state , in which
any person is killed or injured or in which total property damage
of one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) or more is sustained, shall
within ten days report the matter in writing to the Department of
State Police for preliminary processing after which time the re-
port shall be forwarded to the Department of Revenue for
additional and final processing . If such operator be physically
incapable of making such report , the owner of the motor vehicle
involved in such accident , shall , as soon as he learns of the
accident , report the matter in writing to the department . The
operator or the owner shall make such other and additional re-
ports relating to such accident as the department shall require .
Section 2. Subsection ( 2) of Section 189.580 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The operator of a vehicle involved in an accident resulting
in injury to or death of any person or in which total property
damage of one hundred dollars ( $ 100.00 ) or more is sustained
shall within ten days report the matter in writing to the Depart-
ment of State Police .
Approved March 24, 1952 .

CHAPTER 153 401
CHAPTER 152
(Н. В. 143)
AN ACT relating to the Department of Agriculture, Labor and Statistics .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. The Department of Agriculture , Labor and
Statistics is authorized to purchase such equipment , including a
large scale test unit and a large meter test unit , as is necessary
to adequately and properly administer the provisions of KRS
363.170 to 363.990 .
Section 2. For the purposes of this Act there is hereby
appropriated out of the General Expenditure Fund in the State
Treasury to the Department of Agriculture , Labor and Statistics ,
for the fiscal year of 1952-53 , the sum of $ 15,200.00 .

CHAPTER 154 409
Such contributions shall become due and be paid at the offices
of the Division in Frankfort by each subject employer to the
Division for the fund in accordance with such regulations as the
Commission prescribes , and shall not be deducted in whole or
in part from the wages of workers in his employ . In the pay-
ment of any contributions , a fractional part of a cent shall be
disregarded , unless it amounts to one-half cent or more , in which
case it shall be increased to one cent .
(2) If any employer fails to file reports when due , or files
an incorrect or insufficient report , the Commissioner may , after
an investigation , determine such employer's liability and assess
the contributions due on the basis of an estimate of such em-
ployer's payroll , and shall give written notice of such assess-
ment to such employer . Such determination or assessment shall
become final twenty days after such notice was mailed to the
employer's last known address unless an appeal is initiated under
KRS 341.430 . After such determination or assessment shall
have become final as herein provided , it shall be received in
evidence in all courts of this Commonwealth as conclusive of the
amount owing the Commonwealth in respect to the claim or
claims concerning which the determination shall have been
made .

TRIBUTION RATE . ( 1) Each subject employer's contribution

three consecutive years immediately preceding the computation
date , and no subject employer's rate shall be less than two and
seven - tenths percent unless funds were available throughout the
preceding year , from which benefits could have been paid from
his account , to any worker in his employ who became unem-
ployed and eligible .

418 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 155
(Н. В. 146)
AN ACT relating to appointment of members of the board of trustees
of the University of Kentucky .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 164.130 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
The government of the University of Kentucky is vested in
a board of trustees , consisting of the Governor , who shall be
chairman of the board , the Superintendent of Public Instruction ,
the Commissioner of Agriculture, Labor and Statistics , and
twelve competent citizens of Kentucky appointed by the Gover-
nor . The terms of the appointed members shall be for four years
and until their successors are appointed and qualify . Beginning
January 1 , 1953 , three of the twelve appointed members shall
be appointed each year , provided however , in order to give ap-
propriate effect to this provision , that between the effective date
of this Act and January 1, 1953 , appointments shall be as follows :
Three members shall be appointed for a one -year term calculated
to have begun on January 1, 1952 , three members shall be ap-
pointed for a three-year term calculated to have begun on Jan-
uary 1 , 1952 , and three members shall be appointed for a four-
year term calculated to have begun on January 1 , 1952. The
terms of the three members of the present board which will ex-
pire December 31 , 1953 , shall not be affected by the foregoing
provision . Three of the appointed members shall be representa-
tive of agricultural interests, three shall be alumni of the univer-
sity and
sixshall
beother distinguished citizens
ofthe state
. The
Governor shall
somake the appointments
as
todivide the repre-
sentation upon the board
, including the
exofficio members
,
equally between the two leading political parties
ofthis state
.
With the exception
ofthe members
ofthe board
oftrustees ap-
pointed from the alumni
ofthe university
,
notwo appointed
members
ofthe board shall
beresidents
ofany one county
in
Kentucky
. Appointments
tofill vacancies shall
bemade for the
unexpired term
inthe same manner
asprovided for original
appointments
.
Approved March
4, 1952
.

CHAPTER 156 419
CHAPTER 156
(Н. В. 148)
AN ACT concerning promotion of agriculture and horticulture .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 247.130 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) The State Fair Board may appoint a manager who
shall not be one of their number . The appointment of a manager
shall be on the basis of training , ability and experience . A man-
ager shall hold his position during the pleasure of the board , but
may be removed only by an affirmative vote of six members of
the board . A manager shall be responsible for the administration
of the policies set by the board , shall devote his entire time and
attention to the performance of such duties and work as may be
required of him by law or be assigned to him by the board , and
shall receive such salary, fixed on an annual basis , as the board
may determine .
(2 ) A manager may organize such administrative divisions
as may be necessary and may designate chiefs of such divisions
who, under his control and supervision , shall have the duties of
direction of such divisions . A manager may employ such other
employes and agents as he deems necessary for the carrying out
of the policies of the board and to conduct the affairs of the State
Fair , and may
fixthe duties and compensation
ofany employes
oragents with the approval
ofthe board
.
(
3) The board may employ
orcontract with such other
persons
, firms
orcorporations
asthe board may deem necessary
ordesirable
toaccomplish
itsduties and functions
; may
fixthe
compensation and the terms
ofemployment
orcontract
ofthose
employed
orcontracted with
; and may assign
tothem such duties
and responsibilities
asthe board may determine
, including the
responsibility
ofactual operation
ofany
or
all
ofthe facilities
under the control of the board
.
(4)
Amanager and any persons employed under this sec-
tion shall not
besubject
tothe provisions
ofKRS 42.110
or
42.120
.

Section 2. Section 137.040 of the Kentucky Revised Stat-
utes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No person shall engage in any of the occupations or
sell any of the articles or exercise any of the privileges named
in KRS 137.050 to 137.110 until he has paid to the county clerk
of the county in which he is engaged in such occupation or in
selling such articles or in exercising such privilege the license tax
prescribed in KRS 137.050 to 137.110 and obtained the license
therefor .
(2 ) This section and KRS 137.050 shall not be construed
to impose a tax on any county , or community fair association or
corporation or similar enterprise or upon the agent or concession-
aire of any such association or corporation operating within the
grounds or rooms devoted to a festival or fair of any such asso-
ciation or corporation which shall engage in any of the occupa-
tions or sell any of the articles or exercise any of the privileges
named in KRS 137.050 to 137.110 for a period of not exceeding
one week in any calendar year without profit to the members or
stockholders or to any other person connected with such asso-
ciation or corporation . This exemption shall not be construed to
permit such association , corporation or similar enterprise to
possess cigarettes in packages , boxes or retail containers upon
which the tax provided in KRS 138.140 has not been paid.
Section 3. None of the provisions of Chapter 137 and
none of the provisions of section 91.200 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes shall apply to any operations on State Fair Grounds
wherein the State Fair Board shares in the receipts and proceeds
of such operations .
Section 4. Subsection ( 1) of Section 247.160 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The State Fair Board shall have exclusive control of
concessions , exhibitions , shows , entertainments and attractions
at any place on the State Fair Grounds and may , in the discretion
of the board , operate any or
all
ofsuch concessions
, exhibitions
,
shows
, entertainments and attractions
, but the board shall not
allow the operation
ofany gambling device
orgame
ofchance
therein
.
Itmay delegate such control
oroperation
toany
of
its
employes
oragents
,
or
toan executive committee
.
Approved March
6, 1952
.

AN ACT to amend Sections 247.250 , 247.280 , 247.330, 247.340 , 247.350 ,
247.370 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes relating to county farm
bureaus and a state federation of county farm bureaus .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That section 247.250 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
The articles of incorporation of a county farm bureau shall
be as follows :
"We, the undersigned farmers or landowners , residents of
--County , Kentucky , hereby adopt the following
articles of incorporation :
"Article 1. The objects of this incorporation shall be to
advance and improve in ------------ County , Kentucky , the
science and art of agriculture , home economics , horticulture and
animal industry , cooperating with the State Department of
Agriculture, the College of Agriculture of the University of
Kentucky and the United States Department of Agriculture .
"Article 2. The name of this corporation shall be the
------- County Farm Bureau of ---------- County ,
Kentucky.
"Article 3. The affairs of this corporation shall be con-
ducted by a president , a vice president , a secretary and a treas-
urer , who shall perform the duties usually pertaining to such
positions , and by a board of directors of not less than seven
members . All officers and directors shall be elected as provided
for in the by-laws at an annual meeting and they shall hold
office for one year and until their successors are elected , but, so
that the terms of all directors shall not expire at the same time ,
the members , by by-law or resolution , may provide that at one
annual election that the term of half the number of directors ,
or if there be an uneven number , that the term of one less than
the majority or the term of a majority of one, elected at said
election , shall be for one year , and the term of the remaining
directors elected at said election shall be for two years , and that
all elections for directors , after the expiration of the aforesaid

422 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
specific terms , shall be for two years . We , the said incorporators ,
have elected the following provisional officers to hold their
respective positions until their successors are elected at the
annual meeting in the year 19------ .

Vice President

Treasurer

"Article 4. The date of the annual meeting shall be stated
in the by-laws . The by-laws shall be adopted by the members
of the corporation and may be amended at any annual meeting
of the members .
"Article 5. The yearly dues of the members of this corpo-
ration shall be not less than $5.00, payable at the time that
application for membership is made and by or on the same date
annually thereafter . No member who has once paid dues shall
forfeit membership until subsequent dues are 60 days in arrears ,
but so long as any dues owing are unpaid , such member shall
have no right to vote in any election or on any matter .
"Article 6. Any citizen of the county owning or operating
a farm , or evidencing a primary interest in farming by reason of
his position or occupation may become a member of the corpo-
ration by paying one year's dues and complying with the articles
of incorporation and by-laws .
"Article 7. This corporation shall endure until terminated
by operation of law. "

(4) This section does not apply to motor vehicles owned
and operated by franchise companies , common carriers or agen-
cies of the state and federal governments .
CHAPTER 160
(Н. В. 154)
Approved March 4, 1952 .
AN ACT relating to service and sale of natural gas to owners of property
where gas wells or gas pipe lines are located.
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Every gas pipe line company in this state, obtaining gas
from wells located within this state, shall , upon the request of
the owner of the property where any gas well or gas pipe line is
located , furnish service to such owner at rates determined by the
Public Service Commission ; provided , that nothing contained
herein shall be construed as requiring any gas pipe line company
to serve any such owner of property from lines established on the
discharge side of any compressor station .
CHAPTER 161
(Н. В. 157)
AN ACT relating to children , and their care, custody , treatment and
disposition ; including provisions as to juvenile courts and as to
the jurisdiction and powers of other courts concerning children ,
and provisions for establishment of agencies and facilities of the state
for the administration of state functions regarding children .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Sec . 1. As used in this Act, unless the context otherwise
requires :
(1) "Child " means any person who has not reached his
eighteenth birthday.

CHAPTER 161 429
(2) " Court" means the juvenile session of the county court .
(3) "Youth Authority" means the agency of this state so
named .
Sec. 2. (1) Except as otherwise provided in this Act,
the county court of each county shall have exclusive jurisdiction
in proceedings concerning any child living or found within the
county :
(a) Who is neglected as to proper or necessary support or
education as required by law, or as to medical , psychiatric ,
psychological or other care necessary for his well-being , or who
is abandoned by his parent or other custodian ;
(b) Whose occupation , behavior , conditions , environment
or associations are such as to injure his welfare or that of others ;
(c) Who is beyond the control of his parent or other
custodian ; or
(d) Who is alleged to have violated or attempted to violate
any federal or state law, or municipal ordinance , regardless of
where the violation occurred .
