Section 2. ( 1 ) An excise tax of seven cents per gallon
shall be paid on the use ( as defined in section 1 ) of special
fuel , except that no tax shall apply to special fuel delivered into
the fuel tank of a vehicle operated by the government of the
United States .
( 2 ) The tax shall be computed and paid as provided by
sections 7 and 8 .
( 3 ) Two - sevenths of all amounts received by reason of
said tax shall be set aside by the Department of Highways for
the construction , reconstruction and maintenance of rural and
secondary roads and for no other purpose .
Section 3. ( 1 ) A license shall be required of each special
fuel dealer , interstate special fuel user, and special fuel user .
( 2) Application for a license shall be made to the Depart-
ment of Revenue upon forms prepared and furnished by the de-
partment . The application shall contain such information as the
department deems necessary .
( 3 ) Concurrently with the filing of an application for a
license , the special fuel dealer , special fuel user, or interstate
special fuel user shall file with the department a bond as re-
quired under section 4. No license to act as a special fuel
dealer , interstate special fuel user or a special fuel user shall
be issued to any person or continued in force unless such person
has furnished a bond as provided in section 4 to secure payment
of any and
allspecial fuel taxes

Section 8. ( 1 ) The special fuel excise tax imposed by
section 2 of this Act , except as otherwise provided under sub-
section 2 of this section , shall be computed by multiplying the
number of gallons of special fuel used by the tax rate.
(2) Interstate special fuel users shall pay the special fuel
excise tax on that proportion of total special fuel used in motor
vehicles operated on the public highways of this state as the
number of miles operated by these vehicles in this state bears
to the total number of miles operated .
Section 9. ( 1 ) If special fuel on which the tax has been
paid, pursuant to the provisions of subsection ( 1 ) of section 8
has been used by interstate users in motor vehicles operated on
the public highways of both this state and other states , the inter-
state user of such special fuel shall be credited to the extent of
the tax due under this section for the full amount of taxes paid
on special fuel purchased during the preceding month . The
department may defer any credits in excess of taxes paid or due
for a period not to exceed three months .
( 2 ) The Department of Revenue shall , at the close of
each quarterly period ending September 30, December 31 ,
March 31 and June 30, refund all deferred credits granted by
the department during the quarter which have not been credited
against tax paid purchases of fuel in this state .
(3 ) No tax shall be credited or refunded except that paid
upon special fuel used exclusively in motor vehicles operated
on the public highways of both this state and other states and
unless the applicant holds a currently valid credit and refund
permit and makes application as provided under section 11 .
(4) The right to receive any credit or refund under this
section shall not be assignable , except to the executor or admin-
istrator , or to the receiver , trustee in bankruptcy , or assignee in
insolvency proceedings of such person entitled thereto .
Section 10. ( 1 ) Interstate special fuel users who pur-
chase special fuel in this state shall secure from the Department
of Revenue a credit and refund permit , which permit shall
entitle such person to apply for a credit or refund under the
provisions of this Act .
(2 ) Each interstate special fuel user credit and refund
permit shall be valid until surrendered or cancelled . The depart

CHAPTER 191 559
ment may cancel , by giving thirty days notice any credit and
refund permit , when the holder thereof does not apply for a
credit or refund during any six months interval , unless the permit
holder can show cause satisfactory to the department why the
permit should not be cancelled and after notice of 10 days may
cancel for any sufficient cause within the opinion of the depart-
ment .
(3) To secure a credit and refund permit the applicant
shall file with the department an application under oath on
forms furnished by the department , setting forth such informa-
tion incident to crediting and refunding the tax on special fuel as
the department may require .
Section 11. ( 1 ) In order for a holder of a special fuel
credit and refund permit to be entitled to a credit or refund of
the special fuel tax as provided under this Act , he shall on or
before the last day of each month make application to the De-
partment of Revenue on forms and in the manner prescribed by
the department for a credit or refund of the tax paid , pursuant to
subsection ( 1 ) of section 8 on special fuel purchased in this state
and used during the next preceding month . The Department of
Revenue may require an applicant for a credit or refund to
supply with each application copies of special fuel dealer receipts

Circuit Court in the manner and subject to the conditions pro-

Section 12. ( 1 ) The Department of Revenue may , at any
time, audit the books and records of each holder of a special fuel
tax credit and refund permit who has at any time since the last
audit was made applied for a credit or refund thereunder and
make such other investigations as it deems necessary to determine
whether the credits or refunds applied for constitute proper
claims . When the department is satisfied that a credit or refund
is proper it shall credit the applicant for an amount correspond-
ing to the taxes paid pursuant to subsection ( 1 ) of section 8 or
make the refund .
However, the department shall not be required to audit the
books and records of a nonresident credit and refund permit
holder if the amount of credits or refunds for which application
has been made are not sufficient in amount reasonably to justify
probable expenses of making the audit .
(2 ) If special fuel taxes are erroneously credited or re-
funded , the department shall advise the payee by registered mail
of the erroneous credit or refund . If the payee fails to reimburse
the Commonwealth within fifteen days after the receipt of the
letter , an action may be instituted by the department in the
Franklin Circuit Court and the department shall recover from
the payee the amount of the erroneous credit or refund plus a
penalty of twenty per cent .
Section 13. ( 1 ) The reports required by section 7 shall
be accompanied by a certified or cashier's check , payable to the
State Treasurer , for the amount of tax due for the preceding
calendar month , computed as provided in section 8; except that
the department may waive this requirement and accept the check
of the special fuel dealer , special fuel user or interstate special
fuel user if the dealer , user or interstate user is of sound financial
condition and has established a good record of compliance with
the requirements of sections 1 to 19 inclusive .
Section 14. ( 1 ) If any special fuel dealer , special fuel
user or interstate special fuel user neglects or refuses to make
the return or pay the tax at the time provided in section 9, a
penalty of twenty per cent of the tax and interest at
sixper cent

CHAPTER 191 561
per annum from the date when due shall be paid on both the
tax and the penalty .
(2) If any dealer , user or interstate user subject to the
penalty provided in subsection ( 1 ) of this section submits in
writing to the department his reasons for failure to comply with
this Act , and if the department finds the reasons sufficient evi-
dence of justifiable cause for modifying the penalty provided in
subsection ( 1 ) of this section, it may modify the penalty exacted
therein to five per cent of the amount of the tax due and delin-
quent provided the five per cent penalty may be reduced to a one
per cent penalty if the violation is the first violation by the tax-
payer within the twelve months .
(3 ) If the penalties provided by this section are collected
by proceedings in court an additional penalty of twenty per cent
shall be collected and distributed as is authorized by KRS
134.400 and 135.060 .
(4) Whenever any dealer , user or interstate user neglects
or refuses to make and file any report for any calendar month as
required by section 7, or files an incorrect or fraudulent report ,
the Department of Revenue shall determine , after an investiga-
tion , the number of gallons of fuel with respect to which the
dealer , user or interstate user has incurred liability under sec-
tions 2 and 8 for any particular month , and assess and collect the
amount of tax and penalties due .
(5) Any person who fails to make any report or return
required under sections 1 to 19 inclusive within the time required
may be required to pay a penalty of ten dollars to be assessed
and collected in the manner provided for the assessment and
collection of taxes , or in a civil action , at the instance of the
department . In addition such delinquent may be compelled to
make the required report or return .
(6) In any action for the collection of the special fuel tax
and any penalties or interest imposed in connection therewith ,
an assessment by the department of the amount of the tax due
and the interest or penalties due to the state shall constitute
prima facie evidence of the claim of the state , and the burden
of proof shall be on the dealer , user or interstate user to show
the assessment was incorrect and contrary to law.

CHAPTER 192
(Н. В. 274)
AN ACT relating to the office of Receiver of the circuit court in a
county containing a population of 300,000 or over and providing for
the payment of such officer's compensation in case the fees of the
office are not sufficient to meet his salary .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :.
Section 1. If, in the office of Receiver of the circuit court in
a county containing a population of 300,000 or over , a shortage in
the salary of the Receiver shall occur or exist and the Receiver
does not have in his hands sufficient surplus fees or other funds
legally charged with the payment of his salary to make up the
shortage he shall thereupon , immediately after the last day of

CHAPTER 193
(Н. В. 275)
AN ACT relating to the excise tax on gasoline .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 138.210 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
As used in KRS 138.220 to 138.340 , unless the context re-
quires otherwise :
( 1 ) "Accountable loss " of gasoline means loss or destruc-
tion of " received " gasoline , through wrecking of transportation
conveyance , explosion , or fire, that has been reported to the
Department of Revenue in a manner and form prescribed by
the department , supported by positive and conclusive evidence ,
in the judgment of the department , of the alleged loss , and con-
firmed in writing to the reporting dealer by the department ,
after any investigation deemed necessary by the department to
establish the bona fide claim of the loss .
( 2 ) "Dealer ” means any person who imports or causes to
be imported into this state gasoline for storage , use , distribution ,
or sale and delivery in this state and after it has reached this
state ; also any person who produces , refines , manufactures , com-
pounds or stores gasoline in this state for use , distribution or
sale and delivery in this state; also any person who receives
gasoline by tank car, barge or pipe -line delivery from any point
within this state upon which gasoline the excise tax levied by
KRS 138.220 has not been paid ; also any person importing or
causing to be imported into this state gasoline in the fuel tank
or any auxiliary tank of his motor vehicle in a quantity in excess
of thirty gallons .
(3 ) "Department " means the Department of Revenue .
(4) "Gasoline " means all liquid fuels , including liquids
ordinarily , practically and commercially usable in internal com-
bustion engines for the generation of power , and all distillates of
and condensates from petroleum , natural gas , coal , coal tar ,
vegetable ferments and
allother products
sousable except
propane
, butane
orother liquefied petroleum gases
, kerosene
,
cleaner solvent
, fuel oil
,diesel fuel and crude oil
.

CHAPTER 193 565
(5) “Received ," as applied to gasoline , means when it
has been imported into this state by any person for use , distri-
bution , or delivery and sale and when it has been placed in tanks
or other containers for use or subject to withdrawal for use , dis-
tribution or sale and delivery . Gasoline produced , refined , manu-
factured or compounded at any refinery in this state by any
person shall be deemed to be received by such person when it
has been loaded into tank cars for delivery within this state or
placed in any tank from which sales or deliveries are made at
the refinery where it is produced , refined , manufactured or
compounded . Gasoline imported by any person into this state
and received through delivery by boat at a marine terminal for
storage , or delivered by pipe -line at a pipe -line terminal for
storage , shall be deemed to be received when it has been loaded
into tank cars for delivery within this state or placed in any
tank from which sales or deliveries are made at such marine or
pipe - line terminals . Dealers may sell gasoline to licensed bonded
dealers in this state in transport truckload , carload or cargo lots ,
withdrawing such gasoline from refinery , marine or pipe - line
terminal storage tanks, without paying the tax on such gasoline .
In such instances the licensed bonded dealer purchasing such
gasoline shall be deemed to have received such gasoline , and
shall assume responsibility to the state for the payment of the
tax thereon and shall report and pay the tax on such gasoline
purchased and received .
(6 ) "Refinery ” means any place where gasoline is refined ,
manufactured , compounded or otherwise prepared for use .

CHAPTER 193 567
(3 ) There shall be allowed a monthly deduction of that
number of gallons equal to three -fourths of one per cent of the
total number of gallons of gasoline stored in any marine terminal ,
refinery or pipeline terrninal , this deduction being allowed for
reporting , evaporation , shrinkage and unaccountable losses while
in storage . The remaining ninety -nine and one quarter per cent
of such gasoline placed in storage must be fully accounted for
as in physical inventory , accountable loss, withdrawn for export
or withdrawn from storage and received for taxable purposes .
Any gain by virtue of this allowance shall be a credit against
the number of gallons received and reported .
(4) The number of gallons of gasoline added to marine ,
pipeline or refinery storage shall be determined by the depart-
ment by actual measurement of terminal storage tanks in the
manner it deems necessary.
Section 3. Section 138.310 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No person shall receive , use, sell, transport , store
or distribute any gasoline upon which the tax has not been paid
or assumed or engage in the sale, storage or transportation of
any gasoline within this state upon which the tax has not been
paid unless he is the holder of an uncancelled license issued
by the Department of Revenue to engage in such business.
(2 ) Any licensed transporter other than a regularly
licensed gasoline dealer transporting gasoline upon which the
tax has not been paid or assumed by motor vehicle shall have
plainly painted thereon name, address , and permit number of
licensed transporter .
(3) Any person who engages in the business of receiving ,
using , selling , transporting , storing or distributing gasoline in
this state as a dealer , storage operator or transporter without
holding an uncancelled license to engage in such business, or
who, without such license , receives , uses , sells , transports , stores ,
or distributes any gasoline upon which the tax imposed by
KRS 138.220 has not been reported and paid , shall pay a penalty
equal to twenty per cent of the amount of the tax on such gaso-
line so received , used, sold , transported , stored or distributed .

(3) Any dealer or transporter who fails to make any report

shall not be required to file such return and pay such taxes ,

CHAPTER 198
(Н. В. 280 )
AN ACT relating to the use of professional titles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. ( 1) No person shall , in connection with the
practice of medicine , surgery , osteopathy , optometry , dentistry ,
chiropody , pharmacy , chiropractic , psychology or psychiatry ,
nursing , anestheiology, physio or physical therapy , or any other

orinfirmity

as
aDoctor

employ

inany manner the title
"Doctor
"
or
"Dr.
", unless
heactually has graduated and holds
adoctor degree from
a
school
, college
, university
orinstitution authorized by
itsgovern-
ing body
toconfer such degree
.
(2) No person who holds
adoctor degree
,
asprovided
in
subsection
(1)
ofthis section
, shall use
oremploy the title
"Doctor
"
or
"Dr.
"
in
orupon any letter
, statement
, card
, pre-
scription
, sign
, listing
orother writing
, without affixing suitable

CHAPTER 199 585
words or letters designating the particular doctor degree held
by such person .
Section 2. Nothing in this Act shall be construed as
authorizing the unlicensed practice of any profession or business
referred to in section 1. Nor shall anything in this Act be con-
strued as repealing or amending any law or laws relating to
licensure or regulation of the professions or businesses referred
to in Section 1 .
Section 3. (1) Each violation of subsection ( 1) of section
1 shall , for the first offense , be punishable by a fine of not less
than fifty dollars nor more than three hundred dollars or by
imprisonment for not more than thirty days or both, and , for
each subsequent offense, by a fine of not less than three hundred
dollars nor more than one thousand dollars or by imprisonment
for not more than one year , or both .
(2) Each violation of subsection (2) of section 1 shall ,
for the first offense, be punishable by a fine of not more than
one hundred dollars or by imprisonment for not more than thirty
days , or both , and, for each subsequent offense , by a fine of not
less than one hundred dollars nor more than two hundred and
fifty dollars or by imprisonment for not more than ninety days
or both .
(3) Each day of violation of either subsection of section
1 shall constitute a separate offense .
Approved March 12 , 1952 .

Sec. 2 Section 150.015 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
The declared purpose of this Act , and the policy of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky , is to protect and conserve the wild
life of this Commonwealth so as to insure a permanent and
continued supply of the wild life resources of this state for the
purpose of furnishing sport and recreation for the present and for
the future residents of this state; to promote the general wel

CHAPTER 200 589
fare of the Commonwealth ; to provide for the prudent taking
and disposition of wild life within reasonable limits , based upon
the adequacy of the supply thereof ; to protect the food supply of
this state , and to insure the continuation of an important part
of the Commonwealth of this state which depends upon the
existence of its wild life resources . It is further the declared
purpose of this act, and the policy of this Commonwealth , that
an adequate and flexible system be installed to accomplish the
aforesaid purposes . All of the provisions of this Act shall be
liberally construed in such manner as most effectually to carry
out
itspurposes and intent
.

of four years . All persons who shall be members of the Game
and Fish Commission upon the effective date of the 1952 Act by
which this section was amended shall continue to serve as mem-
bers of the Fish and Wild Life Resources Commission until such
time as their respective terms as members of the Game and
Fish Commission would normally have expired .
(3 ) Vacancies through the expiration of terms of the
members of the Commission shall be filled by appointment by
the Governor from a list of five names from each Wild Life Dis-
trict , recommended and submitted by the Sportsmen of each
respective district . When the term of a member expires, the
Commissioner shall call a meeting of the Sportsmen in that dis-
trict not later than thirty days prior to the expiration of such
member's term. Notice of the meeting shall be given by pub-
lication at least two times in a newspaper having state -wide cir-
culation , at least thirty days prior to the date set for the meeting .
At such meeting , the Sportsmen in attendance shall select and
submit to the Governor a list of five residents and citizens of the
district who are well informed on the subject of wild life con-
servation and restoration . The Governor shall appoint a suc-
cessor to the member whose term is about to expire within sixty
days following the submission to him of the list hereinabove re-
ferred to , and in no event later than August 13th .
(4) In the event of vacancies other than by expiration , the
Governor shall fill the vacancy for the unexpired part of the
term from the names remaining on the list previously submitted
for the district from which the vacancy arose .

stitutional oath of office .

is amended to read as follows :

Sec. 23 Section 150.160 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1) Any fine imposed for the violation of this chapter
shall , when collected , be divided as follows : Forty Percent shall
be given to the County Attorney or Commonwealth Attorney
who is present and prosecutes the case and the remaining sixty
percent to the Commissioner , to be paid into the Game and
Fish Fund . If no county or commonwealth attorney actually
prosecutes , the entire fine shall be paid into the Game and Fish
Fund .
(2 ) In the event that an appeal is taken to the circuit
court from any conviction imposed pursuant to the provisions of
this chapter , and in the further event that the same officer who
prosecuted the violation to a successful conclusion in the lower
court is not charged with and does not handle the continued
prosecution of the same in the circuit court , then forty percent
of such fine as may be finally imposed in the circuit court shall ,
when collected , be divided equally among all of the officers
actively participating in the prosecution of said case in both the
lower and circuit courts .
( 3 ) Every judgment entered upon the trial of any viola-
tion of this chapter shall recite whether or not the county at-
torney or the commonwealth attorney , or both , was present and
actively prosecuted the action on behalf of the Commonwealth .
Unless there is such an entry on the judgment book of the court
showing the presence and participation of such officer or officers
in the trial, none shall be entitled to any part of the fine.

Sec. 24 Section 150.170 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) Except as provided in sub-sections (2), (3), (4), (5)
and ( 6) of this section and subject to regulations adopted under
this chapter , no person shall do any act authorized by any kind
of license or permit provided for in this chapter with respect to
game and fish unless he holds the kind of license or permit that
authorizes such act . It is the specific purpose of this chapter
to prohibit the taking or pursuing of any wild life or wild ani-
mals, protected or unprotected , or the angling in any stream or
body of water , whether public or private , without first pro-
curing the license provided for herein , except to such extent as
may be otherwise provided in this section .
(2 ) A person under sixteen years of age may , without a
trapping license , take wild animals by trapping and , without a
fishing license , take fish by angling , or take minnows by the use
of a minnow seine.
(3) Resident landowners , and resident children and les-
sees on their lands may , without a fishing license , take fish from
the waters thereon by angling , such angling , in the case of
public waters , to be from the bank only , and such persons may ,
without a hunting license, take wild animals and wild birds
therefrom ( except by trapping ) , subject to the further provisions
of this chapter .
(4) Residents or non-residents attending and participating
in any annual state or national field trial may so attend and par-
ticipate without obtaining a hunting license.
(5) Any resident of this state who is sixty-five years of
age or older may, without a hunting or fishing license , do any act
authorized by such a license , but while so doing he must carry
upon his person an affidavit stating the year and place of his
birth .
(6 ) The provisions of this chapter for the protection of
wild animals shall not apply to any resident landowner of this
state who kills on his land any wild animal that is causing dam-
age to such land or to any personal property situated thereon .
(7) In the event a reciprocal agreement is entered into
between the Commissioner of the Department of Fish and Wild

CHAPTER 200 603
Life Resources of this state , with the approval of the Commis-
sion, and the similar authority , of the state of Tennessee , pur-
suant to which persons holding a fishing license ( Resident or
Non -Resident ) or a state -wide hunting license (Resident or Non-
Resident ) issued in this state are permitted to perform the acts
authorized by such license upon so much of the waters of any
lake , extending across the boundary between Kentucky and
Tennessee , as lies within the State of Tennessee, then any person
holding a similar fishing license or hunting license issued in the
State of Tennessee may , during the duration of such agreement,
perform the acts authorized by such license upon so much of
the waters of such lake as lies within this state , without having
obtained a license therefor in this state .

Sec . 39 Section 150.280 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
No person shall propagate fish , frogs , fur bearing animals
and other game, nor harbor or maintain any protected game or
fish in captivity for the purpose of making a pet of the same , or
for any other purpose , without first procuring a permit to do so
from the Department . The Commissioner shall make and pub-
lish regulations governing all such activity . The application
for a breeder's permit , or for a permit to harbor and maintain
protected game and fish as pets or for any other purpose , shall
be in writing, addressed to the Commissioner , signed by the
applicant , and shall describe the land or waters owned or leased
by the breeder to be used for breeding purposes and shall con-
tain such other facts as may be required by the Commissioner .
When it appears that the application is made in good faith , the
Commissioner may issue a permit , which shall continue in force
for one year unless sooner revoked by the Commissioner for
cause, upon payment of a fee of $2.00 and fees for tagging which
shall be fixed by the Commissioner . A fish and game club which
enters into an agreement with the Department to operate such
a farm , in cooperation with the Department , for the purpose of
carrying out the Department's program may , with the approval
of the Commissioner , be issued a permit without the payment
of any fee. Any permit issued pursuant to this section may be
revoked by the Commissioner at any time for a violation by the
holder thereof of any of the laws of Kentucky or of any regula-
tion of the Department .
Sec. 40 Section 150.290 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) Any breeder may sell and transport fish, frogs , fur
bearing animals and other game, alive for propagation , and
minnows for bait , and for food purposes during seasons pre-
scribed by the Commissioner . Such fish, frogs , fur-bearing ani-
mals and other game shall be identified either by marking their
container or by individual tagging , as the Commissioner may
prescribe .

Sec. 41 Section 150.300 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No person shall , without first securing the permission
of the Commissioner , enter upon the premises of a propagation
farm , game farm or game refuge or fish hatchery and take fish,
birds , wild animals or game; or, without the consent of the
property owner , enter upon any pond and take fish or am-
phibians or foul the waters of any pond or farm with a substance
injurious to the life or growth of fish or amphibians , or break or
destroy any dam , reservoir or embankment or divert the waters
or otherwise wilfully damage the farm or pond . No person ,
except bona fide employees in line of duty, shall go upon the
premises of any propagation farm , state game farm , or game
refuge or fish hatchery accompanied by a dog of any type or
description or while carrying firearms upon or about his person .
(2) It is unlawful for any person to mar, deface , destroy
or in any wise damage , or attempt to mar , deface , destroy or
damage any sign or posted notice upon any game farm , game or
wild life refuge or fish hatchery in the state .

ofthe Kentucky Revised Statutes

:
(1) No person shall take any wild birds except during the
open season prescribed by the Department for the particular
species
. The open season for quail may
bebetween November

1, and February 1st only, and shall not exceed a total of sixty

Sec. 46 Section 150.340 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
No person shall take more wild birds in any one day than
the bag limit prescribed for the species , but the Department
shall not prescribe bag limits in excess of the following :
Quail 10
Ruffed Grouse or Native Pheasant 2
Doves Federal Regulations Apply
Ducks Federal Regulations Apply
Geese Federal Regulations Apply
Coots and Gallinule Federal Regulations Apply
Woodcock Federal Regulations Apply
Snipe ----------- Federal Regulations Apply
No person shall have in his possession at any time any num-
ber of wild birds in excess of the bag limit as prescribed by the

Department for the particular species , except that a person who
has hunted two or more days in succession may have in his

one day for such birds , and a person may have in his posses-
sion , in cold storage locker as provided in KRS 150.305 a num-
ber not exceeding two days' bag limit after the first day of the
hunting season ; provided , however , that no person shall take
more than ten ruffed grouse or native pheasant during any open
season for the same .
Sec. 47 Section 150.355 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
No person shall use ferrets in hunting . No person shall
keep a ferret as a pet or for any purpose , unless he has procured a
ferret permit from the Commissioner . A permit must be obtained
within ten days after the person acquires possession of a ferret
and one permit shall entitle the holder to keep any number of
ferrets . If the owner of any ferret uses it contrary to the pro-
visions of this chapter , the Commissioner may revoke his permit
and confiscate all of his ferrets . Each use of a ferret in hunting
shall constitute a separate offense .
Sec. 48 Section 150.360 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1 ) No person shall take any wild animals , whether pro-
tected by this chapter or not, except by trapping , gig , bow and
arrow , gun , gun and dog , or dog .
(2) No shotgun used in the taking of wild animals , pro-
tected or unprotected , shall be larger than a 10 gauge and
fired from the shoulder . No wild animals, protected or un-
protected , shall be taken with or by means of any automatic-
loading or hand -operated repeating shotgun capable of holding
more than three shells , the magazine of which has not been cut
off or plugged with a one-piece metal or wooden filler incap-
able of removal through the loading end thereof , in such man-
ner as to reduce the capacity of said gun to not more than three
shells at one time in the magazine and chamber combined .
(3 ) No person shall hunt wild life ( except opossum , and
raccoon , and frogs ) with lights or other means designed to blind
wild life at night .

CHAPTER 200 621
(4) No person shall take frogs with a gig other than one of
the following sizes and dimensions : The gig shall have not more
than four prongs ; no prong shall be longer than two and one-
half inches ; the prongs shall be made out of not heavier than
14-gauge wire or metal , and shall not have more than one barb
on each prong.
Sec. 49 Section 150.365 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
No person shall use fire , or any type of explosive , smoke or
any noxious gas or substance to molest or destroy the den or
hole of any animal , nor shall any person burn a field for the pur-
pose of driving game .
Sec. 50 Section 150.370 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
( 1 ) No person shall take any wild animal protected by
this chapter except during the open season for the particular
species as prescribed by the Department . The open season for
rabbits may be between November 1st and February 1st only , but
shall not exceed a total of sixty days, consecutive or otherwise .
The open season for mink , opossum, raccoon , skunk , muskrat and
red fox may be between November 1st and February 15th only ,
but shall not exceed a total of sixty days , consecutive or other-
wise , except that the Department may allow an additional thirty
days in which opossum and raccoon may be hunted and taken

seling license is issued the holder shall make a written report to
the Department of Fish and Wild Life Resources , stating the
total weight of mussel shells taken under the license , the names
and locations of the waters from which the mussels were taken
and the amount received for shells sold . Upon the failure of any

another license until such report is made .
Sec. 69 Section 150.520 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
The Department shall make such requirements and regula-
tions governing the operations of boats in the taking of mussels
as it may deem best for the proper enforcement of this chapter .

630 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
To prevent depletion of the mussel beds and to insure proper
propagation of the mussels , the Department may close any beds
to operators at any time and for such length of time as it deems
necessary. During such closed season on any bed no one shall
take mussels therefrom . When an order is issued closing any
mussel bed , due notice of the same shall be published in at
least three consecutive issues of a newspaper of general circula-
tion in the county where the bed is located .
Sec. 70 Section 150.525 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes

partment , either directly or through such county court clerks as

vice in his possession for sale to nonresidents outside of this
state.

under , for which no definite fine or imprisonment is fixed by this

CHAPTER 202

AN ACT amending Section 146.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes ,
relating to the Soil and Water Resources Commission .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 146.090 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) The Soil and Water Resources Commission shall consist
of nine members, not more than five of whom shall be of the
same political party, to be appointed by the Commissioner of
Conservation from districts which shall be defined by the Com-
missioner of Conservation with the approval of the Governor .
(2) One member shall be appointed from each of the dis-
tricts from a list of two names submitted from each such district
by the supervisors of the soil conservation districts that have
their principal offices therein . All members shall be supervisors
of soil conservation districts .
(3) The term of office of each such member shall be four
years except that as to the members first appointed the Com-
missioner of Conservation shall designate three members for
terms of two years , three for three years , and three for four
years; provided that , whenever a member of the commission
ceases to hold the office of district supervisor by virtue of which
he is serving on the commission , his term of office as a member
of the commission shall be terminated , in which case, and in
the case of any other vacancy other than one caused by the ex-
piration of a term , a successor shall be appointed by the Com-
missioner of Conservation to serve the remainder of the unexpired
term .
(4) The members of the commission shall designate a chair-
man from among their members and may from time to time
change such designation . The commission may employ such
other officers , employes, and agents as it deems necessary and
shall provide for surety bonds for members, the director , officers ,
employes or agents if entrusted with funds or property , and it
shall keep a record of
itsofficial actions
.
Amajority
ofthe
commission shall constitute
aquorum
. The commission may

may require . It may delegate to

, any
of
itsmem-
bers
, the director

,

ofthe

, but
shall
beentitled

, including traveling expenses
, nec-
essarily incurred
indischarging their duties
.

.

CHAPTER 205
(Η. Β. 308)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Shively , in Jefferson
County , Kentucky .
WHEREAS , satisfactory evidence has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Shively , in Jefferson
County , is such as to justify its being assigned to the fourth class
of cities ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The City of Shively , in Jefferson County , Kentucky , is
transferred and changed from the fifth class to the fourth
class of cities, and is hereby assigned to the cities of the
fourth class .
CHAPTER 206
(Н. В. 312)
AN ACT relating to traffic regulations and equipment of vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1 .
(1) The Commissioner of Highways may restrict or
regulate traffic and may install and maintain traf-
fic control devices upon State -maintained high-
ways in such manner as is reasonably necessary
to promote the safety and convenience of the
traveling public ; provided , however , that this Sec-
tion shall in no way affect the provisions of Section
177.046 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes .
(2) The driver of any vehicle shall obey the instruc-
tions of any official traffic control device applicable
thereto unless otherwise directed by traffic or
police officer , subject to the exceptions granted
the driver of an authorized emergency vehicle .
(3 ) Any person who violates Sub -section 2 of this
Section shall be fined not less than ten dollars
($10.00 ) nor more than one hundred dollars
($100.00 ) for each offense .

CHAPTER 206 639
Section 2. Section 189.340 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) Vehicles overtaking other vehicles proceeding in
the same direction shall pass to the left of them
and shall not again drive to the right until reason-
ably clear of those vehicles . The person operating
or in charge of the overtaking vehicle shall sound
his horn or other sound device before passing . Ve-
hicles overtaking street cars may pass either to
the right or left when so directed by a police of-
ficer, when on a one -way street or where the lo-
cation of the tracks prevents compliance with this
Section , with regard for other traffic .
(2) The operator of a vehicle may overtake and pass
upon the right of another vehicle which is making
or is about to make a left turn . The operator of a
vehicle may overtake, and, allowing sufficient
clearance , pass another vehicle proceeding in the
same direction upon the left or upon the right on
a roadway with unobstructed pavement or suffi-
cient width for four or more lines of traffic when
the movement can be made in safety . No person
shall drive off the pavement or upon the shoulder
of the roadway in overtaking or passing on the
right .
(3) No vehicle shall be driven to the left side of the
center of the roadway in overtaking and passing
another vehicle proceeding in the same direction
unless the left side is clearly visible and free of on-
coming traffic for a sufficient distance ahead to
permit overtaking and passing to be completely
made without interfering with the safe operation
of any vehicle approaching from the opposite
direction or any vehicle overtaken . In every event
the overtaking vehicle must return to the right-
hand side of the roadway before coming within

640 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
one hundred feet of any vehicle approaching from
the opposite direction .
(4) No vehicle shall at any time be driven to the left
side of the roadway under the following con-
ditions :
(a) When approaching the crest of a grade or a
curve in the highway where the driver's view
along the highway is obstructed .
(b) When approaching within one hundred feet
of any bridge , viaduct or tunnel , or when
coming within one hundred feet of or tra-
versing any intersection or railroad grade
crossing .
The provisions of this Sub -section shall not apply
upon a one -way roadway .
(5) The Commissioner of Highways is hereby author-
ized to determine those portions of any highway
where overtaking and passing or driving to the
left of the roadway would be especially hazardous
and may by appropriate signs or markings on the
roadway indicate the beginning and end of such
zones , and when such signs or markings are in
place and clearly visible to an ordinarily ob-
servant person, every driver of a vehicle shall
obey the directions thereof .
(6) Whenever any roadway has been divided into
three or more clearly marked lanes for traffic the
following additional rules shall apply :
(a) A vehicle shall be driven as nearly as may be
practical entirely within a single lane and
shall not be moved from that lane until the
driver has first ascertained that the move-
ment can be made with safety .
(b) A vehicle shall not be driven in the center
lane except when overtaking and passing
another vehicle where the roadway is clearly
visible and the center lane is clear of traffic
within a safe distance , or in preparation for a

CHAPTER 206 641
left turn or where a center lane is at the time
allocated exclusively to traffic moving in the
direction in which the vehicle is proceeding
and is signposted to give notice of the alloca-
tion.
(c) Official signs may be erected directing slow-
moving traffic to use a designated lane or al-
locating specified lanes to traffic moving in
the same direction and operators of vehicles
shall obey the directions of such signs .
(7) (a) The operator of a motor vehicle shall not fol-
low another vehicle more closely than is rea-
sonable and prudent , having regard for the
speed of the vehicle and the traffic upon and
condition of the highway .
(b) The operator of any motor truck , semi -trailer
truck , bus or heavy construction equipment
unit when traveling upon a highway out-
side of a business or residential district shall
not follow within 250 feet of another such ve-
hicle or equipment unit. This Sub -section
shall not prevent overtaking and passing , nor
shall it apply to any lane specially designated
for use of motor trucks or semi -trailer trucks ,
busses or heavy construction equipment
units.
Section 3. Section 189.450 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) No person shall stop a vehicle , leave it standing,
or cause or permit it to stop or to be left standing
upon the main traveled portion of a highway ;
provided , however , that this Section shall not be
construed to prohibit parking off the main traveled
portion of a highway or street in a city or suburban
area where such parking is otherwise permitted .
This Sub -Section shall not apply to a vehicle that
has been disabled while on the main traveled por-
1

642 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
tion of such a highway in such a manner and to
such extent that it is impossible to avoid the oc-
cupation of the main traveled portion or imprac-
ticable to remove it from the highway until repairs
have been made or sufficient help obtained for its
removal and shall not apply to wreckers at the
scene of accidents or emergency vehicles .
(2) Whenever any police officer finds a vehicle stand-
ing upon such a highway in violation of this Sec-
tion , he may move the vehicle, or require the
operator or other person in charge of the vehicle
to move it, to a position off the paved or improved
or main traveled part of the highway .
(3 ) Whenever any police officer finds a vehicle unat-
tended upon any bridge or causeway or in a tunnel
where the vehicle constitutes an obstruction to
traffic , the officer may provide for the removal of
the vehicle to the nearest garage or other place of
safety .
(4) No person shall stop or park a vehicle , except
when necessary to avoid conflict with other traffic
or in compliance with the directions of a police
officer or traffic control device , in the following
places :
(a) On a sidewalk ;
(b) In front of a public or private driveway ;
(c) Within an intersection ;
(d) At any place where official signs prohibit
stopping ; or
( e ) Within thirty feet upon the approach to any
flashing beacon , stop sign or traffic control
signal located at the side of a roadway .
(5) No person shall move a vehicle not lawfully under
his control into any such prohibited area .
Approved March 18, 1952 .

CHAPTER 207
(Н. В. 318)
AN ACT relating to the reporting of contributions and expenditures in
connection with elections .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Those officers with whom statements of election contribu-
tions and expenditures are required to be filed under the pro-
visions of KRS 123.060 , 123.070 and 123.080 shall , at least ten
days before such statements are due , forward , by registered
mail, to every candidate and committee required to file such
statements the necessary statement forms and notice of the due
date.
Approved March 25, 1952 .
CHAPTER 208
(Н. В. 326)
AN ACT relating to eminent domain .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1) In all actions for the condemnation of lands under
the provisions of KRS 416.230 to 416.300 there shall be allowed to
the landowners as damages such a sum as will fairly represent
the reduction in the market value of the entire tract of land
through which such pipe line is to be constructed , which reduc-
tion shall not be limited by the value per acre of the land actually
embraced in the easement .
(2) Where the pipe line is reasonably accessible over the
easement sought to be condemned , ingress and egress to and
from the pipe line and appurtenances shall be limited to the
easement itself .
Approved March 18, 1952 .

CHAPTER 211

Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
SECTION 1. Any department of public welfare established
under the provisions of KRS 83.160 and KRS 98.010 may recover
from any person who receives general assistance in the form of
money payments an amount equal to the sum of such money
payments , and such department may recover an amount of money
equal to the value of any assistance in kind or service to or for
the benefit of any person only in the following circumstances :
if the recipient though destitute at the time he receives assis-
tance subsequently receives from other than public sources
income or other support in sufficient amount to reimburse the de-
partment , partially or wholly. Nothing in this Section shall be
construed to authorize the Department to make or cause to be

be filed , recorded and indexed but shall index said lien only in
the name of the recipient . The lien shall be designated "City's
Lien".
The clerk shall be entitled to a fee of $ 1.00 for filing and
indexing the lien . The department of public welfare of the
city shall pay the fee but the fee shall become a part of the lien
as an added cost to the recipient to be recovered at the time a
lien is satisified.
The lien shall not be enforceable while the real estate
is occupied by the surviving spouse or until she remarries , or
is occupied by a dependent child, provided , no other action is
brought to settle the estate .
In any case in which it appears that it would be to the best

proceeds in other real estate , the department of public welfare
of the city may grant permission and waive the lien to the extent
necessary for the purpose of effecting the transfer but such lien
shall attach to the reinvested property .
Any claim under the Act may be precipitated and the lien
provided by this Act may be enforceable during the lifetime of
any person who has received general assistance in order to re-
cover any amount obtained as a result of such person knowingly
making a false statement or representation or knowingly failing
to disclose a fact to procure , increase , or continue any material
benefit for himself .
SECTION 4. If any inmate or patient of any institution
operated under the provisions of KRS 83.160 , except the city
workhouse , who is eligible for admission to said institution under
the ordinances of the city and rules and regulations of the
city's department of public welfare is financially able to pay a
portion of the cost of his care, the city's department of public
welfare shall have the authority to receive such payment as
he is able to make .
Approved March 20, 1952 .

648 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 212
(Н. В. 350)
AN ACT relating to motor transportation and motor vehicles .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. A new section of Chapter 281 of the Kentucky
Revised Statutes is hereby enacted to read as follows :
Every holder of a certificate of public convenience and
necessity issued by the Department of Motor Transportation
authorizing the transportation of persons or property for hire,
which certificate authorizes service at a point that is also a point
at which service is authorized under the terms of any other
certificate of the same type held by the same person, is authorized
to transport persons or property , as the case may be, for hire ,
from any point on either of said certificated routes to any point
on any other such connecting certificated route or routes , un-
less such service be restricted by the terms of any of said certifi-
cates.
Section 2. It is the purpose of this Act to further clarify
the provisions of Chapter 281 relating to the application of the
administrative and judicial interpretations of the Federal Motor
Carrier Act to the administration of Chapter 281 of the Ken-
tucky Revised Statutes .
Approved March 24, 1952 .

(7) Seventh District: Elliott , Morgan , Wolfe, Breathitt , Perry ,

Section 2. Notwithstanding the provisions of Section 1

alaw without the approval

CHAPTER 222 657
CHAPTER 221
(Н. В. 403)
AN ACT relating to depositories of state funds and moneys ; amending
KRS 41.240 .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 41.240 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is amended
to read as follows :
(1) Before any bank shall be named as a state depository
to receive public funds , it shall pledge to the State Treasurer , as
collateral , securities having a face value at least 10 percent in
excess of the deposits and having a current quoted market value
at least equal to the deposits . The State Treasurer and the
Governor shall from time to time call for additional collateral
to adequately secure the deposits as current market values may
require .
(2) Only the following securities shall be eligible to be
pledged as collateral under this section: State warrants ; state
bonds , including bridge revenue bonds ; county bonds as author-
ized by KRS 41.245; housing commission obligations as author-
ized by KRS 386.040 and 386.050 ; and direct obligation bonds
of the United States Government .
Approved March 18, 1952 .
CHAPTER 222
(Н. В. 415)
AN ACT relating to banking .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. As used in this Act , unless the context requires
otherwise :
( 1 ) “Commissioner " means Commissioner of Banking .
( 2 ) "State bank ” means a bank or trust company organized
under the provisions of Chapter 287 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes .
(3) "National banking association" means one or more
national banking associations .

658 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
Section 2.
( 1 ) A national banking association may convert into or
merge with a State bank under a State charter , provided that :
( a) The action taken complies with Federal law ;
(b) In the case of a merger, the institutions to be merged
are located in the same city or county .
(2) In the case of each conversion , a written plan of con-
version shall be submitted , in duplicate , to the Commissioner .
Such plan shall be in form satisfactory to the Commissioner , shall
prescribe the terms and conditions of the conversion and the
mode of carrying it into effect , and shall have annexed thereto
and forming a part thereof the proposed articles of incorpora-
tion of the State bank which is to result from the conversion .
Such articles of incorporation shall be in the form prescribed by
law for the organization of State banks , with such variations , if
any, as shall be satisfactory to the Commissioner . With such
plan of conversion there shall be submitted , in duplicate , to the
Commissioner a certificate of the president , secretary or cashier
of the national banking association certifying that all steps have
have been taken which are necessary under Federal law to the
consummation of the conversion . The Commissioner shall ap-
prove or disapprove such plan of conversion within sixty days
of the submission thereof to him . If the Commissioner shall
approve such plan, he shall file one duplicate thereof , together
with one duplicate of such certificate submitted therewith and
the original of the approval of the Commissioner , in the office
of the Commissioner , and the other duplicate of such plan, to-
gether with a duplicate of such certificate and a duplicate of the
Commissioner's approval , shall be filed in the office of the Clerk
of the County in which the principal office of the State bank is
to be located . After such filing in the office of the Commis-
sioner, the conversion shall become effective upon the the filing
and recording of the articles of incorporation as provided in
Section 287.050 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes , unless a later
date is specified in the plan, in which event the conversion shall
become effective upon such later date .
(3) In the case of each merger , a written plan of merger
shall be submitted , in duplicate , to the Commissioner . Such plan
shall be in form satisfactory to the Commissioner and shall

CHAPTER 222 659
prescribe the terms and conditions of the merger and the mode
of carrying it into effect . Such plan may provide the name to
be borne by the State bank , as receiving corporation , if such
name is to be changed. Such plan may also name the persons
who shall constitute the first board of directors of the State bank
after the merger shall have been accomplished , provided that the
number and qualifications of such persons shall be in accordance
with the provisions of Chapter 287 of the Kentucky Revised
Statutes relating to the number and qualifications of directors
of a State bank ; or such plan may provide for a meeting of the
stockholders to elect a board of directors within sixty days after
such merger , and may make provision for conducting the affairs
of the State bank meanwhile . With such plan of merger there
shall be submitted , in duplicate , to the Commissioner the fol-
lowing:
( a ) By the national banking association , a certificate of
the president , secretary or cashier of such association certifying

(b) By the State bank , a certificate of the president , secre-

CHAPTER 224 663
CHAPTER 224
(Н. В. 431)
AN ACT relating to fiscal courts .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. Section 67.040 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes
is amended to read as follows :
(1) The fiscal court of each county shall consist of the
county judge , or in his absence the county judge pro tem , and
the justices of the peace of the county ; or of the county judge ,
or in his absence the county judge pro tem, and three county
commissioners elected under KRS 67.050 and 67.060 .
(2) The county judge or in his absence the county judge
pro tem shall preside , if present . If neither the county judge
nor the county judge pro tem is present or can preside , a majority
of the justices of the peace of commissioners shall elect one of
their number to preside during the absence or inability of the
county judge or county judge pro tem to preside . The county
judge , or the county judge pro tem or justice presiding in his
place , shall preserve order , and may fine and imprison for con-
tempt of court in the same manner as the presiding judge of
the county court .
(3) Not less than a majority of the members of the fiscal
court shall constitute a quorum for the transaction of business .
No proposition shall be adopted except with the concurrence of
at least a majority of the members present .
(4) When there is a tie vote in the fiscal court in the
selection of any officer or employe to be selected by the fiscal
court , and a deadlock results and continues for fifteen days or
longer , the county judge shall cause to be entered upon the
minutes of the fiscal court an order reciting the facts as to the
deadlock , and the question upon which it has occurred and
exists , and thereupon , the county judge shall make such appoint-
ment .
Section 2. WHEREAS , there now exists or may presently
exist in numerous fiscal courts composed of justices of the peace

664 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
a deadlock over the appointment of certain officers and employes
required for the proper and continued operation of county affairs ,
and
WHEREAS , the continued existence of such a deadlock
among the members of the fiscal court may cause irreparable
harm with respect to the operation of county affairs ,
NOW , THEREFORE , an emergency is declared to exist
and this act shall be in full force and effect immediately upon
itspassage and approval
bythe Governor
.
Approved March 20
, 1952
.

CHAPTER 227 665
CHAPTER 227
(Н. В. 451)
AN ACT concerning the elimination of grade crossings in cities of the
first class and the issuance of bonds to provide for the payment of
the cities ' share of the expense thereof and amending Section 93.330
Kentucky Revised Statutes .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 93.330 of the Kentucky Revised Statutes is
amended to read as follows :
(1) To provide money so that the city may bear its
proportion of the expense of the elimination of grade cross-
ings of public streets with tracks of steam or electric rail-
roads , the city legislative body may adopt an ordinance sub-
mitting to the voters of the city at any regular election the
question whether bonds of the city shall be issued for that
purpose .
(2) The ordinance shall provide the date and maturity
of the bonds, the rate of interest, and the total amount to be
then issued . The ordinance shall also contain the necessary
details in reference to the execution and delivery of the
bonds , their denominations , coupons to be attached , and tax
to be levied to pay the interest and create a sinking fund to
retire bonds at maturity .
(3 ) The ordinance may be adopted by the city legisla-
tive body either prior or subsequent to the adoption of the
plan to be used in the elimination of the grade crossings .
(4) The proceeds of bonds issued under this section
shall be used exclusively for the city's proportionate share
of the expense of the elimination of grade crossings of public
streets with the tracks of steam or electric railroads , as
provided in KRS 93.300 .
Approved March 24, 1952 .

666 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 228
(Н. В. 452)
AN ACT concerning the definition , apportionment , payment and records
of costs of grade crossing elimination projects in cities of the first
class .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Subsection ( 1) of Section 93.300 of the Kentucky Re-
vised Statutes is amended to read as follows :
(1) The cost of all work incident to or occasioned by
the elimination of the grade crossing and the construction of
a substituted crossing in any city of the first class , including
the costs of preparing the plans and specifications and
supervising the improvement , the acquisition of necessary
property , the construction of approaches , drainage struc-
tures, street and pavements , accommodations for street rail-
way and interurban railway companies and other public
utilities , and damages paid to abutting property owners ,
shall be paid in the proportion of fifteen percent by the
railroad companies affected and eighty-five percent by the
city . Provided , however , that the proceeds of any municipal
bond issue , heretofore voted under the provisions of KRS
93.330 , can be used to defray the proportionate costs of the
city only as such costs are defined in Chapter 153 Acts 1950 .
Approved March 24, 1952 .
CHAPTER 229
(Н. В. 461)
AN ACT changing the classification of the City of Eminence , in Henry
County.
WHEREAS , satisfactory information has been presented to the General
Assembly that the population of the City of Eminence , in Henry
County , is such as to justify its being assigned to the fourth class of
cities;
NOW THEREFORE ,
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the City of Eminence , in Henry County , is changed
from the fifth class to the fourth class of cities .

CHAPTER 230
(S. R. 7)
A RESOLUTION concerning the naming of the body of water im-
pounded by Wolf Creek Dam .
WHEREAS , historically and continuing , people living and doing business
along and adjacent to the reservoir created in the Commonwealth
of Kentucky by Wolf Creek Dam have been and are overwhelming
in favor of naming that body of water " Lake Cumberland " ; and
WHEREAS , it is universally recognized , as recently expressed by
Colonel Henry Walsh , U. S. A. District Engineer at Nashville , that
"The renaming of Wolf Creek Reservoir as 'Lake Cumberland ' by
the people of Kentucky is understandable and commendable ---- .
The Corps of Engineers feel that it is the prerogative of the people
to name the lakes as they see fit ; " and
WHEREAS , the construction of Wolf Creek Dam and the reservoir
has been completed and the lake has been filled with water for
some months ; the area is being developed and patronized as a great
recreation area , especially for fishing and other water sports ; the
Dam has been officially dedicated to public use , removing the last
remaining objection to officially changing the name to " Lake
Cumberland " ; and
WHEREAS , the name "Lake Cumberland " has already become firmly
entrenched in the minds of the people and is in common usuage , and
that the use of any other name in any quarter can only cause con-
fusion and hinder the normal development of the section as a
recreation area,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the Senate of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky , The House of Representatives Concurring Therein :
( 1 ) That the Congress of the United States of America be
requested to designate , as the official name of the body of water
impounded by Wolf Creek Dam , “Lake Cumberland ," and that
the name of the Dam remain "Wolf Creek Dam " ; and

672 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
:
CHAPTER 235
(H. R. 9)
A JOINT RESOLUTION memorializing Congress to continue to main-
tain and operate certain improvements in the Kentucky River .
WHEREAS , the United States Corps of Engineers has given notice that
Locks and Dams 8 through 14 on the Kentucky River will no
longer be operated and maintained by that Division after June 30 ,
1952, unless the reported prospective benefits from the use of those
navigation structures by commerce and navigation are reasonably
assured and sufficient to justify the cost of operation and main-
tenance thereof ; and
WHEREAS , the Kentucky River Collieries Company has leased 5,000
acres of coal land, made contracts for the mining thereof , started
construction work on a barge loading tipple at Beattyville , Ken-
tucky , and executed contracts for barge service, all of which
represents an investment of more than $ 500,000 ; and
WHEREAS , the Falls City Boat Works of Louisville, Kentucky , has
purchased two tugs and ordered six special barges to fulfill its
obligations under the contract with Kentucky River Collieries
Company , representing an investment of approximately $70,000 ; and
WHEREAS , numerous other commercial enterprises are in the planning
stages all of which , together with the indicated operations of the
Kentucky River Collieries Company and the Falls City Boat Works ,
depend almost completely upon the continued maintenance and
operation of Locks and Dams 8 through 14 on the Kentucky River;
and
WHEREAS , the abandonment of the commercial enterprises set out
herein would have a most depressing effect upon the present and
contemplated economy of the Upper Kentucky River Valley,
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That the Congress of the United States is hereby requested
to continue the maintenance and operation of Locks and Dams
Nos . 8 though 14 on the Kentucky River, and in the event that
the Jessamine Creek Dam Project is undertaken , that the plans
therefor provide for locking facilities .
That the Clerk of the House of Representatives is directed
to forward copies of this Resolution to each member of Congress
from Kentucky and to the Clerks of the Senate and House of
Representatives of the Congress of the United States .
Approved March 4, 1952

CHAPTER 236 673
CHAPTER 236
(H. R. 10)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Coburn Bach , as the father and
next friend of Jimmy Bach , an infant , to sue the Department of
Military Affairs and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or either .
Be it enacted by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Coburn Bach , as the father and next
friend of Jimmy Bach, an infant of Rossmoyne , Ohio, for and
on behalf of Jimmy Bach , is authorized to sue the Department
of Military affairs and the Commonwealth of Kentucky , or either ,
in the Gallatin Circuit Court, for damages resulting from per-
sonal injuries and medical expenses caused by alleged negligence
on the part of employes of the Department of Military Affairs
while acting in the line of duty.
Section 2. The suit shall be for a sum not in excess of ten
thousand dollars .
Section 3. The Attorney General may investigate the
claim herein and settle said claim before or after suit is filed , if
he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in said suit or
by settlement with the Attorney General shall be paid out of
the General Expenditure Fund in the State Treasury upon war-
rant drawn by the Commissioner of Finance upon the State
Treasurer .
Section 4. In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered
by the circuit court , either party may appeal to the Court of
Appeals of Kentucky .
Section 5. No statement in this resolution shall be con-
sidered by either the court or jury as an admission of negligence
upon the part of the Commonwealth , and this resolution shall not
be introduced as evidence , nor commented upon by counsel in
argument to the jury.

CHAPTER 241
(H. R. 22)
A JOINT RESOLUTION authorizing Mrs. Catherine Berghaus to sue
the Commonwealth of Kentucky for damages resulting from the
death of her son .
Whereas , in 1937 , Bernard J. Berghaus , a member of the Kentucky
National Guard , was ordered to active duty during the emergency
resulting from the flood of that year ; and
Whereas , while on such active duty , Bernard J. Berghaus contracted
pneumonia for which he died shortly thereafter ; and
Whereas , the Commonwealth of Kentucky is morally liable for the
damages arising from his death ,
Now, Therefore ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That Mrs. Catherine Berghaus of Louisville ,
Kentucky , is hereby authorized to sue the Commonwealth of
Kentucky in the Jefferson Circuit Court for damages resulting
from the death of her son, Bernard J. Berghaus , who died while
performing a public service in behalf of the Commonwealth .
Section 2. Such suit shall be for a sum not in excess of
ten thousand dollars .

authorized herein and settle same before or after suit is filed if
he deems it advisable . Any amount recovered in the suit or by

sioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .

by the circuit court either party may appeal to the Court of

without any negligence on his part , and ,
WHEREAS , as a result of such accident, he sustained severe injuries
which are alleged to have arisen out of and in the course of his
employment , and
WHEREAS , the circumstances surrounding the injury of the said Alvin
Adkins were such as to impose a moral obligation upon the Com-
monwealth :
NOW , THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Alvin Adkins , of Paintsville , Kentucky , is authorized
to sue the Commonwealth of Kentucky in the Johnson County
Circuit Court for damages for personal injuries arising out of and
in the course of his employment as an employe of the Common-
wealth , for which damages the Commonwealth is morally respon-
sible . Said suit shall be for a sum not in excess of five thousand
dollars .
The Attorney General may investigate the claim and may
settle said claim before or after suit is filed , if he deems it ad-
visable . Any amount recovered in said suit or by settlement
with the Attorney General shall be paid out of the General Ex-
penditure Fund in the State Treasury upon warrant drawn by
the Commissioner of Finance upon the State Treasurer .
In the event suit is filed and judgment rendered by the
circuit court , either party may appeal to the Court of Appeals .
CHAPTER 244
(H. R. 27)
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for an investigation , study , and sur-
vey by the Constitution Review Commission regarding prosecution
in criminal cases on information rather than indictment when the
grand jury is not in session .
WHEREAS , under Section 12 of the Constitution of Kentucky no person
may be proceeded against criminally for an indictable offense by
information , except in cases arising in the land or naval forces, or in

CHAPTER 245 681
the militia, when in actual service in time of war or public danger ,
or by leave of court for oppression or misdemeanor in office , and
WHEREAS , such restriction on the extent of use of an information
hinders and delays prosecution in many criminal cases when the
grand jury is not in session , and
WHEREAS , the Constitution Review Commission is eminently capable
to conduct an investigation , study , and survey of amendments to the
Constitution of Kentucky :
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The Constitution Review Commission is hereby requested
and authorized to conduct a thorough and complete investiga-
tion , study , and survey regarding the desirability of an amend-
ment to Section 12 of the Constitution of Kentucky which would
permit prosecution on information rather than indictment when
the grand jury is not in session . Said commission shall sub-
mit
itsreport
tothe regular 1954 session
ofthe General Assembly
.
Approved March 24
, 1952

CHAPTER 251 685
CHAPTER 251
(H. R. 46)
A JOINT RESOLUTION providing for an investigation , study and survey
by the Legislative Research Commission regarding the establishment
of a state -supported medical school at the University of Kentucky .
WHEREAS , the General Assembly finds that the existing shortage of
practicing physicians in many areas of the Commonwealth deprives
large numbers of citizens of proper medical attention on a reasonably
available basis , jeopardizes their health and welfare , and threatens
to become more serious ; and
WHEREAS , it appears that it is becoming more difficult for Kentucky
youth to obtain a medical education ; and
WHEREAS , the alleviation of such situations appears to depend on the
Commonwealth's promptly and actively assuming its responsibility
for providing facilities for the education and training of an adequate
number of Kentuckians for the profession of medicine ; and
WHEREAS , the University of Kentucky is the appropriate agency of the
Commonwealth to carry out such a program of professional educa-
tion and training in a state -supported medical school ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
The Legislative Research Commission , in cooperation
with the University of Kentucky and with the assistance of
such out-of-state and other consultants as it may select , is
hereby authorized and directed to make a careful and
impartial study of the desireability and steps necessary for
the establishment of a state -supported medical school at
the University of Kentucky. The Legislative Research
Commission shall report to the next regular session of the
General Assembly its findings and recommendations with
respect to such matters as it deems essential to its study ,
including particularly the following : (1) The needs for im-
proved medical services in Kentucky ; (2) the scope of the
medical education program necessary to meet these needs ;
(3 ) the detailed requirements for establishing and main-
taining a fully approved medical school at the University
of Kentucky in terms of staff, buildings , equipment , library
and hospital and clinical training facilities ; and (4) a plan
for financing such a program of medical education .

686 ACTS OF THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY
CHAPTER 252
(H. R. 47)
A JOINT RESOLUTION concerning the official name designation of the
State park located within the Pennyrile Forest area located in
northern Christian County near Dawson Springs .
WHEREAS , a recreation site was established by certain agencies of
the Federal Government in the forest area for the use and enjoy-
ment of the people of the Commonwealth ; and,
WHEREAS , it has been operated as a State park by the Division of
Parks , Department of Conservation since 1946 and approximately
10 years prior was under the direction of the U. S. Soil Conservation
Service and the 14,772 acre forest has been under the supervision of
the Division of Forestry , Department of Conservation since 1946 ;
and ,
WHEREAS , the recreation area of the Pennyrile Forest under the super-
vision of the U. S. Soil Conservation Service was referred to as the
Resettlement Project and later has been referred to as the Pennyrile
State Forest Park , the Pennyrile State Park and the Pennyrile Forest
State Park , under the operation of the Division of Parks , has there-
for caused confusion ; and ,
WHEREAS , this State park is one of the important parks in the State
park system ; although undeveloped as far as overnight , eating , picnic
and recreation facilities in comparison to many of the other state
parks in Kentucky's system which has made rapid strides since
1947 in the development of recreation areas for the convenience ,
enjoyment and health of the people of the Commonwealth of Ken-
tucky under the able direction and leadership of Commissioner of
Conservation Henry Ward and Mrs. Lucy Smith , Director of the
Division of Parks and through the interest of former Governor
Earle C. Clements and Governor Lawrence W. Wetherby ; and.
WHEREAS , Commissioner of Conservation Henry Ward has often re-
ferred to this scenic forest area as " the mountain park of western
Kentucky " and " one of Kentucky's most beautiful and scenic
recreation areas "
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
(1) That from the date of final passage and approval
of this resolution be this park officially named and hereafter
designated the "Pennyrile Forest State Park" ; and ,
(2 ) That those responsible for the development of
the State park system of the Commonwealth of Kentucky
give due priority in order that the Pennyrile Forest State
Park may receive the proper attention for its increasing ,
continued and complete development it so richly deserves ;
and ,

CHAPTER 253 687
(3) That the purpose of this resolution not only be
to call attention to Pennyrile Forest State Park but in
testimony and appreciation of the untiring efforts of Con-
servation Commissioner Henry Ward and his entire staff
who have worked so diligently to give the citizens of the
Commonwealth of Kentucky one of the best and most
progressive State park programs in the nation ; and
(4) That the Clerk of the House of Representatives
is directed to forward copies of this resolution to all
members of the Kentucky Congressional delegation, the
heads of all State government departments connected with
the development and operation of this park and to the
newspapers and radio stations in Kentucky and in the larger
cities in the states adjacent to Kentucky .
Approved March 24, 1952 .

gency .

wealth of Kentucky in favor of the Chief Clerk of the Senate .

ances on the basis of the number of days that such employes and

sembly in the year 1952 , the rate per day being computed by said

CHAPTER 260
(H. R. 62 )
A JOINT RESOLUTION recommending the procurement of an auto-
matic roll -call system for use in the House of Representatives of
the General Assembly .
WHEREAS , a joint committee composed of senators and representatives
of the General Assembly was created for the purpose of studying
the advisability of procuring an automatic roll -call system for use
in the House of Representatives of the General Assembly ; and
WHEREAS , the joint committee's study of automatic roll -call systems
now in use in the legislatures of other states reveals that the use
of such systems increases substantially the efficiency and accuracy
of voting in the legislatures where such systems are in use ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
Section 1. That an automatic roll-call system is found
to be efficient and satisfactory and the use of such a system
in the House of Representatives of the General Assembly
of the Commonwealth of Kentucky is deemed desirable ;
and
Section 2. That the State Property and Building
Commission , in conjunction with the Legislative Research
Commission , is urged to acquire , with funds for capital
outlay, an automatic roll-call system for installation in the
Chamber of the House of Representatives of the General
Assembly of the Commonwealth of Kentucky .

CHAPTER 262
(H. R. 65)
A RESOLUTION relating to the Murray State College Basketball Team.
WHEREAS , the basketball team of Murray State College advanced to
the finals of the N. I. A. B. Tournament at Kansas City , Missouri ,
and
WHEREAS , said Murray State College , although losing to Southwest
Missouri Teacher's College in the finals , brought distinction and
glory to the Commonwealth of Kentucky by their fine exhibition
of sportsmanship and skill , and

CHAPTER 262 695
WHEREAS , Garrett Beshear , a Murray Player , exhibited true courage
when he became the leading scorer in the tournament despite the
fact that there had been a recent death in his family , and
WHEREAS , Benny Purcell, another Murray Player, was voted the out-
standing player of the tournament ;
NOW, THEREFORE ,
Be it resolved by the General Assembly of the Common-
wealth of Kentucky :
That Coach Harlan Hodges and the Murray State
College basketball team are hereby congratulated for their
fine effort on behalf of the honor and glory of the Com-
monwealth of Kentucky and that a copy of this resolution
be telegraphed to Coach Hodges and the Murray State
College basketball team .
Approved March 21 , 1952
